body: Please login to the VPN to follow this link Please login to the VPN to follow this link Academic and Digital Innovation Academic Workload Planning Course and module catalogue Employee Self Service HR People Hub (Hornbill) Modern Work Programme MyPort student website Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Research and Innovation Report & Support Tool Staff Rewards and Benefits Staff Wellbeing and Support The pavement in front of Wiltshire Building will be closed from w/c 6 April for a minimum period of 15 weeks. This is due to the latest remedial works being carried out on the building. Ruby Stevenson, who works as a Disability Officer in Student Services, shares her story of being diagnosed with stage 3 cancer, how it has affected her and how she hopes her story will show what’s possible while she fundraises to help others who might one day face their own turning point. A mathematical model used to simulate sports match outcomes has identified England as a serious contender for the FIFA World Cup. We’re currently aware that University bus tickets are not listed on the First Bus app following an update. Tickets already activated should still appear within your 'tickets' on the app. Colleagues purchasing staff day tickets should purchase a ticket on board, or avoid updating their app. First Bus are investigating this issue, and updated instructions will be available shortly.

title: Staff Essentials - Staff Services
headings: Staff Essentials Staff Services General Services Staff health and wellbeing Staff networks, unions and support Travel Academic services Training and Development Marketing and communications
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. Staff health and wellbeing Staff networks, unions and support Training and development Marketing and communications People Hub - One-stop-shop access to the People Operations Team (formally called HR Services). Staff Covid Guidance webpages Staff Portal - access timetables and other services My Benefits - information about the range of benefits and entitlements available to current members of University staff. Employee Benefits Platform (Vivup) - the discount and benefits scheme for employees Staff Directory - find contact details for members of staff at the University Service Desk - raise requests for IT, DSAA, Marketing and more IS Service Status - check if any of the University's IT services are having issues A-Z Staff Essentials - find a link to a specific section of the staff intranet Document Warehouse - a list of University-wide documents eRecords - our online records storage system Employee Self Service (ESS) - book annual leave, record absences, update your personal details, download your payslips and P60 form, and access your learning record Manager Self Service (MSS) - for managers to approve requests and information for their teams Printing Services - order printed materials from the in-house printing service Finance Systems - including the University's financial management system e5, collaborative planning, and finance reports Library - the University Library website SITS Student System - the system for applicants, current students and staff to manage and access student information Customer Relationship Management - how we use Salesforce to manage and analyse our interactions with customers Occupational Health Portal - provides staff and managers access to the Occupational Health Portal for pre-employment questionnaires, manager referrals and health surveillance Staff Noticeboard - notices and ads posted internally by staff We encourage you to explore our dedicated Staff Wellness Hub, which stands to provide support for your health and wellbeing. Inside the Hub, you'll discover a wealth of invaluable links that can connect you with a range of services and resources to uplift your mind and body, at work and beyond. Association of University Administrators (AUA) Staff Networks - support networks for staff, including LBGTQ+, multicultural, parents and carers, disability, women’s, and neurodiverse staff networks Staff Associations - two associations represent the views and champion the wellbeing of all staff, and facilitate two-way communication between staff and the University's management structure Support during conflict, humanitarian, and natural disasters - help and advice for students and colleagues affected by home and world events University & College Union (UCU) - professional association for academics, lecturers, trainers, researchers and academic-related staff UNISON - represent non-academic staff who provide professional services Equality and diversity team - advice, support, training and policies relating to equality and diversity for students and staff The Report and Support online tool can be used to report bullying or harassment at the University The Harassment Adviser Network is available to help all staff and students who feel they may have been bullied, or have themselves been accused of bullying. While the advisers can't directly advise you what to do, they can inform you of your options and offer support. Travel to Work - discounts and schemes to help you travel sustainably Travel and Expenses System - how to book travel and make an expense claim Moodle - our Digital Learning Environment (DLE), hosting course learning materials and activities Course and module catalogue - retrieve course and module information Portsmouth Research Portal (Pure) - our publicly accessible research database showcasing our research community, research projects, outputs and outcomes. All active research staff should have a profile on Pure. eAssessment - guides and resources to help you set up and mark electronic assessments Year dates - term dates for the academic year Academic Workload Planning Academic policies and guidance - regulations, policies and guidance covering exams, assessments, course approvals, annual course monitoring, collaborative partnerships and more. Timetabling - hw to request a timetable, amend timetables, view the academic timetable and book rooms for teaching activities. Teaching rooms - see what each teaching room looks like and what equipment and resources are available so you can decide which room to book for a specific teaching activity. Skills Forge - an an interactive Learning and Development platform for Postgraduate Research Students and Supervisors Staff Portal - access timetables and other services Personal tutoring - information and resources for personal tutors TEL Tales Blog - updates from the Technology Enhanced Learning team Learning and Teaching Innovation – key resources and guidance from the Academic Development and Technology Enhanced Learning team to support academics in delivering their teaching in a blended learning context Enterprise Educators Toolkit - resources to help embed enterprise into the curriculum Learning and development - new staff induction, core training, learning programmes and learning resource centre Performance and Development Review (PDR) - the tool for recording your Performance and Development PDR Webpages - what you need to know about your performance and development review LinkedIn Learning - free online courses Equality and Diversity training - wide range of training and briefings, including staff induction, e-learning, management briefings and bespoke sessions Digital Skills - Training and mentoring and support for Digital Skills. Project documentation and videos on request – email us: digitalskills@port.ac.uk IT support - request help from IS with technology or software issues Research Supervision Events - development events for supervisors of postgraduate research students Learning and Innovation Training calendar - Events calendar for Learning and Teaching and Technology Enhanced Learning training, and resources for colleagues working towards HEA Fellowship via the APEX programme. Service Desk - Request a marketing service such as making a change to a webpage, creating a staff profile on the main website, making a change to the MyPort website, adding a news article, blog post, research participant ad, event, webinar, podcast or short course to the website, creating a microsite, and reporting a website fault Marketing Portal - Request branded collateral such as posters, flyers and leaflets; download images; and access brand guidelines and advice about effective marketing materials Business Intelligence Dashboards - data from different areas of professional services

title: Staff Essentials - Resources and Information
headings: Staff Essentials Resources and Information Buildings and facilities News Strategy
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. Links to useful resources for University of Portsmouth staff: Printing services - order printed materials from our in-house print service Catering services - find a cafe on campus University buildings and campus - get addresses and directions for our buildings University Bus - find out about our bus service for students Visitor parking - arrange a parking permit for visitors Library - the University Library website Teaching rooms - see what each teaching room looks like and what equipment and resources are available so you can decide which room to book for a specific teaching activity. Book a room - Room Check has been decommissioned. Please use CMIS Go to view room availability Breastfeeding and Expressing facilities - designed to provide a comfortable and private space for breastfeeding and expressing milk Staff News - internal staff news stories All-Staff Emails - copies of recent emails sent to all staff Vice-Chancellor’s Bulletin - read the latest bulletin from the Vice-Chancellor MyPort news - the most recent news stories for students Emails to students - copies of recent emails sent to all or a large proportion of students from the central Internal Communications Team University News - external news stories for the media and public University Strategy - our plans to become the top modern university in the UK and one of the top 100 young universities in the world Vice Chancellor’s awards - annual awards to celebrate contributions by colleagues, students and the community. Structure and Governance - our academic structure and professional service departments, our management structure, governance, and more

title: My Benefits
headings: Human Resources My Benefits Health and Wellbeing Staff Discounts Welcome to our New Employee Benefits Platform Your Care - our brand new Wellbeing Platform Car Benefit Scheme Pensions Life Insurance Leave Travel Family Friendly Explore
body: Information about the range of benefits and entitlements available to current members of University staff. Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) Speak in confidence to an independent and fully qualified counsellor 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year to discuss any emotional, personal or work-related problems. Talking to one of Vivup’s experienced counsellors or psychotherapists can help you understand a problem, make a tough decision, try a different approach or just learn how to cope with a difficult situation. The aim is to support you and find a solution to whatever is worrying you. Ravelin Sports Centre The University has an outstanding new sports centre. Students, staff and local residents are all able to enjoy the facilities, with a range of classes and courses, personal training and very affordable membership with great benefits available. Staff members can access discounted membership. Dentistry The Dental Academy provides dental care by students to patients who do not currently have a dentist and who are available for multiple, potentially long appointments during the day. You can check on the NHS Dental Helpline website to find out if the Academy is currently taking on new patients. Bupa University Scheme New Bupa University Scheme, offered by Uni-Health and available to all University staff and their families. Some of the benefits include: Comprehensive Cover including Full In-patient & Out-patient Cover / Full Cancer Cover / Full UK Hospital Cover / University discount. Visit www.uni-health.co.uk or call 01929 477 318 for further information. Chaplaincy The Chaplaincy is available to staff and students, offering a sanctuary room as a quiet place for reflection and prayer, as well as Islamic prayer rooms, or just providing a quiet space to have a tea or coffee. The Chaplaincy also run regular events and provide information and support for all. DSE Sight Test If you are a regular user of display screen equipment, then you are entitled to a free eye test at Specsavers and a voucher worth £45 towards the cost of spectacles if they are required. We are excited to announce the launch of our brand new employee benefits platform in partnership with Vivup. This new benefits platform will over time, will bring together all of our employee benefits in to one easy to access place, online and via a mobile phone App. As part of this launch, we're also partnering with Vivup to introduce some new benefits which are designed to support your physical, financial and mental wellbeing, including a great new Employee Assistance Program. You now have access to a huge range of discounts across the UK’s major retailers including supermarkets, technology, fashion, travel and much more! To access these savings, please register at http://www.port.ac.uk/mybenefits The platform has 100’s of fantastic discounts and offers, you’re sure to find many that are relevant to you. Whether you’d like to save money on your grocery shopping, days out and entertainment or household purchases, there are plenty of offers for you. Here are just a few examples: • Save on groceries with 5% off at Morrisons and Sainsbury’s and 4% at Tesco, Asda and Waitrose, plus 6.5% at Marks and Spencer stores • Save on household appliances and technology (with 7% off at Currys, 20% at AEG, and 5% off at Beko), • Save on DIY and items for your home with 5% off at B&Q, 6% off at Wickes, 6% off at Argos, and 7% off at IKEA), • Save on fashion and sportswear with 5% off at Boohoo, 7.5% off at ASOS, 7% off at River Island and 8% off New Look, 7% off at Nike, or 10% off at Footlocker and Footasylum) • Save on your morning coffee with 10% off at Costa and 8% at Caffè Nero • Save on entertainment and days out with up to 30% off cinema tickets, around 8% off at attractions such as Chessington World of Adventures, Thorpe Parke, Alton Towers and many more, including an 11% saving (and free £5 voucher) for the Spinnaker Tower. (All offers correct at the time of writing) Any issues, please contact reward@port.ac.uk or the Vivup helpline on 01252 784540 Click here for important information about use of the previous Sodexo platform. We believe we are all happier and healthier when able to take control of our own wellbeing, so in addition to our Employee Assistance Program we are now launching ‘Your Care’, a dynamic wellbeing platform offering a range of personal, emotional and financial wellbeing tools to help you be a happier, healthier you! • Assessments - Identify the risk of developing leading lifestyle-related diseases, along with mental health and financial scores • Goals - The option to set health-boosting goals with small, sustainable lifestyle changes • Activity Data - Connectivity with your health apps to automatically track goal progress • Resources - Blogs, videos, recipes and more resources providing inspiration and motivation • Support - Access to 24/7 support and counselling (via our EAP), 365 days a year Visit Health & Wellbeing at http://www.port.ac.uk/mybenefits to access the platform Are you looking for a brand new electric or plug-in hybrid car with an inclusive motoring package and one convenient monthly amount taken from your gross salary? Our car benefit scheme can do all that and more! Holiday Purchase Scheme - The Holiday Purchase Scheme allows eligible employees to apply for up to 5 days (37 hours) additional time off (pro-rata for part-time employees) in a leave year, over and above existing entitlements to holidays, public holidays and University closure days. For more information please follow this link. FREE Will Writing Service - We have partnered with the Taylor Lucas Partnership to make available their will writing services at no cost to all University of Portsmouth colleagues (and also partners and spouses). This is a quick, easy and free way to achieve certainty and peace of mind for you and your loved ones. This brochure explains the scheme. FREE Mortgage Education, Advice and Brokerage Services - We have partnered with Charles Cameron & Associates (CC&A) to provide colleagues with mortgage education, advice and brokerage services. Whether you are a first-time buyer looking to re-mortgage or finance an investment property, they can help you access the best possible financial solutions for your needs. (Please note - any advice provided to you by CC&A is a strictly individual arrangement between yourself and CC&A. The University of Portsmouth will not be liable for that advice or any actions you take as a result of it under any circumstances.) Tuition Fees Discount - Staff discount on tuition fees of 30 per cent is also available on tuition fees for children of staff. This applies to undergraduate and postgraduate taught, full-time and part-time study. The detailed conditions and how to apply are set out in the Tuition Fee Discounts Policy (pdf). TOTUM discount cards - including 10% off at the Co-op – staff are eligible to purchase cards, allowing access to many retailers' student discounts. Staff are also eligible to sign up to UNiDAYS which offers discounts similar to that of Totum. Portsmouth Football Club - free tickets and discounts to both the men's and women's matches. Learn a Language - The School of Languages and Applied Linguistics is responsible for the delivery of the Institution Wide Language Programme (IWLP). IWLP provides opportunities for all campus-based taught students from across the University to learn a foreign language alongside their studies. Furthermore, each year there are a limited number of places reserved for staff to also learn a language. At present, there are eight languages that can be studied, Arabic, British Sign Language, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Mandarin and Spanish. IWLP is an important part of the University’s commitment to global engagement. Furthermore, it can encourage the development of intercultural awareness in a mobile working environment and is recognised as a ‘career-enhancing activity’. Each year there is a number of course places available to University staff where a module has already recruited to the minimum class size requirement. Staff places will be allocated on a first come, first served basis. However, those who have not previously undertaken an IWLP module will be prioritised over those who have. There will be no charge to staff, nor their department. However, their participation in an IWLP class will need to be supported by their line manager and would be expected to feature in their PDR. Staff can submit an application request via email to iwlp@port.ac.uk. Staff are reminded that places are subject to availability. For further information on the how the programme works; the routes available and application dates please see the Student Learn a Foreign Language page. Entrepreneurial staff can get a 40% discount on work space at Innovation Connect for their first year to work on their business. Joining Innovation Connect, which is based at the University in 3 different centres, means staff will be part of an engaged business community, get access to networking events with experts as well as free business support from Barclays Eagle Labs to help them grow their business. Entrepreneurial staff can get a 40% discount on work space at Innovation Connect for their first year to work on their business. Joining Innovation Connect, which is based at the University in 3 different centres, means staff will be part of an engaged business community, get access to networking events with experts as well as free business support from Barclays Eagle Labs to help them grow their business. Entrepreneurial staff can get a 40% discount on work space at Innovation Connect for their first year to work on their business. Joining Innovation Connect, which is based at the University in 3 different centres, means staff will be part of an engaged business community, get access to networking events with experts as well as free business support from Barclays Eagle Labs to help them grow their business. We are proud to offer competitive and leading pension schemes to all our employees, and these form one of the biggest benefits available to you as an employee. Please go to our Pensions page to access the information you need to know. Members of Teachers' Pension, Local Government Pension Scheme and NHS pensions are provided Death in Service cover of three times their salary. To maintain this benefit even where employees have decided to opt out, and for eligible employees in other occupational pension schemes, the University has put in place an equivalent Life Insurance policy to provide this same cover. Employees who are added to this scheme will be informed in writing by HR. Further details may be found here. The University provides a generous leave entitlement of 32-35 days per annum and additional Christmas closure days*. Family-friendly Entitlements* are available to employees include maternity, paternity, adoption and shared parental leave, flexible working and time off for dependants. As a University, we aim to reduce congestion in Portsmouth and improve the air quality for everyone who studies, lives and works in our city. A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. Travel to work with discounts on the bus, ferries and cycling. Family-friendly Entitlements* available to employees include maternity, paternity, adoption and shared parental leave, flexible working and time off for dependants. title: Policies
headings: Policies The Document Warehouse What's in the Document Warehouse? What's not in the Document Warehouse? What if I'm not sure what I'm looking for or where to find it? A B C D E F G H I J K L M O P Q R S T U V W Explore
body: The Document Warehouse is intended to house the University's university-wide documents: strategies, policies, procedures and codes which affect all staff in their work. See the A-Z list. Some items only appear on the external facing policy page found here; https://www.port.ac.uk/about-us/structure-and-governance/policies-and-standards The Document Warehouse does not include operational documentation for every single process or department. More information can be found on the relevant University academic, support or service department web page:https://staff.port.ac.uk/staffessentials/departments/ If you can't find what you are looking for, or if you are not sure that you have the latest version then please contact Jacqui Bryden at jacqui.bryden@port.ac.uk. title: Printing Services
headings: Printing Services Staff Office printing Academic printing Printing for Students Contact us: Explore
body: The Design, Print and Publications Team provides the complete printing services for staff and students. Printing covers anything we require created or branded, from all paper based products (business cards, leaflets, posters, prospectuses) through to merchandise, exhibition materials, window graphics and building signage. We no longer print on campus but have a range of external suppliers to ensure that work is priced competitively and efficiently managed across all our printing needs. To get started, please fill in a Hornbill request. For printing that you would have undertaken on your office printer (e.g. over 50 pages) simply attach a PDF of your work to the Hornbill form, with your cost centre, and delivery address and we will deliver your print requirements to campus (or home). We support academic colleagues with production of teaching and learning materials as well as submitted coursework printing. Please raise a Hornbill request. We provide a cost-effective and quality printing service for our students. You can also print your posters, and bind your theses (or dissertations) to University specifications through our University Online Store. Email: designandprint@port.ac.uk Contact and departments

title: Departments
headings: Customer Relationship Management Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Explore
body: One view of our University. One view of our customers. Better outcomes for everyone. CRM is an approach that allows us to manage and analyse our interactions with past, current, and potential customers (including students, alumni, businesses and any other individuals or organisations the University comes into contact with). There is 1 CRM software solution we use to do this, called Salesforce. There are also 5 other software solutions that support customer relationship management and work alongside Salesforce. These are: SITS – Current applicants and students (data and communications) Pure – Research and Innovation (data and engagement) Target Connect – Careers and Employability platform for jobs, placements, volunteering, events and appointments Eventbrite – Events (excluding Student Recruitment events) Campaign Monitor – Income generating R&I bulk communications, and other communications to mailing lists of over 500 where there's a really good reason that the other systems listed above can't be used! All six software solutions will help us maximise and streamline our activities to support our relationships with all our customers, achieve our data compliance targets, reduce data entry, and develop strategic partnerships and collaborate more closely with organisations. This will support us in implementing the University Strategy. For more information and guidance on Student communications for colleagues, including the channel options and responsible teams/departments for student communications, please visit the Student Communications Guidance webpage. If you need access to one or more of these systems, wish to report an issue, suggest an improvement, or simply contact the team to let them know what a great job they’re doing then please choose the appropriate route from the list below: Request a CRM account access or amend your account details Request a modification to CRM (e.g. change of form field label, new field type, new form) Report an issue with CRM For general information and support, please contact pure@port.ac.uk or 02392 846 191 Target Connect, Eventbrite, and Campaign Monitor Email Becky Miles, Interim System Administrator (becky.miles@port.ac.uk). Contact and departments

title: Occupational Health Service
headings: Human Resources Occupational Health Service What is Occupational Health? Explore
body: 'The promotion and maintenance of the highest degree of physical, mental and social wellbeing of workers in all occupations by preventing departures from health, controlling risks and the adaptation of work to people and people to their jobs.' Professor Dame Carol Black, FRCP - Adviser to the British Government on Work and Health (2006-2016) The University’s Occupational Health Service provides advice to managers and individuals on work related health problems and health problems that can affect work further to referral from a Manager. Occupational Health is a distinct branch of health care which specialises in the relationship between work and health. Advice to management will only include medical information with the consent of the employee but will mainly focus on advice regarding work fitness and any adjustments that might support the employee. All information kept by the University's Occupational Health Service is held in line with the University's Data Retention Policy. For further information, please read the Occupational Health Privacy Statement (pdf). To contact the Occupational Health Service, please call on 011 8207 6190 or email: admin@cordellhealth.co.uk HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Occupational Health Service Role of Occupational Health Referral Sickness absence Risk Assessment for New and Expectant Mothers Mental Health Bullying & Harassment Information Job Hazard Information Protocols and Guidance notes Stress information Job Functional Assessment - Information for Managers Research Passports Disability Guidance Access to Work PGCE applicants Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement Role of Occupational Health Risk Assessment for New and Expectant Mothers Bullying & Harassment Information Job Hazard Information Protocols and Guidance notes Job Functional Assessment - Information for Managers Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

title: Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP)
headings: Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP) AHEP at the University of Portsmouth
body: The Association of Higher Education Professionals (AHEP) aims to promote excellence in HE management and administration and to raise the profile of those roles in HE as a profession through the development of best practice and high standards in the area. Not only are they a representative voice for HE Professionals in the sector, they also provide support and guidance for members in developing your their careers. Find out more here. The AHEP core commitment for members are to: Empower your career journey Improving your professional practice Supporting your professional development Championing the vaules of: Credible, Collaborative, Inclusive and Personable They provide a Professional Framework linked to 8 key professional behaviours, which can be used whatever your role or level. Membership is open to everyone, whether you are and existing or aspiring HE Professional. Click here to find out more about why you should join, or here to explore the membership levels available. To find out more check out the National Events and local activities via our Padlet AUA@UoP Community. If you have any questions or would like to find out more, please contact the UoP Branch Advocate: Nikki Sowerby. Nikki is also the South Region Network Deputy Coordinator. Contact and departments

title: Staff Essentials - Support networks
headings: Staff Essentials
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. The University provides a number of staff networks intended to support issues relating to harassment, bullying and intimidation in the workplace, or more general counselling and guidance, available at any time of the day or night. A 24- hour confidential telephone helpline is available to all University staff through the University Employee Assistance Programme. You can access information on health, wellbeing and support from MyPort The University Chaplains are trained to listen and be alongside people at the high and low points of life in a non-judgemental way. They work confidentially across the University with staff and students. As part of the University's commitment to equal opportunities, the Equality and Diversity Unit has supported the development of a number of staff networks. These exist to allow staff from similar backgrounds to meet, network and discuss the issues that matter to them.

title: Staff Networks
headings: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Staff Networks Disability Staff Network LGBTQ+ Staff Network Multicultural Staff Network Neurodiverse Staff Network Parent and Carers Staff Network Women's Staff Network Explore
body: Staff networks are pivotal in building an inclusive culture at the University, where everyone is able to thrive. The networks are supported by the Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion team. Staff networks allow colleagues space to come together to exchange views, ideas, resources, support and advice, as well as providing a platform to raise awareness and instigate positive change within the University. Networks are run for staff by staff and are inclusive spaces open to all colleagues, no assumptions are made about individuals because they join a community. Representatives of the staff networks sit on the University's EDI consultative group, EDI steering committee, University Athena SWAN and REC SAT groups, where the networks are invited to present information on their work and views to these committees. To join a network click the relevant Join Network button and complete the membership form, this goes straight to the Co-Chairs who can provide you access to the network Spaces. title: Staff Essentials - Staff Associations
headings: Staff Essentials
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. ‌‌As a University employee you are automatically a member of either the Academic Staff Association (ASA) or the Professional Support Staff Association (PSSA). Both associations promote a spirit of unity and communication, represent the views and champion the wellbeing of all staff, and facilitate two-way communication between staff and the University's management structure, including the Board of Governors and Directorate. What can we assist you with? Advising you on the best person/department to speak in regards to any issues and concerns you may have. Highlighting issues that are of general concern to senior management. Finding out more about the departments and services provided across the university. For more information, please visit the ASA or PSSA websites.
links: University of Portsmouth Home Staff Services Departments Resources Academic Staff Association (ASA) Professional Support Staff Association (PSSA) PSSA © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: Staff Essentials - Support during conflict, humanitarian and natural disasters
headings: Staff Essentials Support for colleagues and students during conflict, humanitarian, and natural disasters Help and advice for colleagues affected by home and world events Supporting students
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. We are proud to be a university with a strong global outlook and deep local roots. From our earliest days, our community has included people from every corner of the globe. This global connection means when conflicts, humanitarian crises, or natural disasters occur anywhere in the world the impact is felt by our students, staff and alumni here in Portsmouth and beyond. World events can have a profound impact on our wellbeing, peace of mind and our sense of connection to others. We understand that everyone’s experience is unique, and that responses to such events may change over time. During these times of uncertainty, it's important to remember that seeking support is a positive step and a sign of strength. We encourage you to stay connected with friends, family, and colleagues, and please ask for help if you need it. Whether you are concerned about family and friends in affected regions, processing difficult news and emotions or finding it challenging to concentrate on your studies or work we encourage you to reach out. This guide is designed to help those affected by world events, whether seeking support for themselves or looking for ways to support others. Our university stands united in supporting one another through these difficult times. As a community, we draw strength from our diversity and shared commitment to understanding and supporting one another. We understand that each person's experience is unique, and we are committed to providing a safe, inclusive, and supportive environment for all. If you are a member of staff you can: Speak to your line manager, who can be a supportive first point of contact and help you in accessing any further support you may benefit from. You can also contact staffwellbeing@port.ac.uk, where you can find out about the support on offer. Reach out to the University Chaplaincy service for confidential, non-judgemental support. This service welcomes people of all beliefs, including those with no religious affiliation. Contact the Employee Assistance Programme (EAP), which provides confidential support from an external service, available 24 hours a day. If you support students, you can signpost them to a range of services available to support their wellbeing: University Chaplaincy: Offering a safe space to be heard and supported to all students and staff, regardless of faith or belief. Student Wellbeing: Providing counselling, mental health support, and practical advice. Our other Student Services: providing support from budgeting, student route visa advice, general support and life at university Students' Union: Offering peer support, social connections, and a voice for student concerns.

title: Staff Essentials - UNISON
headings: Staff Essentials Steve Oliver Jill Rice Vicki Parker Colin Fudger Gareth Jones Tyson Marsh Annie Dorrington Contact details
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. title: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion
headings: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Explore
body: With students arriving at the University of Portsmouth from over 120 countries each year and the University's commitment to widening participation in higher education attracting an ever-increasing variety of students - and indeed colleagues - understanding and accommodating their needs is essential to its continued success. The Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion unit undertakes a wide variety of work to achieve this. We offer: Advice, support and training for colleagues, to ensure the University meets its legislative requirements and its commitment to create conditions where all colleagues and students are treated only based on their merits, abilities and potential. Access to policies, guidelines and the University Equality Objectives, which outline how we work to meet our equity, diversity, and inclusion requirements. Guidance during the development and review of University policies, procedures and functions to ensure they don't disadvantage any group of colleagues, students, prospective colleagues, prospective students or graduates, and where possible, improve equality of opportunity. This review is titled Equality Impact Assessment (EIA). Staff Networks which enable staff from similar backgrounds to meet and discuss the issues affecting them, as well as offering a platform to initiate positive change in the University. Coordination and training of a Harassment Adviser Network to offer help and advice to students and staff who believe they may be a victim of bullying or harassment. Liaison with students, staff, other HE institutions and the wider city to help organise events and activities which celebrate diversity, inclusion and dignity & respect. An Equality and Diversity Moodle site for students We also produce a comprehensive range of reports detailing the University's performance on - and priorities regarding - equality and diversity within the institution covering both staff and students The Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion Team is part of Human Resources. Should you require advice or further information on any area of equity, wellbeing & inclusion don't hesitate to get in touch with the team via equality@port.ac.uk Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion,Human Resources,Floor 6,Mercantile House,Portsmouth, PO1 2EG Inclusion Calendar 2023/24 Equality Impact Assessment (EIA) Equality and Diversity training The Report and Support Tool Contact and departments

title: Travel to work
headings: Human Resources Travel to work Sustainable travel Explore
body: As a University, we aim to reduce congestion in Portsmouth and improve the air quality for everyone who studies, lives and works in our city. A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. Leaving the car at home and making sustainable travel choices can save you time and money. You may also benefit from improved wellbeing and reduce your environmental impact at the same time. Whether you’re looking for information about local cycle routes to work, walking maps or links to public transport you can access it and plan your journey on the My Journey Hampshire website. You can also commute together with others by using easitShare. This scheme covers journeys made by car, bicycle, taxi, motorbikes, train, buses and on foot. title: Staff Essentials - Workload Planning
headings: Staff Essentials Workload Planning
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. A new Workload Allocation Model (WAM) framework is being developed to replace Simitive to record academic workloads. The function of the new framework will balance workloads in an equitable, fair and transparent manner, and enable effective planning of our academic staff time to support the achievement of the University's strategies for education, research, impact, and innovation. All relevant documents will be held in this section. Guidance for Research Workload Allocations – Supporting document Nov 2025: Framework for Engagement with, and use of, Academic Workload Planning Summary of Workload Allocation Model edits - November 2025

title: Equality and Diversity training
headings: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Equality and Diversity training Explore
body: The Equality and Diversity Unit provide a wide range of training/briefings, including staff induction, e-learning, management briefings and bespoke sessions as requested by business areas. All Staff Core Training 1. All staff, as part of their core training requirements, should undertake the following equality e-learning within their probationary period: Bullying & Harassment (and an additional session for managers) Diversity & Inclusion in the Workplace Equality, Diversity & Inclusion in the Workplace (for managers) Race in the Workplace Safeguarding in Higher Education Sexual Harassment Awareness Access Core and Role-specific training from Docebo. Additional training, including Equality Impact Assessment (EIA), Menopause, Disability, Dementia, and Autism Awareness, can be accessed via Docebo. 2. Staff may also consider completing the Harvard Bias Implicit Analysis Test. This anonymous test is for your information only and is not a requirement of University training. 3. The Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion Team will introduce all staff to the support offered during the staff induction event that all new staff attend. The Equity, Wellbeing & Inclusion team can offer bespoke tailored sessions for team meeting updates or specific sessions. To discuss this further, please email the Equality Manager at equality@port.ac.uk If you have any problems with the e-learning procedure, please contact equality@port.ac.uk. title: Academic Registry
headings: Academic Registry Explore
body: About Us and Who to Contact Welcome to the Academic Registry! The Academic Registry plays a central role in our institution’s commitment to maintaining academic excellence. Our role is to develop and support an academic quality regulatory landscape which supports the highest standards of academic quality, through academic regulations and policies that support a fair, transparent, and effective learning environment. Our Mission is to ensure the University’s academic quality environment complies with mandatory regulatory requirements, upholds academic standards, enhances the student experience, and supports all students, regardless of their background, to succeed. Academic Excellence: Promoting the highest standards of education and quality of student experience Integrity: Ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability in all our actions. Collaboration: Partnering with students, staff and external stakeholders to uphold and improve academic quality. Responsiveness: Adapting to the changing landscape of higher education through innovation and foresight. Working collaboratively across the University, and informed by sector leading-practice we will: Ensure all academic policies, regulations and procedures meet mandatory regulatory requirements whilst being efficient and maintaining a forward-thinking approach. Provide an assessment regulatory framework which supports student achievement, and eliminates unwarranted barriers to learning and success; Create a student and staff-centric academic quality environment that promotes high standards and continuous improvement; Generate and analyse reports on academic performance, student feedback and outcomes to drive informed decision-making for shaping the education portfolio and enhancing quality; Efficiently manage and compassionately resolve student casework relating to academic quality and regulations, providing support and solutions to minimise the impact on all students involved. Offer expert leadership and advice on academic quality and regulations to support the academic and professional services community. title: Estates and Campus Services
headings: Estates and Campus Services Explore
body: We are responsible for the construction and maintenance of the University's estate. The estate is, broadly speaking, based on two main campuses, these being Guildhall and Langstone Student Village and extends to approximately 1.5 million sq ft of buildings together with associated playing fields and open parkland. The building stock extends to some 70 main buildings together with sundry additional minor structures. These range from Victorian buildings, either purpose built for education use or converted from original uses, to modern purpose built academic and residential buildings. The department recently completed a large development programme together with the resultant major refurbishment of existing building stock and subsequent departmental moves. It is also currently responsible for the implementation of the University's Mobility Policy. As a department providing a service to the University we recognise the importance of making you aware of the following: What services we provide What to do if you have any problems To help achieve this we have put together this web site that we hope will be of use to you. Please use the links to find the information you are looking for. The site will be updated as appropriate. Please let your colleagues know that this facility is available to them. We hope that you find these pages of use and would appreciate any feedback that you may wish to give. Please send any comments to Chris Burke-Hynes. title: Global
headings: Global Admin Team Exchanges and Study Abroad International Student Advisers Recruitment Team Marketing and Conversion Global Engagement Team Admissions Team Overseas Offices What do we NOT do in UoP Global? Brexit Explore
body: UoP Global is responsible for the University's support of international students, overseas partnerships and recruitment of new students from outside of the UK. It consists of several teams, dealing with wide-ranging international aspects from admissions and recruitment to visa support, student engagement, marketing and international exchange and study abroad partnerships. With a recently revamped structure, we work to deliver the aims of the Global Engagement Strategy through our activities here in the UK, as well as at our international offices across the globe. If you would like to discuss matters relating to Global activity you can contact Bobby Mehta, Director of Uop Global (bobby.mehta@port.ac.uk) or Mark Ovens, Deputy Director of UoP Global (mark.ovens@port.ac.uk). To speak with a specific team please see the information below. The Admin Team deliver outstanding customer service to enquiring students and colleagues around the University (as well as external agents and partner organisations) and providing comprehensive administrative support to the wider department, ensuring all policies and procedures are carried out in accordance with University rules and regulations and aligning with best practice. The Exchanges and Study abroad team provides advice and support to all students interested in studying or working abroad as part of their degree, as well as providing opportunities for Academic and Professional staff to undertake teaching or training activities with the Erasmus+ programme. The team is also responsible and the first point of contact for all incoming exchange and study abroad students to the University. The International Student Advice team provide immigration advice and support to international students studying at the university. They are are the only team in the university who are qualified to give visa advice to students. They also manage the International Orientation Programme and social and cultural events. The team of Global Recruitment Officers attend recruitment fairs, raise awareness of the University and promote us around the world. They each have regions that they look after and build and maintain relationships with agents, schools, partners and colleges in those regions. The Global Marketing Team supports all marketing and communications requirements for the UoP Global including digital and social media to printed publications. They are involved with raising brand awareness and the promotion of the University and its courses around the world. The Conversion Team assist with converting offer holders into students by providing details of outstanding conditions and answering applicant questions. The Global Engagement Team manage partnership agreements, visiting delegations, international student data analysis, the International Student Ambassador Scheme and outgoing International Summer Schools. The Admissions Team process Undergraduate and Postgraduate applications who have applied via an official Agent or Partner of the University. This involves assessing the applicant meets the University entry requirements for each course. The Team also produces offer letters, liaising with Agents and Partners directly over the admissions cycle. The overseas offices work closely with the Portsmouth based Global teams to market and recruit in their respective regions. issue CAS statements - DSAA: contact ukvi-student-compliance@port.ac.uk monitor student attendance - faculty contacts visa advice for staff provide advice about complex immigration advice including settlement and British citizenship book travel for staff or students provide financial support to students - Student Finance Centre process direct applications - DSAA: international.admissions@port.ac.uk advise on academic issues such as Extenuating Circumstances etc - Students’ Union Advice team: advice@upsu.net give advice on housing, money or wellbeing - we can signpost to the relevant information or services provide student status letters, bank letters or Council Tax letters - MyPort Hub: myport@port.ac.uk provide confirmation of working hours letters - BRP card and term dates on University website/MyPort - https://www.port.ac.uk/about-us/key-dates fee assessments for international students - contact feeassessment@port.ac.uk For information relating to Brexit, please visit our webpages. title: Human Resources
headings: Human Resources HR Services Quick Links: The People Hub CLICK HERE TO ACCESS THE PEOPLE HUB University People Strategy 2030 Explore
body: The Human Resources department provides advice, guidance and support for University staff and managers across all University HR policies and procedures. Our services include: advising on employment solutions, best practice, and legislative advice; providing information about pay, reward and benefits, including current salary scales, cost of employment figures, employee benefits, pensions, recognition awards and long service awards; creating, organising and facilitating development events, and providing advice and guidance relating to leadership, management and staff development; providing confidential Occupational Health advice and support on any health issues; widening participation in higher education by attracting a broader variety of students and staff; maintaining HR information systems and data, providing management information to enable analysis and insight to support decision-making, and completing relevant surveys and statutory returns. University People Strategy 2030 Employee self service People Operations team Checking an individual's right to work Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) HR/Payroll deadline dates* Employee Benefits Platform (Vivup) The People Hub provides you with a one-stop-shop to access the People Operations team (formally called HR Services) and find out everything you need to know about HR. Whether you have questions about benefits, policies, or you have a general enquiry, the People Hub is there to assist you. Access to a new ticketing system. The functionality works the same way as the current IS, H&S and Marketing self-service pages If you have an HR or Recruitment enquiry please raise a ticket on the People Hub and a member of the team will respond directly from within the People Hub Easy to access HR web pages and system links Key dates and calendars The University People Strategy 2030 will be achieved by a collaborative University-wide commitment, which is underpinned by an innovative and future-focussed high-performing Human Resources team. The Strategy will be a key contributor supporting our underlying principal of educating and transforming lives and will drive a culture of excellence where all our all people can achieve great things. title: Planning Department
headings: Planning Department Explore
body: The Planning Team report to the Vice-Chancellor. We focus on: Supporting the University's new strategic and financial planning processes. Introducing enhanced approaches to setting KPIs and monitoring the strategic plan. Management Information Providing strategic management information and analysis. Sharing management information through our 'Planning dashboards' using business intelligence software. Liaison across the University's HESA returns to ensure consistency of data. Annual Planning Processes Leading annual planning processes, including student target setting and contributing to budget development. Preparing and monitoring the University's annual Access Agreement. Contributing to development and implementation of the Home/EU recruitment strategy. New entrant target setting and monitoring for Home/EU full-time undergraduates. Leading teaching-related bids for the University, including the recent successful bid for £5million HEFCE funding to contribute to an £11million project to build a 'Future Technology Centre'. Responding to external consultations on public policy both as an institution and as part of a wider group, including the University's response to the Government's call for evidence as part of its 'Review of Post-18 Education and Funding'. title: Recruitment, Marketing and Communications
headings: Recruitment, Marketing and Communications Marketing Portal Explore
body: The Recruitment, Marketing and Communications Department offers a complete in-house service to promote the University to its various stakeholders.Our activities cover a wide range of marketing communications disciplines including alumni and advancement, brand and corporate communications, campaigns and and marketing, marketing administration, media and communications, student recruitment and outreach and web and digital marketing. Please visit the Contact Us page for more details. The new Marketing Portal makes it easier for staff to find University images and self-serve designed material including corporate stationery. You’ll find downloadable photography, logos, document and collateral templates, business cards, letterheads and much more. The Portal complies with GDPR legislative requirements. title: Vice-Chancellor's Office
headings: Vice-Chancellor's Office Explore Related Links
body: The Vice-Chancellor leads the University Executive Board (UEB) – which comprises the Vice-Chancellor, Provost, Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Education), Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Global Engagement & Student Life), Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Research, Innovation & External Relations), Chief People Officer, Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director of Corporate Governance, Executive Deans for the Faculties of Business & Law, Creative & Cultural Industries, Humanities & Social Sciences, Science & Health and Technology and the Principal of London Campus. Members' responsibilities‌ foster high performance, and be passionate about achieving results for the University; challenge assumptions and be creative around problems and solutions; recognise the impact of their decisions on students and colleagues; be committed to enabling all staff to contribute to innovative solutions; seek to work with empathy, openness and honesty. The Executive Board will be: characterised by a trusting and enabling mindset; resilient in taking cabinet decisions and seeing them enacted; efficient and effective in managing finances and resources; enabling in its approach to supporting change across the University; committed to ensure that the entire institution is focused on the future direction of the University. The team will normally meet fortnightly to discuss strategy, policy and resourcing and to ensure the University is managed effectively. title: Staff Essentials - Disability Advice
headings: Staff Essentials Departments What Disability Advice does Jump to: Advice for Departments Academic Staff What’s changed for students? Faculty Service Centre Managers What’s changed for students? Accommodation team, Timetabling team, Fire Officer and Campus Security What’s changed for students? SITS training Guide to Reasonable Adjustments Guidance for Academic Staff on Accessible Examination and Assessment Frequently Asked Questions Additional Resources for Staff
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. Disability Advice provides a confidential service for all students with additional learning needs and can advise on the additional academic support available while they study. They can also discuss what reasonable adjustments are required and refer students to other appropriate agencies for support. Students can be advised to contact Disability Advice, in confidence, if they have a condition that might impact on their ability to access the campus, curriculum, teaching, learning, assessments and any other University service. Advice for Academic Staff Advice for Faculty Service Centre Managers Advice for Accommodation team, Timetabling team, Fire Officer and Campus Security Guidance on Accessible Examination and Assessment Frequently Asked Questions We have introduced five new reports that you will be able to filter, run and view when you need adjustment and disability information for students that require additional support. These reports are called: Adjustments report for Examination and Assessment (Including in-class tests) Adjustments report for Learning and Teaching Adjustments Report for Timetabling and Access Adjustments report Evacuation Plan Disability Support Needs Report 143 These reports are available to run in SITS and are located on the Disability Advice Page of SITS eVision. There are multiple benefits to these reports, such as: You will also have access to a ‘Staff view’ where you can search for students and see if they have adjustment or disability information available to view. If you would like some more information on the filters within these reports, please refer to the Disability Advice Academic Staff View user guide. Whilst having this information so readily available is a benefit, these reports do contain special category (disability) data that is only available to nominated staff who have been given access. Please comply with the Information and Governance guidance on personal sensitive data when using these reports. Students will now have the ability to give Consent to Share for reasonable adjustments and/or disability information using their ‘My Student View’. Reasonable adjustments will automatically Consent but Disability information will not. Where a student does not give Consent to Share, the information will not be available on the reports or Staff View. If you have any questions, please contact disabilityadvice@port.ac.uk We have introduced six new reports that you will be able to filter, run and view when you need to see the reasonable adjustments. These reports are available to run in SITS and are located on the Disability Advice Page in eVision. They are called: Adjustments report for Examination and Assessment Adjustments report for Learning and Teaching Adjustments Report for Timetabling and Access Adjustments report Evacuation Plan Adjustments report Registration Disability Support Needs Report 143 In addition to the new reports for Faculty Service Centre Managers, there are also five new reports available to nominated Academic staff that require access to view the information they require to support our students. These changes bring come some benefits to your area, such as: requests for data from UoP staff will be reduced as many will have access to their own nominated information reports have refined and more accurate filters to view new and updated student information reports have been split across five categories to help you find the information you need more efficiently, although you can still access the Disability Support Needs Report 143 in SITS if required If you would like more information on the filters within these reports, please refer to the Disability Advice Staff View user guide. Whilst having this information so readily available is a benefit, these reports do contain special category (disability) data that is only available to nominated staff who have been given access. Please comply with the Information and Governance guidance on personal sensitive information when using these reports. Students will now have the ability to modify the sharing options of their adjustment and disability information using their ‘My Student View’. Reasonable Adjustments will automatically consent, Disability Information will not. Where a student does not give Consent to Share, the information will not be available on the reports. If you have any questions, please contact disabilityadvice@port.ac.uk We have introduced reports that you will be able to filter, run and view when you need to see the reasonable adjustments. These reports are available to run in SITS and are located on the Disability Advice page of eVision. They are called: Adjustments Report for Timetabling and Access Adjustments report Evacuation Plan Adjustments report for Accommodation Disability Support Needs Report 143 These changes bring benefits to your area, such as: The reports have refined and more accurate filters to view new and updated student information You will only see reports relevant to your area of work and will to help you find the information you need more efficiently If you would like more information on the filters within these reports, please refer to the Disability Advice Staff View user guide. Whilst having this information so readily available is a benefit, these reports do contain special category (disability) data that is only available to nominated staff who have been given access. Please comply with the Information and Governance guidance on personal sensitive information when using these reports. Students will now have the ability to modify the sharing options of their adjustment and disability information using their ‘My Student View’. Reasonable Adjustment will automatically consent, disability information will not. Where a student does not give Consent to Share, the information will not be available on the reports. If you have any questions, please contact disabilityadvice@port.ac.uk Additional information on how to access the reports and guidance for students can be found on the Student Systems training site. Guidance is available to support staff to understand what is a reasonable adjustment and the duties and responsibilities we have to reduce barriers in accessing the University. Read our Guide to Reasonable Adjustments. Guidance is available for Academic Staff on making online exams and assessments more accessible. Read our Guidance for Academic Staff on Accessible Examination and Assessment. Can Academic departments step outside the Regulatory Framework for reasons related to a Disability? Academic departments may need to step outside the regulatory framework to support a student for reasons relating to disability. The Academic will need to agree with their Head of Department how they intend to step outside the regulatory framework while still allowing the student to meet the required learning outcomes. They can then ask Disability Advice to support their suggestion on Disability grounds. Should I apply Sympathetic Marking to students appearing on the Disability Advice Reports? No, Adjustment and support is given to minimise disadvantage in the assessment process but should not be applied to the Learning outcome. Do Disability Advice require Diagnostic Evidence to support a student? Disability Advice do not require a diagnosis before putting academic support in place. We may use medical evidence to help inform whether some support and adjustments are appropriate. If a student is eligible for DSA they are required to submit diagnostic evidence and we can advise them how to obtain this. What Additional funding is there to Support Disabled Students?Disabled Students who are from the UK and on a Designated courses can apply for additional academic support through the Disabled Students Allowance. This is an external process via the students funding authority and can take up to 3 months to be approved. International Students and those on courses such as the Degree Apprenticeship can still access some support through Disability Advice where a need is identified on the grounds of a Disability of Specific Learning Difference such as DyslexiaCan Disability Advice Diagnose for Autism or ADHD? Disability Advice will signpost students to discuss diagnosis with their GP. A referral may take up to two years before the student is assessed. We will discuss the impact of the difficulties experienced on learning and determine interim support while they wait to be assessed. More information on diagnosis can be found in the resources section of this page. Disability Advice is not responsible for diagnosing a Disability/Specific Learning Difference. Can Disability Advice diagnose Specific Learning Differences such as Dyslexia? Disability Advice can signpost students to qualified assessors via the following organisations: The British Psychological Society (BPS) – the BPS has an online directory of chartered psychologists they can use to find a psychologist to assess for learning differences such as Dyslexia The British Dyslexia Association – their diagnostic assessment guide outlines the steps you will need to be assessed through their service The Professional Association of Teachers of Students with SpLDs (PATOSS) – their online directory can be filtered to show teachers near you We will discuss the impact of the difficulties experienced on learning and determine interim support while they wait to be assessed. Will Disability Advice pay for a diagnosis? Disability Advice is not responsible for diagnosing a Medical condition or Specific Learning Differences. Students can approach the Student Finance Centre if they have concerns over the cost of a diagnosis. How does Disability Advice determine a reasonable adjustment? A Disability Officer or Adviser will determine a reasonable adjustment based on the information provided by the student through discussion and/or evidence submitted. Where a Disability of Specific Learning Difference is likely to impact on an area of study, we will consider adjustments that will minimise the difficulties and still allow for the student to meet the required learning outcomes. We may sometimes discuss more complex adjustments with an academic department before agreeing to put it in place. What practice competence issue will make Extra time no longer applicable in an exam? The academic department will need to decide if an adjustment for extra time is likely to negate a students’ ability to achieve the required competence standard for the profession in which they are being trained to enter. This might be due to health and safety or another professional standard. Eg assessing a students’ ability to carry out medical procedures on a patient. Do I have to let Students Record my Lectures? Students should be permitted to record lectures for their own personal use where the Disability Advice reports identify it as a reasonable adjustment. The Content Capture Policy offers further guidance for this. Diagnosis pathways for Adult ADHD - ADHD UK Pre-diagnosis support - a guide for adults who think they might be autistic - National Autistic Society Ten top tips to improve engagement with deaf students - Disability Advice Resources and guidance for educators working with deaf students -National Deaf Children's Society

title: Global
headings: Global Admin Team Exchanges and Study Abroad International Student Advisers Recruitment Team Marketing and Conversion Global Engagement Team Admissions Team Overseas Offices What do we NOT do in UoP Global? Brexit Explore
body: UoP Global is responsible for the University's support of international students, overseas partnerships and recruitment of new students from outside of the UK. It consists of several teams, dealing with wide-ranging international aspects from admissions and recruitment to visa support, student engagement, marketing and international exchange and study abroad partnerships. With a recently revamped structure, we work to deliver the aims of the Global Engagement Strategy through our activities here in the UK, as well as at our international offices across the globe. If you would like to discuss matters relating to Global activity you can contact Bobby Mehta, Director of Uop Global (bobby.mehta@port.ac.uk) or Mark Ovens, Deputy Director of UoP Global (mark.ovens@port.ac.uk). To speak with a specific team please see the information below. The Admin Team deliver outstanding customer service to enquiring students and colleagues around the University (as well as external agents and partner organisations) and providing comprehensive administrative support to the wider department, ensuring all policies and procedures are carried out in accordance with University rules and regulations and aligning with best practice. The Exchanges and Study abroad team provides advice and support to all students interested in studying or working abroad as part of their degree, as well as providing opportunities for Academic and Professional staff to undertake teaching or training activities with the Erasmus+ programme. The team is also responsible and the first point of contact for all incoming exchange and study abroad students to the University. The International Student Advice team provide immigration advice and support to international students studying at the university. They are are the only team in the university who are qualified to give visa advice to students. They also manage the International Orientation Programme and social and cultural events. The team of Global Recruitment Officers attend recruitment fairs, raise awareness of the University and promote us around the world. They each have regions that they look after and build and maintain relationships with agents, schools, partners and colleges in those regions. The Global Marketing Team supports all marketing and communications requirements for the UoP Global including digital and social media to printed publications. They are involved with raising brand awareness and the promotion of the University and its courses around the world. The Conversion Team assist with converting offer holders into students by providing details of outstanding conditions and answering applicant questions. The Global Engagement Team manage partnership agreements, visiting delegations, international student data analysis, the International Student Ambassador Scheme and outgoing International Summer Schools. The Admissions Team process Undergraduate and Postgraduate applications who have applied via an official Agent or Partner of the University. This involves assessing the applicant meets the University entry requirements for each course. The Team also produces offer letters, liaising with Agents and Partners directly over the admissions cycle. The overseas offices work closely with the Portsmouth based Global teams to market and recruit in their respective regions. issue CAS statements - DSAA: contact ukvi-student-compliance@port.ac.uk monitor student attendance - faculty contacts visa advice for staff provide advice about complex immigration advice including settlement and British citizenship book travel for staff or students provide financial support to students - Student Finance Centre process direct applications - DSAA: international.admissions@port.ac.uk advise on academic issues such as Extenuating Circumstances etc - Students’ Union Advice team: advice@upsu.net give advice on housing, money or wellbeing - we can signpost to the relevant information or services provide student status letters, bank letters or Council Tax letters - MyPort Hub: myport@port.ac.uk provide confirmation of working hours letters - BRP card and term dates on University website/MyPort - https://www.port.ac.uk/about-us/key-dates fee assessments for international students - contact feeassessment@port.ac.uk For information relating to Brexit, please visit our webpages. title: Venues
headings: Catering Services Venues Cafe Coco Chimes Cafe Library Coffee Shop Portland Coffee Shop The Hub Dennis Sciama Building 901 Internet Cafe Park Coffee Shop The Eldon Catering Facility Ravelin Sports Centre Cafe Explore
body: Cafe Coco is based in Gun House, right next to the Student Centre. It provides a warm and friendly atmosphere for staff, students and members of the public all year round. It is decorated in a retro style to give you the atmosphere of days gone by. Our friendly staff serve breakfast from 8.am until 11am, then onto a delicious affordable lunch menu served until 2pm. With healthy Fairtrade eats and sinful treats available along with bean to cup coffee. Enjoy a burger and chip if you prefer. Cafe Coco is also available for use for internal and external meeting bookings, where catering is required. We can provide an informal, relaxed atmosphere. Please contact Cafe Coco on ex 3847 to discuss availability and catering options. Term Time Opening 8.00am - 3.00pm Monday - Friday Come and visit our friendly team offering a variety of great food. The venue offers the full Starbucks range of hot drinks, along with a variety of snacks to meet all your needs. Our fantastic breakfast menu offers an extensive range of delicious breakfast items which are served daily until 11.00am. Our bacon butties are great value and will set you up for the day. Term Time Opening from 8.00am - 5.00pm Monday - Thursday, 8.00am - 4.30pm Friday Located on the ground Floor of the City Learning Centre Winston Churchill Avenue. Enjoying a Starbucks drink at campus prices. The Library coffee shop provides a welcome rest bite between studies and offers a variety of caffeine kicks to keep your attention levels piqued. There is a fast and efficient service that provides both value for money and quality, that will suit all pockets. The seating area is bright due to the floor to ceiling windows, and if you fancy eating alfresco, there is an outside seating area too. The menu has a lot to offer, from baguettes and hot pastries, to a large selection of sandwiches and snacks that can tide you over until the next study break. Term time opening hours: 8.00am - 10.00pm, Monday - Thursday, 8.00am - 8.00pm Friday, 11.00am - 3.00pm Saturday How to find us: On the ground floor, to the right of the main entrance. Our friendly staff are ready to serve you with healthy food or if you prefer sinful treats. You can also enjoy a wide range of refreshing cold drinks or hot beverage made to your taste. Term Time opening hours 8.30am - 5.00pm Monday - Thursday, 8.30am - 4.30pm Friday How to find us: On the ground floor, Portland building. Come in and enjoy the friendly welcome from the staff at the Hub. Offering a wide selection of food and fantastic daily specials. We can offer a hearty breakfast to help you start your day off alongside our popular “Your Roll” concept where you can pre-order rolls via our Facebook page, sandwiches or salads of your choice. The Hub + Great Coffee + Fresh Food = the place to be! Term Time Opening hours: 8.00am - 5.00pm Monday - Thursday, 8.00am - 4.30pm Friday How to find us: Ground floor entrance. Situated in Anglesea building, this small but perfectly formed coffee booth, offers a wide range of delicious hot and cold drinks, sandwiches and snacks. Perfect if you only have a short break and need a little pick-me-up! Open 8.45am - 2.30pm Monday - Thursday and closes at 2.00pm on Friday How to find us: On the ground floor of Anglesea. The Park coffee shop has the most cosmopolitan clientele and is situated in the second oldest building on campus. Steeped in history it has served many different purposes, from library to town hall. This large coffee shop located in the basement, has ample seating for team meetings, so why not visit and have a freshly brewed coffee at the same time? Term Time Opening hours: 8.30am - 4.00pm Monday - Friday How to find us: In the Student common room on the lower ground floor. The Courtyard catering facility in the West wing of Eldon Gallery Space offers a relaxed atmosphere to enjoy the art on show. The catering team provide a large selection of cold drinks, hot beverages made to your taste, freshly made sandwiches, sub rolls, salad pots, soups and snacks all at competitive prices. Term Time Opening hours: 8.30am - 4.30pm Monday - Thursday, 8.30am - 4.00pm Friday How to find us: On the ground floor, turn right as you come through the front door from Middle Street. Serving hot and cold drinks and snacks and a full range of Starbuck drinks at campus prices. 8.00am - 8.00pm Monday - Friday, 9.00am - 6.00pm Saturday and Sunday How to find us: Located opposite the entrance on the ground floor of the sports centre Venues Location maps Vending facilities Contact and departments

title: Travel to work
headings: Human Resources Travel to work Sustainable travel Explore
body: As a University, we aim to reduce congestion in Portsmouth and improve the air quality for everyone who studies, lives and works in our city. A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. Leaving the car at home and making sustainable travel choices can save you time and money. You may also benefit from improved wellbeing and reduce your environmental impact at the same time. Whether you’re looking for information about local cycle routes to work, walking maps or links to public transport you can access it and plan your journey on the My Journey Hampshire website. You can also commute together with others by using easitShare. This scheme covers journeys made by car, bicycle, taxi, motorbikes, train, buses and on foot.
