title: Visitor car parking
headings: Estates and Campus Services Visitor car parking Out of Hours Parking Explore
body: Spaces are only reserved for Corporate Business Use via Departments. Staff inviting visitors to the University must make them aware of the University’s parking regulations. Visitors must display a car parking scratch card in their vehicle’s windscreen. Visitors can park either in a reserved visitors’ bay or any standard car parking space available. Reserved bays are for the sole use of visitors on business only – the University has centralised visiting bays in two main areas on the campus, in St Andrew’s Court (This car park is for visitors only staff are not permitted to park) One-day scratch card parking permits can be purchased from the MyPort Information Hub in the Nuffield Centre. Each scratch card is valid only for the day of use and the cost is £2. To make a reservation for a visitor, please email parking.reservations@port.ac.uk with your request, specifying the date, time, location, the name of the visitor and the registration number of their vehicle. All requests for car parking spaces must be made no later than 3pm on the day before the day the reservation is required. Please note: visitor spaces are limited and cannot be guaranteed. Any permit holder may park in a designated visitors’ area as long as they remove their car 30 minutes prior to the arrival time indicated on the A4 post. If a visitor space has not been used after 1 hour of the stated arrival time, the bay reverts to being an available space. Please note: This excludes St Andrew's Court. Students and contractors are not permitted to purchase scratch cards. Please see link below for any additional Public Parking when attending Interviews , Open Days or External Events held in our University Buildings. https://mapsengine.google.com/map/edit?mid=zxrsmmtqI7U8.k78xQjPKzD30 Due to limited parking availability on our city centre campus, we cannot reserve parking spaces for our interview candidates. Separate parking arrangements are made for our large recruitment events, such as Open Days. We would encourage you to use public transport where possible. The University is a gentle 10 minute walk from Portsmouth & Southsea train station, and National Express coaches have nearby coach stops. Car parking is available in all public city centre car parks for a small charge, as well as on the roads using parking meters. We now offer 'Pay & Display' parking during the hours of 5.00 pm - 8.00 am Monday - Friday and all day at weekends. This facility is available to all users in the following car parks: Langstone Campus (Locksway Road) This car park operates 24/7 Pay & Display If you need any further information or advice please ring the Car Parking Office on 02392 842805 or email car.parking@port.ac.uk. title: Breastfeeding and expressing facilities
headings: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Use our breastfeeding and expressing facilities Pod location and booking Accessing and using the rooms Explore
body: There are two dedicated breastfeeding and expressing facilities on campus. These facilities are designed to provide a comfortable and private space for breastfeeding and expressing milk, and to help ease the transition back to work for new mothers, promote the well-being of both parent and child, and uphold our commitment to celebrate diversity and equal opportunity through an inclusive culture. The two pods feature a comfortable chair with plug sockets and a small table, and can be locked for privacy. The facilities are bookable and are accessed via a combination lock. The pods are located in: the University Library, on the second floor opposite the lift; Richmond Building, fifth floor. Locate the key code: the key code for accessing the room is provided in the automatic booking acceptance email Use the key code: enter the key code on the box located on the outside of the pod, then turn the dial to open to access the fob key Hold the key fob to the scanner: once you have the key fob, hold it to the scanner to unlock the door Lock the door: after entering, lock the door from the inside to ensure uninterrupted use Keep the room neat and tidy for the next person, and take any personal items or waste with you after use Return the fob: after using the room, please return the key fob to the key press so it’s ready for the next user If you experience any issues using the rooms, please report these to estates.helpdesk@port.ac.uk. headings: The VC Awards Awards categories Internationalisation Green Impact Outstanding Contribution to Equity, Diversity and Inclusion Outstanding Contribution to Civic Impact and Community Engagement Partnerships, Collaboration & Knowledge Exchange Professional Services Excellence Research & Innovation Excellence Student Experience and Outcomes Teaching Excellence Technician of the Year - NEW 2026 School/Department of the Year - NEW 2026 Outstanding Internal Collaboration - NEW 2026 Shortlisting The Vice-Chancellor's Awards for Excellence event Make a nomination Your nomination should: Related news Staff celebrated at the Vice-Chancellor’s Awards for Excellence 2025 Staff and students celebrated at the Vice-Chancellor’s Awards for Excellence 2023 We use cookies
body: Recognising our staff, alumni and local community Our people are at the heart of our success and our colleagues continue to make exceptional contributions to the University and the wider community. The Vice-Chancellor’s Awards for Excellence recognise and celebrate staff, alumni and members of the community who have made an exceptional contribution to the University, its values, its ambition and its success, as set out in the University Strategy. The awards complement the newly established Student Impact Awards, which recognise the achievements of our students and their contributions to our city and wider region. Awarded to an individual or team that demonstrates an outstanding contribution, activity or collaboration that shows the University operating with global vision and ambition. Awarded to an individual or team that demonstrates commitment to environmental issues, encompassing innovative approaches and showing clear evidence that the work is making a difference. Awarded to an individual or team initiative through which colleagues and/or students are treated fairly and with respect, and where diversity is championed. Initiatives may focus on colleagues or the student body, or both, and can be either innovative in themselves, or based on implementing ideas or practice from within or outside the University. Awarded to an individual or team that has made a significant contribution to aid the wider community, whether socially, culturally or economically. Awarded to an individual or team that has achieved an outstanding partnership with a business or an industry – regional, national or international – where knowledge is applied and resources are shared for the benefit of the wider economy. Awarded to an individual or professional services team that demonstrates excellence, and is making a significant contribution to achieving our University Strategy and values. Awarded to an individual or team that demonstrates research excellence and/or innovation that significantly advances knowledge and/or delivers tangible benefits to wider society. Awarded to an individual or a team initiative that has positively enhanced the academic and/or personal experience of our student body, including enhancing our student outcomes or helping our students develop and demonstrate the Hallmarks of a Portsmouth Graduate. Awarded to an individual or academic team that has delivered an outstanding teaching initiative or learning support that inspires student learning and attainment through innovative pedagogy. Awarded to an individual or team that demonstrates exceptional technical expertise, innovation, and dedication to supporting teaching, research, and operations, and is committed to improving technical services to enhance the student experience Awarded to an academic Department or School that has demonstrated outstanding achievements in teaching, research, and student experience, making a significant positive impact on the University community. Awarded to a team or individuals that demonstrate effective collaboration across different disciplines to deliver outcomes with significant impact and align with the university's strategic goals. Nominees will be shortlisted in each award category after the nominations close on Friday 1 May 2026. The shortlisting panel is made up of members of the University Executive Board, Staff Networks, Students’ Union Officers and previous award winners. Nominations will be assessed on the outcomes and impact of the activity. Nominees and their nominators will be informed of the outcomes after the panel has met in May. Shortlisted nominees and their nominators will be invited to our Awards event, where winners of the Vice-Chancellor's Awards for Excellence will be announced. The event will be hosted by the Vice-Chancellor and other members of the University Executive Board. The VC Awards for Excellence offer a wonderful opportunity to recognise individuals who consistently exceed expectations, both within the University and in the wider Portsmouth community. Professor Graham Galbraith CBE, University Vice-Chancellor You can nominate University staff, or any community members who work in partnership with the University, who have made an exceptional contribution to the University, its values, its ambition and its success, as set out in the University Strategy. Your nomination can recognise an individual or a team. You can select one award category per nomination, but can submit more than one nomination. Staff and community members involved with the University can make a nomination but cannot nominate themselves. Nominees must not be made aware of their nomination. Nominations must be received by Friday 1 May 2026, and can be submitted by completing this form. Include clear evidence that your nominee meets the award criteria. We’ll shortlist nominees using only the information that you provide. Demonstrate the positive impact your nominee has had. This impact may have been on the University, or a city or region in the UK or overseas, and can be a one-off or ongoing activity. Provide as much detail as you can to evidence the impact and reach of the activities of the nominee. Make it clear how the achievement is above and beyond the individual or team’s job. Submit your nomination We may use cookies to analyse visitor data, improve our website, show personalised content and give you a great website experience. title: About us
headings: We're a new breed of university Welcome to the University of Portsmouth Our story Our Strategy Our facilities Our ambition Structure and governance Key dates Working at Portsmouth Contact us Our London campus You may also be interested in Policies and standards Freedom of Information Publications Our staff Maps and directions We use cookies
body: Find out how we make a positive difference to the world and how you can join us We’re proud to be a breath of fresh air in the academic world – a place where everyone gets the support they need to achieve their best. We’re always discovering. We don’t fit the mould, we break it. Just as the world keeps moving, so do we. We’re closely involved with our local community and we take our ideas into the global marketplace. We partner with business, industry and government to set the course for a better future. And we educate and transform the lives of our students to help them achieve their potential. We stand out, not just in the UK but in the world. We excel in research areas from cosmology and astrophysics to cyber security, forensics and sport science. Since the first day we opened our doors, we've looked towards the future. And we're here to help you shape it. Study in London at the University of Portsmouth, in the heart of Waltham Forest. Our purpose-built, capital city campus puts world-class teaching and industry connections on your doorstep, allowing you to fuel your ambition in one of the world's greatest financial centres. title: Employee Assistance Programme (EAP)
headings: Human Resources Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) Accessing the Helpline Is it confidential? Explore
body: Supporting the mental health and wellbeing of all our colleagues is incredibly important to us, and our new Employee Assistance Programme will help us do just that. Whether you’re facing problems at work or at home, you can access impartial, independent and entirely confidential advice from qualified counsellors for any issues you may be facing, such as Anxiety; Bereavement; Stress; Depression; Workplace Issues; Trauma; Relationships; Finances and debt; and Family Difficulties. Confidential 24/7 Helpline: The telephone helpline is available 24/7, 365 days a year and is completely confidential and to anyone needing mental health support. To access the helpline, simply call 0800 023 9324 from any standard UK landline or mobile phone Digital Resources and Self-Help:A wide offering of resources is also available and includes downloadable self-help workbooks, debt advice and more. Visit the EAP Platform to find out more: www.port.ac.uk/myeap The University does not know who uses the service unless the individual personally chooses to tell someone about their contact with Vivup. Vivup do provide statistics to the University to show how many employees use the service and the broad types of issues raised, for example; ‘relationship breakdown at home’ or ‘bullying and harassment in the workplace. No information is ever passed on which could potentially identify an individual. title: Dental Academy Patient Services
headings: Patient care You can access our dental services if: Treatments you can get as one of our patients: Patient applications Opening hours and contact information Patient satisfaction Quarterly reports Dental Services Privacy Notice Visit us Dental Academy Information for GDPs Referrals Periodontics Endodontics Extractions Paediatrics How to refer a patient All referrals should be posted to: Closures For dental students For hygiene and therapy students Useful documents We use cookies
body: Operated under an NHS contract and located in the University's Dental Academy, our dental practice is where our dental students can provide high-quality dental care to patients, for free, under the supervision of our qualified staff. Our student dental teams are skilled at their work but operate slower than qualified professionals, so appointments can take longer than expected. You don't currently have a dentist You're available during the day for long appointments, typically around 1.5 hours You can come to multiple appointments Fillings and preventative treatment Crowns and bridgework Oral health promotion Please visit our page on the NHS website to find out if we're currently accepting patients. The Dental Academy is open 8.30am – 5.00pm Monday to Thursday, and until 4.00pm on Fridays. We are closed on weekends. You can contact the Dental Academy at 023 9284 5285. We're continually improving our patient services. At the end of each course of treatment we encourage our patients to share their experiences with us. We ask our patients for their views and opinions through our patient satisfaction surveys, NHS Friends and Family Test (FFT), and our comments book to see where we can improve. Patient comments and satisfaction ratings are collected and put together in bi-annual reports which are shared with all staff, students and NHS England. These reports can also be seen by patients and members of the public online and on display in our waiting area The Dental Academy is committed to using your personal data responsibly. Our Privacy Notice explains what personal data the practice holds, why we hold and process it, who we might share it with and your rights and freedoms under the Law. For more information on your rights, read our Individual Rights Request form. A minimum of one course of treatment and a maximum of two courses will be offered with the aim of leading to stabilisation of disease. Details of the patient’s smoking history and current level of oral hygiene must be included on referral. Basic Periodontal Examination (BPE) scores of 2, 2*, 3, 3*, 4 or 4* in at least 2 sextants BPE scores of 1 or 1* patients requiring specialist periodontal care should be referred directly to a specialist periodontal care provider and not to the Dental Academy conventional endodontics on all teeth if appropriate (i.e. restorable or of strategic value to the restorative care of the patient) teeth requiring retreatment, removal of posts or apicectomy are outside the scope of treatment at the Dental Academy if the tooth is unrestorable, the patient will be referred back with extraction recommended extractions which are appropriate for primary care and do not require hospital facilities surgical or potential surgical extractions of 8s patients receiving bisphosphonate therapy/patients considered to be at high risk of developing bisphosphonate associated osteonecrosis of the jaw (BONJ) patients with a highly complex medical history or anatomy patients who are very anxious/require sedation Patients requiring one or more restorations which are appropriate for primary care Patient can tolerate treatment under local anaesthesia All referrals must have recent good quality intraoral radiographs (within last six months) of teeth requiring treatment Patients who are very anxious/require sedation Patients with special needs or complex medical history Orthodontic assessment or orthodontic extractions Patients requiring general anaesthesia extraction Please refer patients using our GDP referral forms: Adult - GDP Referral Form 2024 (PDF) Paediatric – GDP Referral Form‌ (PDF) GDP Referrals Dental Academy Reception William Beatty Building Hampshire Terrace Portsmouth PO1 2QG Your patients should be made aware that failure to attend appointments will result in discharge back to their GDP. We close during student examinations and holidays. This may delay treatments and reduce the number of referrals we can accept at these times. Christmas holiday: Monday 18 December 2023 – Friday 5 January 2024 Easter holiday and exam period: Monday 4 March – Friday 12 April 2024 Christmas holiday: Monday 18 December 2023 – Friday 19 January 2024 Easter holiday: Monday 1 April – Friday 19 April 2024 Examination period: Monday 13 May – Friday 7 June 2024 Clinical criteria and thresholds New clinical referral arrangements Q&A We may use cookies to analyse visitor data, improve our website, show personalised content and give you a great website experience. title: The Chaplaincy
headings: Our Chaplaincy What is the Chaplaincy? Speak to us Events and activities Contact the Chaplaincy Out of hours support London campus students We use cookies
body: Confidential support, whatever your religion or beliefs Life can be full of surprises and sometimes it can be difficult to cope. Whatever troubles you face whilst at uni – a sudden change in circumstances, a loss of some kind or a tough decision – our University Chaplains can offer support, guidance and a willing ear. The Chaplaincy Centre and Prayer Rooms can be found on St Pauls Road. It is open to all students and staff for a confidential chat, somewhere to have lunch with friends, as a place to pray or just find some quiet space. We'll listen to you confidentially about whatever you want to explore. Our Chaplains have pastoral skills that can help with establishing values, meaning and purpose for living. The Chaplaincy Centre & Prayer Rooms are now open with the following times: Monday – Thursday: 9.00am – 5.00pm Friday: 9.00am – 4.00pm This area is a great place to get away and to meet new friends. If you need a talk, this is the place. If you want to join a religious society, this is the place. I enjoy this place because the atmosphere is stress-free and you can always talk to staff. 2nd year student, Anonymous The Chaplaincy is a safe and inclusive social space. We're open to all students, regardless of your values and beliefs. Free tea and coffee is available every day during the week. Confidential support – drop in and ask to see a Chaplain for a one-to-one chat, or to make an appointment email us at chaplains@port.ac.uk Islamic Prayer Rooms – now open in the Chaplaincy Centre in St Paul's Road, for Muslim staff and students, with separate ablution facilities for Brothers and Sisters Multi-faith Room - a quiet space for reflection, stillness and personal prayer, open to everyone Events - throughout the year, including Mug Painting during Freshers Week, and supporting events held by many other Uni departments and the Student Union Our Chaplains will listen and support you. We'll: listen non-judgmentally encourage you to develop and grow support you for as long as it takes Your visit and conversations are kept confidential unless you give us permission to tell someone else. If we think you're a risk to yourself or others, we may need to tell someone, but we'll discuss this with you first if we can. We provide support and can tell you about other services across the University and city for issues such as: Faith issues and concerns Anxiety and depression The Chaplaincy offers you an opportunity to build relationships with people from other cultures, faiths and religions. We also have the resources and contact details for local faith groups. If you are unsure about how to approach someone from a different cultural or faith background to yourself, then the Chaplains will be able to help. There are guidelines for building relations with people of different faiths and beliefs, available both nationally, and within the University. We can direct you to groups and organisations that specialise in building interfaith relationships. The Chaplaincy is a marvellous place to spend some free time. If you want, you can get into conversation with others, play games or seek advice about something. Or if you prefer, you can sit on your own, quietly, reading a book or simply listening to Classic FM Radio, recovering from the exertion of university life. Whichever you choose, you will be glad you went. Staff member, Anonymous Throughout the year, the Chaplaincy team organise social events for students and staff to meet new people within a peaceful and impartial environment. Activities include: Carol Singing around campus Everyone in the University community is welcome to visit and participate in our social events, speak to us, socialise with friends or use the space for quiet time and reflection. Chaplaincy Centre and Prayer Rooms, St Pauls Road, Southsea, PO5 4AQ We are open 9.00am to 5.00pm Monday to Thursday, and Fridays from 9.00am to 4.00pm. You can also find us on Instagram @uopstudentlife For 24/7 urgent welfare support, call our free confidential helpline on 0800 028 3766. If you're attending our London campus, you can contact studentlifelondon@port.ac.uk for further information about faith organisations in your local area. title: Display Screen Equipment (DSE)
headings: Health, Safety and Compliance Health and Safety Introduction Defining DSE Users Request an eye test DSE Assessment Key Contacts DSE, Eyecare Information, etc. Computers, Software, etc. Health concerns or issues Useful Resources Explore
body: Display Screen Equipment (DSE) are devices such as screen monitors, laptops, touch screens and other similar devices. Overuse or improper use of DSE can cause fatigue, eye strain, upper limb problems and backache to staff or students. These problems can also be experienced from poorly designed workstations or work environments. The causes may not always be obvious and can be due to a combination of factors. The use of DSE is governed by the Health and Safety (Display Screen Equipment) Regulations. In order to comply with these regulations, the University of Portsmouth provides employees with: An eye test if a worker requires one (habitual users) Display Screen Equipment awareness training and assessment Training and information for workers Occupational Health Referrals The DSE regulations define a user as an individual who habitually uses display screen equipment as a significant part of their normal work. As a guide, an individual is considered a user if they have sessions of continuous DSE use for more than 1 hour nearly every day. A large amount of display screen equipment work, particularly that carried out by academics and other researchers, is intermittent. Therefore in these cases, the total time spent using display screen equipment over a period of a week should be considered and the following guide used: If the average is about 2 hours a day or more, the individual is considered a user If the average is less than 1 hour then, subject to an examination of the type of usage, the individual is not defined as a user If the average is between 1 and 2 hours, the type of usage must be taken into account Members of staff can request an eyesight test via the Health and Safety Hub, and via the link below: Request a Eye Test Voucher Upon receipt an eyesight test voucher, staff members will then need to make their own arrangements with any Specsavers branch to have an eye test. The Specsavers voucher will entitle the user to a free eye test and £45 towards the cost of glasses, if solely for DSE use. Please note that this scheme does not include the provision of contact lenses. Should staff members wish to upgrade to more expensive frames, lenses or varifocals, this may be done at their own expense. The Health, Safety and Compliance Team provide online DSE training and assessment. This training is mandatory for all University staff and must be completed every 3 years, or sooner if circumstances change, such as new equipment, furniture, or a change in desk setup. The training can be found through the link below: For any queries or issues concerning accessing this training, please email hsservicedesk@port.ac.uk. For any further queries or information, please find the relevant contact details below: E: hsservicedesk@port.ac.uk E: servicedesk@port.ac.uk E: admin@cordellhealth.co.uk This information can also be found via the following link: Display Screen Equipment (DSE) Please contact the Health and Safety Office if you require further information Health and Safety Team Contact and departments

title: Sodexo Scheme Closure
headings: Human Resources Explore
body: University of Portsmouth will no longer use our existing provider for discounts. My Discounts will therefore no longer be managed by Sodexo moving forwards. Employees will no longer have access to the site as of 01/10/23. Please see the below FAQ’s to help with any queries you may have: I have purchased unused e-vouchers; how do I access these once the site is closed? We suggest all employees download their e vouchers prior to 01/10/23. Employees who have not been able to do this can email us at employeebenefits@sodexoengage.com and we will provide employees with the link manually by email. I have purchased unused cinema e-codes. How do I access these once the scheme is closed? We suggest all employees download their ecodes prior to the 01/10/23. Employees who have not been able to do this can email us at employeebenefits@sodexoengage.com and we will provide employees with the link manually by email. Am I able to reload my reloadable card after the scheme is closed? No, but funds will remain on cards to drawdown, retailer T&Cs and expiry date still apply. I have an existing holiday booking; what are the implications for me after the scheme has closed? There will be no impact for employees that already have a booking with the travel team. They will have our contact details and reference numbers on their paperwork so will be able to contact us directly. Call 01908 303531 or email travel.uk@sodexo.com and our dedicated travel team will be able to help. Can I use my spree card after the scheme has closed? Employees will be able to use their Spree cards until their card expires (2 years). Any cashback outstanding – you can call customer care and they will arrange for any funds due to be transferred to the nominated bank account. I have purchased paper vouchers and have not yet used them, are they still valid? Yes, you can use the vouchers, however you will not be able to order further vouchers. I have cinema vouchers and e-codes, can I still use these? Yes, they can still be used until they reach their expiry date. We are here to help - get in touch and we will aim to respond to your query within 48 hours: Mon-Fri: 9.00am-5.30pm Or email our Customer Care team at employeebenefits@sodexoengage.com Any further questions, please direct these to your HR team at reward@port.ac.uk My Role My Responsibilities My Benefits Portsmouth FC Mortgage Advice Car Benefit Scheme Holiday Purchase Scheme My Pay Leave and Absence Managing My Contract Developing My Career Ensuring My Health, Safety and Wellbeing Dealing With Change My Benefits Portsmouth FC Mortgage Advice Car Benefit Scheme Holiday Purchase Scheme Holiday Purchase Scheme Ensuring My Health, Safety and Wellbeing HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Occupational Health Service Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

title: Car Benefit Scheme
headings: Human Resources Car Benefit Scheme Eligibility Access the scheme today New car has arrived? About Tusker Explore
body: The Car Benefit Scheme is brought to you by CPC Drive in partnership with Tusker, and is a new way for University employees to run a car. A fixed monthly amount is taken directly from your gross salary and in return you get the use of a brand new electric or plug-in hybrid car. The reduction to your gross salary is made before income tax and National Insurance (NI) although, under the scheme you will need to pay Benefit in Kind as the car is being provided as a benefit by your employer. There are additional savings available on pure electric vehicles as Benefit in Kind is set at just 2% until April 2025, increasing by 1% each year until April 2028. You can choose a car from a number of leading manufacturers and every car comes complete with: Maintenance of the vehicle including all servicing, batteries, exhausts and tyres; Fully comprehensive motor insurance including all business travel for you; Lifestyle protections; Roadside assistance (homestart & recovery UK & European cover); Relief car for when your car is off the road (if selected). What’s more, there is no deposit needed to secure a car on this scheme. In order to access the car benefit scheme, you must: be a permanent employee or on a fixed term contract where your contract of employment is for a longer period than the proposed Salary Sacrifice agreement; have completed a minimum of 6 months service and not be in receipt of any warnings under the disciplinary policy; and your gross salary after taking into account all salary sacrifice arrangements must be above the National Minimum Wage. To get a quote and see the range of cars available simply visit My Benefits and log in. Then select the Car Benefit tile to create an account and access the scheme. If you need a hand with the site, or you want to discuss ordering a car, you can get in contact by phone: 0333 400 7431 or email: EETeam@tuskerdirect.com. Once you have taken delivery of your new car, if you intend to park on University premises and already have a permit, please ensure you update the registration details of your new car on both the permit and the Evology parking sites. If you would like to obtain a permit please visit the Staff Parking pages to find information about the restrictions. Full details can also be found in the Car Parking Policy and Regulations. Tusker has been committed to reducing emissions and moving towards sustainable transport for a long time having been a carbon neutral organisation for over 10 years now. Tusker is a founding member of the EV100 (a group of organisations championing the move to zero emissions) and has multiple awards for its commitment to the environment, having offset over 200,000 tonnes of carbon through various verified carbon offsetting projects overseas and in the UK. title: Holiday Purchase Scheme
headings: Human Resources Holiday Purchase Scheme Explore
body: The Holiday Purchase Scheme application window for the 2025/26 leave year is now closed. 2025/26 Holiday Purchase Timeline Wednesday 16 July to Friday 22 August - Application Window Open Sunday 31 August - Management Review and Approvals Final Deadline Friday 12 September - Deadline for HR letter confirming costs and terms to be sent to applicant via ESS Tuesday 30 September - Final deadline for applicant to sign agreement letter and confirm payment option in ESS The Holiday Purchase Scheme allows eligible employees to apply to purchase up to 5 days (37 hours) additional time off (pro-rata for part-time employees) in a leave year, over and above existing entitlements to holidays, public holidays and University closure days. Provides flexibility to achieve the best possible balance between work and personal commitments Ensures a clear, equitable, and consistent approach to considering applications, and outlines the circumstances in which an application may be declined under management review Permits the cost to be spread over the entire leave year via a salary sacrifice arrangement (reducing costs of tax and national insurance), or optionally a non-salary sacrifice arrangement deducted after tax, national insurance and other statutory deductions have already been made Does not permit the selling of untaken or unwanted annual leave Is available to salaried permanent and fixed-term employees whose contract extends beyond the end of the upcoming leave year Please read the Holiday Purchase Scheme Guide in full prior to undertaking any application or management review, which covers further information on the policy, eligibility, impact on salary and approvals process. Applications may only be submitted via Employee Self Service (ESS) during the application window. It is recommended that these are submitted as soon as possible, and that applicants check with individual line management first to establish any team deadlines and expectations. Applications will be considered by line management in accordance with scheme policy, who should approve requests so long as it will not cause unmanageable operational or workload impacts, or additional costs to the University. Management may also approve with conditions, such as how much may be purchased or when it may be taken. Final management decisions must be confirmed via Management Self Service (MSS) no later than 31st August. Applicants will then receive correspondence via ESS confirming costs and terms of the agreement, which must be signed by 30th September. For instructions on how to apply and review requests in ESS and MSS, please follow our step-by-step guidance. This scheme is supplementary to the University’s policies on annual leave/holiday and other leave entitlements. It is discretionary and does not confer any contractual entitlement. The University reserves the right to amend or withdraw the scheme at any time, without notice, where doing so is in the interests of the University. title: Mortgage Advice
headings: Human Resources Free mortgage education, advice and brokerage services Explore
body: A new partnership with Charles Cameron & Associates (CC&A) provides all university colleagues with mortgage education, advice, and brokerage services for free. Whether you are a first-time buyer looking to re-mortgage or finance an investment property, they can help you access the best possible financial solutions for your needs. Their services include: Educational webinars tailored for all life and purchase stages; Free, flexible video or telephone meetings with a dedicated adviser; Full application support; 24/7 mortgage information, available via an online tool; Affordability calculator and downloadable report; CC&A Mortgage Pledge – a mortgage tracking service for all existing homeowners to ensure you are never paying more for your mortgage than you need to; Topical newsletters and buyer guides. The Benefits of their services are: Tailored mortgage advice for First Time Buyers, Home Movers, Re-mortgagers and Buy-To-Let Landlords, including Equity Release, Commercial and Bridging Loans; Access to preferential rates unavailable to customers directly; Totally independent and whole of market advice; Completely Broker Fee Free for both yourself and your family & friends; Sustainability pledge – CC&A will plant a tree for every mortgage arranged. Not only will CC&A source the best possible mortgage product for your circumstances, but they will also save you time by taking care of all the paperwork involved – chasing the lender, surveyor and conveyancers, then updating you all the way through to completion, making the whole process as stress-free as possible. To book an individual online appointment or to access their educational content visit the CC&A website, email: info@ccameron.co.uk or call 020 7680 7139. Important Disclaimer: Any advice provided to you by CC&A is a strictly individual arrangement between yourself and CC&A. The University of Portsmouth will not be liable for that advice or any actions you take as a result of it under any circumstances. title: Tuition fee discount
headings: Human Resources Tuition fees discount PhD/Professional Doctorate support for Academic Staff Funded Apprenticeships Explore
body: The University offers a 30% discount to self-funding, Student Loans Company funded and externally sponsored University of Portsmouth staff members undertaking part-time HE3 courses. Eligible employees should email income@port.ac.uk to apply for the staff discount, no more than four weeks prior to the course start date. Application is necessary for each year of registration. The discount is only available for the duration of employment with the University. The University also offers a 30% discount to children of University staff* members undertaking undergraduate and postgraduate taught, full-time and part-time study. The discount is available to self-funding, Student Loans Company funded and externally sponsored students who are studying programmes delivered by the University of Portsmouth. The discount is not applicable to students studying franchised or collaborative provision. Children of University staff are defined as biological, step or adopted, aged under 25 on 31 August in each academic year of registration. Eligible employees/students should enquire by email at: income@port.ac.uk. Note that the discount cannot be fully confirmed until the student is registered on the course due to the criteria set for staff eligibility. This application for discount does not affect the UCAS application process. Applications for funding via Student Loans Company should be made quoting the full course fee. The University will submit a change request to reduce the liability when the discount is confirmed. The detailed conditions and how to apply are set out in the Tuition Fee Discounts Policy (pdf). * A staff member needs to be on a permanent or fixed-term contract of employment (with at least a year to run) with at least one year’s continuous service with the University. The University will waive the tuition fees for academic staff (those on full or fractional contracts) wishing to commence a doctoral programme of study in the Faculty in which they are based. This will apply providing such study concords with the strategic interests of the Faculty, and a suitable local supervisory team can be found. For more information, please consult the Faculty Sponsored Doctorates for Academic Staff document. If you are interested in exploring this opportunity further then please discuss with your Head of School in the first instance. NOTE: This opportunity is only available to those academic staff who currently (i) do not possess and (ii) are not already enrolled on a programme of doctoral study. Staff in various roles across the University can complete an apprenticeship delivered by the University of Portsmouth or an external provider. Degree Apprenticeships offer staff the opportunity to develop skills and knowledge in a professional field while working towards the completion of a degree. This is particularly suitable for those in the early stages of their careers. Apprenticeships for University staff are funded by the Apprenticeship Levy, meaning that staff can achieve a full degree without the financial costs. Non-Degree Apprenticeships are available for staff using external providers. Find out more about apprenticeships and how you can learn whilst you earn. We invite applications from existing University staff who are on an open-ended contract, or a fixed term contract that does not expire before the end of the selected course. All staff must have the support of their line manager prior to applying. If you have any questions, please contact staff-apprenticeships@port.ac.uk. title: Portsmouth FC
headings: Human Resources Portsmouth Football Club - Free and Discounted Tickets Free Tickets Discounted Tickets Portsmouth FC Women - Ticket Ballot Explore
body: Through its partnership with Portsmouth Football Club, the University has access to spectator ticket seating at Fratton Park for each of the club's men's first team home matches. For the 2023/24 season, the University has launched a staff-wide ballot for these free of charge spectator seats at Fratton Park.The allocation is for stadium seats only. No travel or wider in-stadium benefits are offered by the club for these seats. Staff can apply for tickets via this Google Form. You can apply for a range of men's first team matches running from 29th July 2023 to 20 April 2024 to either attend as a work group (up to six tickets per request), which could be colleagues, students or external partners, or to take a friend/ family member as a plus one (two tickets per request). Tickets will be drawn at random for each match and staff will be notified by email if they are successful. Ballots will be done in two month intervals to ensure staff have the chance to enter throughout the season. E-tickets will then be emailed out prior to each match date. If you're unsuccessful in the ballot or want to be sure of seeing a match, as a member of staff you can watch Pompey in action at Fratton Park for a discounted price of £16. To book, first register with the Pompey e-ticket site then, once logged in, click on this link to access the £16 discounted University of Portsmouth tickets. Once you’ve selected your seat in your match of choice, scroll down the Price Class Options to find the University of Portsmouth ticket. And remember to take proof of University I.D. with you to the match for spot checks at the turnstiles. Through its partnership with Portsmouth Football Club, University staff have the opportunity to enter the ballot for tickets to watch the Portsmouth FC Women's team in action.The semi-professional side play their home matches at Westleigh Park, Havant in the National League Southern Premier Division. Staff can request up to four tickets per match to attend with colleagues, friends or family via this Google Form. Ticket winners will be drawn at random for each match and staff will be notified by email if they are successful. Ballots will be done in two month intervals to ensure staff have the chance to enter throughout the season. E-tickets will then be emailed out prior to each match date. title: Pensions
headings: Human Resources Your Pensions & What you Need to Know Explore
body: This section directs you to the information you need to know about your pension, what it is worth to you, and what it means to be a member. We aim to point you to the right information throughout your membership, from starting your career to preparing for life in retirement. We have employees in a range of pension schemes, depending on how you are employed, when you were employed, and what role(s) you hold. Follow the appropriate links below for further information (if you aren't sure what scheme you are in, check your contract or payslip): Local Government Pension Scheme (LGPS) - available for University of Portsmouth employees in Support, Administrative, Professional Services who started prior to August 2024 and all non-teaching Research roles Teachers' Pension Scheme (TPS) - available to University of Portsmouth employees in academic teaching roles who are expected to undertake research. Teaching roles with no research duties, such as Teaching Fellows, are enrolled MyPensionPlus instead (see below) MyPensionPlus - for permanent and fixed term employees of UoP Academic Services Limited (UASL) Portsmouth from August 2024 UASL NEST - for casual, zero-hours and hourly paid workers at UoP Academic Services Limited (UASL) Portsmouth from August 2024 NHS Pension Scheme - new employees in eligible healthcare roles may be able to remain in their NHS Pension if they are on a medical register and were in NHS scheme employment prior to joining Portsmouth Aviva for London Campus - for UoP Academic Services Limited (UASL) London campus employees only NEST (Other Subsidiaries) - available for ASTA Technology UK Ltd. and University of Portsmouth Enterprise Ltd. workers Additionally, members of all schemes should keep up to date via our News & Updates section, and can read more in the Additional Information section, which contains some general hints and tips, and information on the State Pension. If you have question about your pension, contact details for each scheme are provide via the above links. If you have a specific question for us, or a form you need to provide us, please do so via email at pensionenquiries@port.ac.uk or by post at University of Portsmouth Pensions, Floor 6 Mercantile House, Hampshire Terrace, PO1 2EG. OUR FUNCTIONIt is our role in the University of Portsmouth Pensions team to let you know where to go to find information about your scheme(s) and what you need to do when making decisions or taking action related to your pension. We undertake the employer duties for the schemes, such as authorising actions, confirming data is accurate and resolving issues where necessary. We are also here to help explain and clarify if there is something you aren't sure about, and escalate to the schemes where required. DISCLAIMERThe University of Portsmouth is not your pension provider and the Pensions team acts in the 'Employer' role between yourself (the 'Member') and your pension (the 'Scheme'). This means that while we are here to help, most pension queries you have will be best directed to the schemes themselves, where they have dedicated contact centres to help you. We are not Financial Advisors, and nothing contained within our pages may be seen as financial advice. Your pension scheme will not provide financial advice directly either. This means that we are not able to answer questions such as 'what should I do' or 'what is best for me' - we can only provide you with the information and let you make the decision yourself. Where this is not completely clear to you, we would always recommend you do pursue Independent Financial Advice, with a qualified advisor who is familiar with pensions and your particular scheme. title: Pay, Reward and Benefits
headings: Human Resources Pay, reward and benefits Employee benefits Pensions Life Assurance Grading of Roles Explore
body: This section provides information about grading of roles, employee benefits and pensions. Information about the range of benefits and entitlements available to current members of University staff. This is also available under My Role. Information and links to the schemes available to University employees can be found here. The three main occupational pension schemes (TP, LGPS and NHS) provide death in service cover to members for three times their salary. In order to support staff who have decided to opt out of the occupational pension schemes, the University has put in place equivalent life assurance cover. Staff covered by this policy will be contacted in writing. For further details about the policy cover, and terms and conditions, please refer to the Registered Policy Document. If you have opted out of one of the occupational pension schemes because you have reached the Lifetime Allowance, please refer to the Excepted Scheme Policy Document. Please note, you will need to raise a ticket via People Hub, with a copy of the HMRC certificate to confirm Lifetime Allowance protection before this cover can be provided. Group Life cover is provided by the University on a discretionary basis. The provision of the cover will be reviewed on an annual basis. The University is committed to equal pay for work of equal value for all employees and ensures compliance with the Equal Pay and the Equality Act 2010. Trained role analysts within HR, evaluate the level of responsibility and competence required to do each job using the HERA job evaluation scheme. For more information, please refer to the Grading of roles* pages. PhD/Professional Doctorate support for Academic Staff Please visit the Tuition Fees page for more details. title: Leave and Absence
headings: Human Resources Leave and absence Annual leave Planning and Booking Annual Leave Rearranging annual leave Sick leave at the same time as annual leave Saving leave until the end of the leave year in September Carrying leave forward Half-day working start/finish times Christmas closure dates Managing sickness absence Entitlement to Sick Pay for Variable Hours Staff Family-friendly entitlements Leave for religious festivals Compassionate leave Sabbaticals Other absence and leave Adverse weather and travel disruption Unauthorised absence Explore
body: For information on Leave and Absence please follow this link. The information below provides information on leave and absence options, with links to relevant policies and guidance. Some may be paid/unpaid/annual leave or combination thereof; and some also may be subject to eligibility/qualifying periods, therefore the full policy should be referred to for actual details. The annual leave year for all staff runs from 1 October to 30 September in any year and staff are expected to take their entitlement within this timeframe. Academic and Research staff receive an annual leave entitlement of 35 days per year (259 hours) per year. Professional Services staff receive an annual leave entitlement of 32 days (236.8 hours) per year. Part-time staff have a pro-rata entitlement to leave based on the number of hours/weeks they work (their fte). Temporary staff on variable hours contracts have an entitlement to 32 days per annum pro rata and this will be calculated in proportion to the number of hours worked. You can check your leave entitlement, balance and leave taken by viewing your Self Service record, which you can access directly via Employee Self-service (ESS). You can also access Self Service from the Staff Essentials page. Joining or leaving the University - leave entitlement Holiday entitlement for part-years on starting (and leaving) is calculated on complete days of service. On leaving employment at the University, any outstanding leave entitlement to date of leaving should be taken. If (in exceptional circumstances and as agreed with the line manager) this is not possible, outstanding leave entitlement will be paid with the final salary. Leave already taken that is in excess of entitlement will be deducted from the final salary. Bank Holiday entitlement All staff benefit from a proportional entitlement to Bank Holiday and Christmas closure days off, which is included within the annual leave entitlement. The entitlement period runs from 1 October to 30 September each year. Staff wellbeing is important and it is essential that managers work with their teams to ensure that annual leave is planned and taken on a regular basis for the entire leave year. Annual leave should be booked via ESS for approval by your line manager. If your holiday plans have changed and you would like to cancel your holiday booking, please ensure that you discuss any alterations to annual leave requests with your manager. Once agreed with your manager, changes to annual leave arrangements should be confirmed online via ESS. If you become unwell while on annual leave and would have been unfit for work, the normal sickness absence policy will apply. You must notify your manager immediately in line with the usual sickness reporting procedure. You may request that the period of illness is recorded as sickness absence rather than annual leave, subject to the reporting requirements set out in the policy. You must then have a return-to-work discussion with your manager following your return. The period of illness will be recorded as sickness absence where the reporting requirements have been met. You will then either continue the remainder of your annual leave period or return to work. For sickness absence of more than 7 consecutive calendar days, a Fit Note is required in line with the Sickness Absence and Ill Health Policy. It is important that leave is taken throughout the year to support health and wellbeing. Managers should work with their teams to plan annual leave with their staff and to ensure that there is sufficient staffing cover throughout the year. This will be a collaborative process and every attempt will be made to accommodate leave requests. We need to be mindful of other team members who will be looking to take their annual leave before the end of the leave year. It may not be possible to accommodate all requests for leave if the number of requests are high and cannot be balanced with the need to maintain sufficient cover. It is possible that it may be necessary for managers to ask staff to take leave on alternative dates. By booking hours with Friday being a shorter working day for many staff, there will be occasions when odd hours of leave are created which cannot immediately be booked if less than half a day. When these accumulate to half a day, they can be booked but there is a chance that at the end of each Scheme year there will be a few odd hours left over. A maximum of 41 hours annual leave outstanding at 30 September is automatically carried forward into the next leave year, and may be taken at any time during that leave year. The automatic carry forward of up to 41 hours of annual leave allowance includes part-time staff and is not pro-rated. Hours in excess of 41 hours outstanding at 30 September will be lost. There may be some unusual and exceptional circumstances (generally long term sickness or maternity, adoption or shared parental leave) which prevent staff from taking their full annual leave entitlement, even taking into account the fact that up to a maximum of 41 hours outstanding annual leave will be automatically carried forward into the next leave year. Where an individual’s absence due to one of these reasons for a continuous period of at least four weeks has been recorded on the HR System, their full remaining leave entitlement will automatically be carried over into the next leave year. It is recognised, however, that individuals may be required to undertake additional work to support our students and colleagues, which for some makes the taking of all leave a challenge. You are urged to work with your line manager to find ways to continue to take annual leave where at all possible, as everyone needs a time to rest and rejuvenate. If a member of staff wishes to take a half-day, the system will deduct half of their working day, regardless of start and end times. For example, if you work a 7 hour day, it will deduct 3.5 hours. The 'work pattern' for each employee will dictate the number of half or full day hours. The majority of the University is closed over the Christmas period. Staff are not expected to take annual leave to cover this time: working days within the period are allocated as a combination of statutory and customary holidays University Christmas Closure dates. The University is committed to the fair and consistent treatment of staff in relation to sickness absence and to supporting individuals during periods of ill health. Our Managing Sickness Absence page provides information you may find useful. Managers are require to complete a return to work form after every period of sickness absence. These discussions should take place in an appropriate confidential setting and provide an opportunity to support the employee’s return to work. Managers must complete and submit the return to work form via MSS following each discussion to ensure documentation and appropriate follow up Guidance on how to complete this form can be found on Moodle. Your manager can refer you to Occupational Health (OH) for advice if your health is affecting your fitness to carry out your role, or the role is adversely affecting your health. For more information, visit our Referral to Occupational Health webpage. It is important that policies for Variable Hours staff reflect fair and consistent Terms and Conditions. Specifically, Variable Hours are entitled to claim Occupational Sick Pay in line with their contract of employment. Full details are available at our page Entitlement to Sick pay for Variable Hours Staff. The University provides a wide range of Family Friendly entitlements including information and guidance regarding Adoption/Maternity/ Paternity Leave and Pay, Parental Leave, the Right to Request Flexible Working and Time-off for Dependents. Short term and extended leave for religious or cultural holidays/festivals will need to be booked as annual leave. No reasonable request for annual leave should be refused by a manager. However, in deciding what is reasonable, a manager will need to look at all the implications on the team/department or service they deliver when authorising leave. See the University's Religion and Belief Policy for further details. Head of Schools/Professional Service are authorised to grant up to 10 days leave in any one year. In exceptional circumstances, leave in excess of 10 days may be granted, please discuss this directly with your Strategic HR Business Partner. Line managers must notify HR of any compassionate leave taken by their staff by raising a ticket through the People Hub. Any academic member of staff, on a permanent contract to undertake research, innovation and teaching, and who has three years’ continuous service with the University of Portsmouth at the time of commencing the sabbatical, is normally eligible to apply for a sabbatical. Periods of maternity, paternity, adoption leave or long-term sickness absence do count towards the three years’ continuous service. Our Sabbatical Leave Policy provides full details. Information and guidance is available regarding other absence and leave including: Time off for public duties The University recognises that adverse weather conditions can prevent employees from reaching their normal place of employment. All employees are expected to make every reasonable effort to attend for work, but without compromising their health and safety. Where employees are unable to attend for work because of adverse weather, they should report this to their manager as early as possible. If member of staff reports that they are unable to get to work because of adverse weather, and the University is open, then the individual should be offered to take the time as either unpaid, annual leave or flexi-time that they will need to work back at a later date, or where appropriate, work from home on agreed areas of work. If the University takes the decision to close because of adverse weather, casual and STF staff who were scheduled to be in work should be paid for the hours they would have been expected to work whilst the University was closed. Timesheets should be completed in the normal way. Staff taking booked annual leave for all or part of the time when the University was closed will not get these days credited back. Unauthorised absence is not acceptable and may lead to disciplinary action including dismissal. title: Travel to work
headings: Human Resources Travel to work Sustainable travel Explore
body: As a University, we aim to reduce congestion in Portsmouth and improve the air quality for everyone who studies, lives and works in our city. A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. Leaving the car at home and making sustainable travel choices can save you time and money. You may also benefit from improved wellbeing and reduce your environmental impact at the same time. Whether you’re looking for information about local cycle routes to work, walking maps or links to public transport you can access it and plan your journey on the My Journey Hampshire website. You can also commute together with others by using easitShare. This scheme covers journeys made by car, bicycle, taxi, motorbikes, train, buses and on foot. title: My Responsibilities
headings: Human Resources My responsibilities Explore
body: The information below explains the expectations the University has about the behaviour and actions of staff members. The University needs to process personal information about you as a staff member in order to carry out necessary administration, this includes your home address, emergency contact details and current bank details It is the responsibility of each member of staff to ensure that your personal information is kept up-to-date. You can do this via Employee Self Service (ESS). When joining the University, new employees will be given a probationary period. This is a formal structured procedure aimed at assessing and reviewing the employee's performance, capability and suitability for the role. The probation process is intended to support you in your new role and to highlight training and development needs or measures required to help you to fulfil your new responsibilities. Declaration of interests The University’s Declaration of Interests Policy states that all staff must make a declaration of interests if, having read the policy, they find that they have an actual or potential conflict of interest between their private interests and their employment by the University. If you need to make a declaration of interest then please complete the online form (please note that you will need to be logged into your University Gmail account in order to access the form). The University provides support and development to ensure that people can achieve their best and play their part in meeting the goals and objectives of their team in a way that is consistent with the University’s vision and values. Your manager should be able to assist you in managing your performance, and provide additional support where required. The Portsmouth Hallmark The Performance and Development Review Process The University expects all members of staff, including others who may be working on behalf of the University, to behave in an acceptable manner - treating others with courtesy, respect and consideration – and conducting themselves professionally when interacting with members of the University community. We are fully committed to creating and sustaining a positive and mutually supportive working environment where staff can work collaboratively and productively together, and where staff are equally valued and respected. The University of Portsmouth is committed to achieving excellence in the creation and communication of knowledge. The information underpinning that knowledge must be accessible when required and protected from theft, loss or damage. However, all information is not of equal value or importance and the steps necessary for protecting University information must be commensurate with risk, and compliant with applicable legislation. The Information Security Policy sets out the core regulations that must be followed by all users of University of Portsmouth information systems. The Employee Alcohol & Drugs Policy covers both the use and misuse of alcohol or drugs that may impair an individual’s performance or affect their behaviour. The term ‘drugs’ has been used in this Policy to include all drugs and substances, legal or illegal, which adversely affect behaviour or performance. Grievances are concerns, problems or complaints raised by a staff member with managers regarding working conditions or relationships with colleagues. It is clearly in the University’s and employee’s interests to resolve problems early before they escalate into major difficulties. You can download the Grievance procedure and FAQs for more information. It is the policy of the University that all enclosed spaces (including University vehicles) and at entrances to buildings will be entirely smoke free. Staff, students, customers, contractors and visitors are asked to respect the rights of others to enjoy a smoke-free environment. The University prohibits electronic cigarettes in the same way as smoking. Click to read the University Smoke Free Policy. The University Equality and Diversity Unit publishes a wide range of policies and reports including the Dignity and Respect Policy, Religion and Belief Policy and Equality and Diversity: Staff rights and responsibilities. Staff Handbooks (These are currently available as Academic and University Tutor) Find out what to do if you are required to use vehicles on University business This guidance has been developed to define and support the evolution of hybrid working practices for staff and workers employed by the University of Portsmouth. The purpose of the hybrid working guidance is to establish the standards, working practices and supported configurations of hybrid working solutions. Hybrid working will be evolving as it is implemented over the 2021/22 academic year and it will be reviewed regularly as we learn together how to best work with our new arrangements. This guidance must be adhered to by all individuals carrying out University business remotely. Hybrid Working Guidance Hybrid Working Flowchart Working with specific client groups When working with specific client groups (such as children or vulnerable adults, or with NHS patients) there are specific processes which must be followed. The University is required to carry out Disclosure and Barring checks with the UK Government’s Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) on individuals in specific circumstances dictated by UK legislation. A Research Passport is the mechanism for non-NHS staff (that is, higher education institution employees who have no contractual arrangement with the NHS) to obtain an Honorary Research Contract or Letter of Access Travelling overseas on University business Without exception, overseas travel must be formally approved in principle by the Head of Department and/or Line Manager. This is by means of an authorised FIN1 (staff) or FIN30 (student). If the trip is being financed by an external organisation and therefore does not require a FIN1/ FIN30, authorisation from the Head of Department and/or Line Manager must still be sought. Please see the University Overseas Travel section for full information and guidance on the procedures for travelling overseas on University business. Dealing with situations The following information will help you to deal with unexpected situations. Fire Safety (inc Evacuation Procedure) Managing health & safety is essential in creating a safe working environment. First Aid (inc Accident Reporting) Reporting Faults or requesting maintenance The term ‘whistleblowing’ is the disclosure of information by an employee or worker (see the definition in section 1 of the Whistleblowing Policy for a full definition of those individuals who come within these categories) which relates to some danger, fraud or other illegal or unethical conduct in the workplace. title: My Pay
headings: Human Resources My Pay Salary scales The Real Living Wage Timesheets, claims and expenses Reward & Recognition Explore
body: Your salary will be paid monthly in arrears on the last working day of each month (apart from December, when it is paid earlier) by credit transfer to your bank account. The University issues electronic payslips to staff which can be viewed on Employee Self Service (ESS). If you need to change the details of the bank account your salary is paid into, you must complete and submit a Change of Bank Account Details form. You can view the University of Portsmouth current Salary Scales listing all spine points and grades. Progression to the top of your grade is by annual increment. The University has made a commitment to the real Living Wage set by the Living Wage Foundation. The current rate for the real Living Wage outside of London is £13.45 per hour. All hourly paid employees including Casual employees, Zero Hours Workers and Academic employees must claim for their worked hours via Paid Time which is accessible through Employee Self Service. You will be paid on a monthly basis on the last working day of the month and all claims should be submitted as soon as the work has taken place. All staff members should be reimbursed in full for all types of properly authorised expenses incurred in the course of the University’s business. The regulations and procedure for claiming expenses are described in the University’s Expenses Manual. Each month, forms and claims must be submitted by the relevant Payroll deadlines*. Recognition Awards - Under the Reset programme, Recognition Awards are currently suspended. title: Managing My Contract
headings: Human Resources Managing my contract Changing my working pattern Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) checks Research Passports Vetting policy Police Apprenticeship consortium, Non Police Personnel Vetting Confirmation of Employment Regrading my role Information for staff about EUSS University Negotiating and Consultative Committe (UNCC) Explore
body: The information below provides useful information and advice on aspects of your contract. All employees have the chance to request a change to their contractual terms and conditions of employment to work more flexibly, by making a Flexible Work Request, which is part of our range of family-friendly entitlements*. This could be a change to your working pattern, or perhaps the number of hours you work. It is important to note that this is the right to request a change only, and not the automatic right to actually change your pattern. A DBS check is required for individuals are working in certain professions, or with children or vulnerable adults. The University’s Human Resources (HR) department is responsible for administering Disclosure and Barring Service checks with the UK Government’s Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS), for staff where they are required for employment purposes, as dictated by UK legislation. The HR department also administers DBS checks for staff who need to apply for a Research Passport. A Research Passport is the mechanism for non-NHS staff (i.e Higher Education institution employees who have no contractual arrangement with the NHS) to obtain an Honorary Research Contract or Letter of Access when they propose to carry out research in the NHS. The Vetting policy Police Apprenticeship consortium, Non Police Personnel Vetting (NPPV) is applicable to all university employees who have access to Police apprenticeships data and/or are likely to visit police premises during their employment. The appropriate vetting is identified as Non Police Personnel Level 2 (NPPV). If you require a letter to confirm your employment at the University, please raise a ticket via the PeopleHub, giving details of the specific information you need to be included in the letter (such as length of employment, type of contract, salary, etc.) and whether you wish to collect the letter from HR on Floor 6, Mercantile House, or for it to be sent to your work address via internal mail. We are unable to respond to requests sent from accounts other than your University email account. The National Framework Agreement, which has been in place at the University since 2005, recognises that every member of staff may request a review of their job/role. Best practice would suggest that you meet with your line manager in the first instance, to discuss what tasks and responsibilities you are required to undertake and which of these you believe require higher levels of competence and/or responsibility. If your manager doesn’t agree with your view, you can still request that your role is evaluated. You should follow the principles of the regrading an existing role*, and once you have your paperwork ready, send this to HRGradings@port.ac.uk for evaluation. You will not be able to use the automated Job Evaluation Request Form. You may be contacted by a trained role analyst to gather more information if required. Your line manager will also be contacted as they will need to confirm that you are undertaking the work that you make reference to in your regrading request. If you are an EU/EEA national you can find information and guidance on the EU Settlement Scheme here, including how to evidence your EUSS status. "The University Negotiating and Consultative Committee (UNCC) is the forum for all consultation and negotiations at the University and comprises representatives from both management and trades unions." The trades unions recognised by the University of Portsmouth are UNISON representing support staff and UCU representing academic and research members of staff. title: Developing My Career
headings: Human Resources Developing my career My Career and development goals The Personal Development Review (PDR) Explore
body: The University of Portsmouth is committed to providing a programme of good quality leadership, management and staff development to ensure that you understand your role in delivering University ambitions, and have the skills you need to be fully effective within your role. Staff are encouraged to develop skills and self-awareness at work to contribute to their career development, find out more about the Career Development Framework. The University of Portsmouth Staff Mentoring Scheme Mentoring is a supportive opportunity to aid staff in identifying and achieving career goals. It supports the development of a culture of equality and diversity and contributes to our Athena SWAN and HR Excellence in Research award commitment. Find out more about the University Staff Mentoring Scheme. Development opportunities The University offers various development opportunities for staff and support for leaders and managers. Learning and Development offer a two-day event entitled Developing your Supervisory Skills. This is aimed primarily at those aspiring or new to the role of first line supervisor. The event is participative and designed to develop practical supervisory management skills and confidence in the role. A full range of Learning & Development workshops are available, just click on the links in the calendar of events to find out more. We all benefit from taking time out to think about how our work is going and what we are trying to achieve. The Personal Development Review (PDR) process offers a chance to discuss your own thoughts and hear another perspective on your contribution to your department and the University. Professional, honest conversations enable us to celebrate successes and discuss problems. Feedback provides us with information about what we are valued for and what we could improve on.
