title: Ensuring My Health, Safety and Wellbeing
headings: Human Resources Ensuring my health, safety and wellbeing Explore
body: The University of Portsmouth is committed to ensuring the health, safety and wellbeing of its employees. Covid-19 Pandemic - Working from home safely Given the current Covid-19 Coronavirus Pandemic, it has been necessary to develop Remote Working Resources to help staff and managers to support staff as we move to home working. This guidance covers working safely from home and remote working via laptop, desktop PC or paper-based. The University’s Occupational Health Service provides advice to individuals and managers on work related health problems and health problems that can affect work. Our Occupational Health pages also contain information and advice on Mental Health. The University considers that a strong health, safety and wellbeing culture together with a safe and secure environment are fundamental to its success. The Health & Safety office provides information on all aspects of health and safety within the University, which includes accident prevention and reporting, first-aid provision, workplace inspections and the Health & Safety training events calendar. To find out more, visit the Health and Safety Policy webpage where you can download the Health & Safety Policy Statement and the Health & Safety Policy Organisation & Arrangements. Travelling overseas on University business Without exception, overseas travel must be formally approved in principle by the Head of Department and/or Line Manager. This is by means of authorised FIN1 (staff) or FIN30 (student). If the trip is being financed by an external organisation and therefore does not require a FIN1/ FIN30, authorisation from the Head of Department and/or Line Manager must still be sought. Please see the University Overseas Travel section for full information and guidance on the procedures for travelling overseas on University business. The Wellbeing section of the Staff Essentials pages also contains information and advice on Mental Wellbeing, as well as Family, Physical Activity and Diet and Nutrition. Employee Assistance Programme The University provides an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) which offers a free, confidential Helpline (0800 023 9324) which provides access to information, advice and support, and onward referral to telephone or face-to-face counselling where appropriate. The service is designed to help individuals deal with a range of problems, no matter how big or small, which may affect their personal lives or performance at work. With students arriving at the University of Portsmouth from over 120 countries each year, and the University's commitment to widening participation in higher education attracting an ever-increasing variety of students - and indeed staff - understanding and accommodating their needs is essential to its continued success. The Equality and Diversity team undertake a wide variety of work to achieve this. You can find out more on the Equality & Diversity web pages, which also includes the Equality & Diversity Policy Statement. The Dignity and Respect Policy (formerly the Anti-Bullying and Harassment Policy) sets out the University’s commitment to delivering a working and learning environment of dignity and respect, free from harassment and bullying, ensuring we adhere to the Equality Act 2010. The Policy sets out the responsibilities of everyone in ensuring all feel valued and respected and are able to work and study in a discrimination-free environment that promotes equality of opportunity and where difference is valued and respected by all. As part of the University's commitment to equal opportunities, the Equality and Diversity Unit has created a number of Staff Networks. These exist to allow staff from similar backgrounds to meet, network and discuss the issues that matter to them. They also allow for the exchange of views, mutual support and advice as well as giving a platform from which to express concerns, raise awareness and instigate positive change within the University. Support for staff with a disability The University has a Disabled Staff Network , for consultation with disabled staff and for their mutual support. The Network exists for all staff 'with a long term illness, disability or infirmity that affects their everyday life or the work that they do'. The University of Portsmouth offers a range of support services for individuals with disabilities and their managers, you can find more details on the Support for Staff with a Disability webpage. Harassment Adviser Network The University provides a Harassment Adviser Network, which is available to help all students and staff who feel they may have been bullied or have themselves been accused of bullying. Employee Alcohol and Drug Policy The Employee Alcohol and Drug Policy sets out the approach the University will take when the use of alcohol or drugs adversely affects the performance or behaviour of employees. The University is committed to offering information and guidance to help individuals find professional help and to ensuring that they are treated sensitively. The University also has a responsibility, however, to ensure that the health, safety and welfare of students, staff or members of the public are not jeopardised through staff use of alcohol or drugs. It also has a responsibility to safeguard University property. title: Dealing With Change
headings: Human Resources Dealing with change Explore
body: Change is something that everyone experiences. By understanding how you might be affected, and by preparing appropriately, you can adapt to and benefit from these changes successfully. How Does This Change Make Me Feel? Our Occupational Health Department can advise and support where an individual’s health and well-being may be affected by change. The Employee Assistance Programme can offer you support, information and advice on a range of change issues, you can also call and talk in confidence to one of their telephone Helpline advisors. The Organisational Change, Redeployment and Redundancy Policy (OCRR) sets out the University’s approach to dealing with organisational change that may lead to a reduction in employee numbers. Change management and Delivering successful change The Change Management section of the online Learning & Development Toolkit is packed with professional development materials designed to assist you in dealing with the day-to-day challenges of your job. University Negotiating and Consultative Committee (UNCC) "The University Negotiating and Consultative Committee (UNCC) is the forum for all consultation and negotiations at the University and comprises representatives from both management and trades unions." The trades unions recognised by the University of Portsmouth are UNISON representing support staff and UCU representing academic and research members of staff. title: HR Business Partnering Team
headings: Human Resources HR Business Partnering Team Explore
body: The HR Business Partnering (HRBP) Team provides professional and proactive advice and guidance on HR policies and procedures. We support full case management and consultations on employment-related matters ensuring consistency of employment practice across the University. The HRBP Team are dedicated Human Resources professionals providing strategic support for University leadership. Partnered with the University Executive and their extended teams, we offer guidance to enable the delivery of key people initiatives, which in turn maximises organisational performance and we are focussed on the delivery of strategic HR, supporting the embedding and implementation of the People Strategy, ensuring HR resource and practice is aligned to the University’s strategic and operational requirements. The HRBP Team comprises Senior HR Business Partners, Strategic HR Business Partners, Employee Relations Advisers, Employee Relations Officer and a HR Coordinator. Contact details and a full summary of representatives for each Faculty / Professional Service can be located on our Contact Us page. title: Learning and Development
headings: Human Resources Learning and Development Explore
body: Here at the University of Portsmouth, we are committed to providing a programme of good quality leadership, management and staff development to ensure that staff understand their role in delivering University ambitions, and have the skills they need to be fully effective. The following Learning and Development pages have been created to support you through your Induction period, inform you of your Core Training, and signpost to our Learning Events, Programmes and Resources to support your Career development, including your Personal Development Review (PDR). body: Here at the University of Portsmouth we are committed to providing financial, personal and social benefits that make our people feel valued, supported and empowered. If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk You now have access to a huge range of discounts across the UK’s major retailers including supermarkets, technology, fashion, travel and much more! If eligible, you will be registered on the platform within a month of joining the University and will receive an email from email@employeebenefits.vivup.co.uk asking you to accept the registration. To find out more about the platform please follow this link or if already registered, access the savings at https://port.ac.uk/mybenefits. Please click on the links below to find more information on the benefits and rewards open to you as a member of staff. Do you have any ideas of what would be a great new discount or know someone who would be willing to offer staff a discount, then please complete this form. Find out more about upcoming Reward and Benefit Events and limited time offers. Do you know what you want to find or just want to see a list of what rewards and benefits are available to you? Click here. For those employees in the MyPensionPlus pension scheme and those eligible employees who have opted out of either the LGPS, NHS or Teachers' pension scheme, the University has put in place death in service cover. Follow this link for more details. The University is committed to equal pay for work of equal value for all employees and ensures compliance with the Equal Pay and the Equality Act 2010. For more details, click here. For details of discounts on PCs and other computer equipment and software available to employees please click here. title: Payroll
headings: Human Resources New Employee Change Bank Account Details Claims Submission External Worker Pay Claims Submission Paid Time Hours Authorisation HR/Payroll deadline dates - 2026 External Worker Claims Authorisation Explore
body: All new employees must provide their bank details and tax status information via their Onboarding checklist. Until the checklist is fully completed, we will be unable to pay you. If you want to update your bank account details that your salary is paid in, you will be able to do this within Employee Self-Service. For support on how to complete a bank change visit the HR Systems, Employee Self-Service site. If you have been appointed on either a Casual Employee or Zero Hours contract, you will be required to claim your hours via Paid Time, accessible through Employee Self Service. Training on Paid Time, including user guides, will be provided to you by your line manager. The below SAL forms are to be used by the External Worker to claim their fee and, where allowed, any incurred expenses. Please ensure you select the relevant claim for the engagement being undertaken. SAL5 - External Visiting Lecturer claim SAL6 - External Examiner claim SAL8 - External Assessor claim If changes are required to your Paid Time authorisation group, for instance, staff to be removed or added, send details of your change to Payroll by submitting a ticket via the HR People Hub. Faculty changes: Submitted from the Faculty Manager Professional Services changes: Submitted from either the Deputy Director or Director HR / Payroll Deadlines 2026 This form is to be used if you wish for a member of staff to be added as a signatory for the purpose of authorising external worker claims. title: Equality and Diversity
headings: Human Resources Equality and diversity Explore
body: The University is dedicated to ensuring equality of opportunity and the promotion of diversity and inclusion for the benefit of our staff and students. We welcome students to Portsmouth from over 120 countries each year, and we are committed to widening participation in higher education across an ever-increasing variety of students - and indeed staff. The Equality and Diversity Unit owns the policies, guidance and University Equality Objectives that outline the way in which we understand and accommodate the needs of students and staff. We also produce annual comprehensive data reports detailing the University's performance on key areas and their impacts on the staff and student experience. Read more about the Equality and Diversity unit. title: Staff Surveys
headings: Human Resources Staff Surveys Inclusive Culture Survey 2022 Staff Survey 2021 2017 Staff Survey Actions Update 1. Engagement and Building Trust 2. Delegation and Empowerment 3. Delivery against the University’s People Strategy Explore
body: The University is committed to seeking the views of staff on the quality of their working life, and suggestions by staff on the ways in which the University can change and improve to ensure the continued and successful delivery of the University’s Strategy. One of the ways this is achieved is through the staff survey. Staff surveys help senior leadership understand how staff feel about working at the University. Staff surveys also provide an opportunity for staff to gather ideas and work together to improve staff development opportunities, our University environment and, ultimately, the services we provide to students, the community and our partners. People Insight Ltd were commissioned to undertake the University’s 2022 inclusive culture survey as an external independent provider. The University chose to partner with People Insight to ensure that staff had the opportunity to share their honest opinions about their experiences of working at the University through a confidential survey process. The survey was conducted between 31 January and 18 February 2022 and achieved an overall response rate of 52%. 3,301 staff members were invited to take part in the survey and 1,725 responded. This was up from a response rate of 31% for our previous gender culture survey (when 986 staff responded). This report provides a top level summary of the survey responses. Included are comparisons to the like for like questions (where available) from 2018 when the last gender culture survey was conducted. Further comparisons are provided through People Insight’s Higher Education Institutions (HEI) sector benchmark, consisting of responses from other HEIs that People Insight partner with on staff surveys over the past three years, including surveys held before the Covid pandemic. These include a range of institutions, including pre-1992 and post-1992 universities. This report focuses on survey results. Next steps will be discussed and identified by UEB and the EDI Steering Group, Faculties, Schools and Departments as part of the analyses and action planning over the coming months. Inclusive Culture Survey Report People Insight Ltd were also commissioned to undertake the University’s staff pulse survey 2021 as an external independent provider using a similar methodology as the inclusive culture survey in 2022. The pulse survey was conducted between 14 June and 4 July 2021 and achieved an overall response rate of 69%. 3280 staff members were invited to take part in the survey and 2250 responded. Further details can be found in the report below. Staff Survey 2021 Report A number of local actions were taken following the 2017 Staff Survey, as outlined in this Summary of Faculty / Departmental Action Plans. The University Executive Board (UEB) focused on the three following areas to have the greatest positive impact to address key issues from the 2017 Staff Survey. Staff said: Be open and transparent in explaining decision making, and involve staff in discussions about the future of the University. More than 800 staff helped us identify our strategic goals for 2025 by taking part in 20 workshops, town hall events and focus groups in 2019, and through online feedback. The strong view from staff that the University should be more ambitious and bold was the catalyst to develop our Vision for 2030 – to be the UK’s top modern university and one of the top 100 young universities in the world. More than 800 staff helped us identify our strategic goals for 2025 by taking part in 20 workshops, town hall events and focus groups in 2019, and through online feedback. The strong view from staff that the University should be more ambitious and bold was the catalyst to develop our Vision for 2030 – to be the UK’s top modern university and one of the top 100 young universities in the world. Several in-person Vice-Chancellor Question & Answer sessions were organised in 2018 and 2019, which have since become more frequent in 2020 with hundreds of staff attending monthly online Q&A sessions. Several in-person Vice-Chancellor Question & Answer sessions were organised in 2018 and 2019, which have since become more frequent in 2020 with hundreds of staff attending monthly online Q&A sessions. Strategic updates and explanations about UEB decision making are regularly communicated in all-staff emails and the monthly VC Bulletin, with UEB minutes regularly published in the weekly staff newsletter. Strategic updates and explanations about UEB decision making are regularly communicated in all-staff emails and the monthly VC Bulletin, with UEB minutes regularly published in the weekly staff newsletter. Staff said: Trust colleagues to take responsibility for decision making. A Delegation and Empowerment Review, chaired by Executive Director of Finance Emma Woollard, looked at efficiencies in research and innovation, procurement and contract management, HR and student/academic administration. A Delegation and Empowerment Review, chaired by Executive Director of Finance Emma Woollard, looked at efficiencies in research and innovation, procurement and contract management, HR and student/academic administration. As part of this, approving bids for research and innovation are now increasingly done locally within the agreed parameters of the Research and Innovation Matrix. Extra resources from the Finance Department have been provided to Faculties to offset any increase in workload whilst reducing duplication. This has been slower than anticipated due to the extended working from home but is progressing. As part of this, approving bids for research and innovation are now increasingly done locally within the agreed parameters of the Research and Innovation Matrix. Extra resources from the Finance Department have been provided to Faculties to offset any increase in workload whilst reducing duplication. This has been slower than anticipated due to the extended working from home but is progressing. A new approved Procurement Policy now provides increased delegation to budget holders with orders up to £25,000 entirely authorised by the faculty or professional service concerned, allowing increased ownership of expenditure by budget holders. A new approved Procurement Policy now provides increased delegation to budget holders with orders up to £25,000 entirely authorised by the faculty or professional service concerned, allowing increased ownership of expenditure by budget holders. Finance staff in Professional Services and Faculties can now order “high volume, low value” goods such as office supplies via a supplier portal without the need to input orders and goods receipts into the main finance system, reducing the tasks required on these lower risk purchases. Finance staff in Professional Services and Faculties can now order “high volume, low value” goods such as office supplies via a supplier portal without the need to input orders and goods receipts into the main finance system, reducing the tasks required on these lower risk purchases. Some changes in approach had to be introduced at the start of lockdown, notably a pause on hiring staff, to enable careful financial control in an unprecedented situation, given early intelligence was student numbers would be significantly below previous expectations. As more information slowly becomes available about our student numbers, controls are being lifted and additional funding allocated to meet priority needs, now our position is more stable. Some changes in approach had to be introduced at the start of lockdown, notably a pause on hiring staff, to enable careful financial control in an unprecedented situation, given early intelligence was student numbers would be significantly below previous expectations. As more information slowly becomes available about our student numbers, controls are being lifted and additional funding allocated to meet priority needs, now our position is more stable. Staff said: Provide greater consistency and transparency about managing performance, and for managers to feel empowered and have the right skill set to effectively manage staff. The University’s People Strategy is now included in our new University Strategy which was launched in January 2020, which states that our overarching ambition is to deliver our Vision for 2030 by inspiring our staff community to be creative and bold. The new online Performance and Development Review (PDR) system was launched in April 2019, to help staff and managers have meaningful ongoing conversations on objectives, contribution and achievements, and to plan career and development goals. The new online Performance and Development Review (PDR) system was launched in April 2019, to help staff and managers have meaningful ongoing conversations on objectives, contribution and achievements, and to plan career and development goals. The Human Resources Department has fully reviewed 9 policies, with 4 of them completed and ready for implementation are Probation, Grievance, End of Fixed Term Contract and Disciplinary. The remaining 5 policies including Dignity and Respect and Anti-Bullying and Harassment are in the process of progressing towards full agreement. There will be training sessions for managers in understanding and implementing the new policies during spring/summer 2021. There will be training sessions for managers in understanding and implementing the new policies during spring/summer 2021. The Report and Support Tool was launched in May 2020 for staff to anonymously report or get support from an advisor to report incidents of physical assault, sexual harassment, sexual assault, online harassment, hate crimes, stalking, bullying, or discrimination. title:
Staff Essentials -
			Staff Essentials
headings: Staff Essentials Quick links Latest news Pavement closure outside Wiltshire Building Ruby’s story: Moving against cancer Computer model predicts England as favourites for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Campus notices First Bus app issues affecting the University bus
body: Please login to the VPN to follow this link Please login to the VPN to follow this link Academic and Digital Innovation Academic Workload Planning Course and module catalogue Employee Self Service HR People Hub (Hornbill) Modern Work Programme MyPort student website Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Research and Innovation Report & Support Tool Staff Rewards and Benefits Staff Wellbeing and Support The pavement in front of Wiltshire Building will be closed from w/c 6 April for a minimum period of 15 weeks. This is due to the latest remedial works being carried out on the building. Ruby Stevenson, who works as a Disability Officer in Student Services, shares her story of being diagnosed with stage 3 cancer, how it has affected her and how she hopes her story will show what’s possible while she fundraises to help others who might one day face their own turning point. A mathematical model used to simulate sports match outcomes has identified England as a serious contender for the FIFA World Cup. We’re currently aware that University bus tickets are not listed on the First Bus app following an update. Tickets already activated should still appear within your 'tickets' on the app. Colleagues purchasing staff day tickets should purchase a ticket on board, or avoid updating their app. First Bus are investigating this issue, and updated instructions will be available shortly.

title: Other absence and leave
headings: Human Resources Other absence and leave Jury Service Witness Service Reservist Duties Time Off for Public Duties Explore
body: In this section, you will find information about jury and witness service, reservist duties (service in non-regular forces) and time off for public duties. Employees may be required at any time to undertake a period of jury service. The court will pay the juror an allowance for financial loss and expenses. The University does not make any deductions from pay in respect of absence on account of jury service. The court will issue a form to a juror which should be forwarded to the Payroll Office. A financial loss claim is not necessary. Please raise a ticket through the People Hub so this leave can be added to iTrent. A procedure similar to that of Jury Service operates for employees undertaking witness service. The Criminal Prosecution Service will issue a form to a witness to claim compensation for expenses. A financial loss claim is not necessary. Please raise a ticket through the People Hub so this leave can be added to iTrent. Staff who are volunteer members of the non-Regular Forces, eg Territorial Army, and who attend summer camp are eligible for 2 weeks additional paid leave irrespective of their normal leave. This leave will be recorded on the HR System by HR Department staff. Occasionally, staff are called up for active duty and in these circumstances, you should contact your line manager and your HR Business Partner/HR Adviser immediately. Employees are allowed ‘reasonable’ time off during working hours for the performance of the following duties: a Justice of the Peace a member of a Local Authority a member of a Statutory Tribunal a member of a Health Authority a governor of an educational establishment maintained by a Local Authority ‘Reasonable’ time off is defined as up to 18 days paid leave per annum for staff undertaking any of the duties described. If further leave is required, please contact your HR Business Partner/HR Adviser. This will be recorded on the HR System by HR Department staff. title: Staff Essentials - Association of University Administrators
headings: Staff Essentials Association of University Administrators - University of Portsmouth Branch
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. The AUA is the professional body for managers and administrators working in HE in the UK and Ireland. It was formed from a merger of CUA and APA (Conference of University Administrators and Association of Polytechnic Administrators) when the Polytechnics acquired university status, so has a history dating back to the 1960s and 70s. Its aim is to promote excellence in HE management and administration and it is committed to raising the profile of management and administration in HE as a profession, developing best practice and high standards in the area. Please visit the University of Portsmouth AUA pages at www.port.ac.uk/aua.
links: University of Portsmouth Home Staff Services Departments Resources www.port.ac.uk/aua © University of Portsmouth Cookie policy Modern Slavery Statement Privacy policy Website terms and conditions

title: University Bus
headings: Estates and Campus Services University Bus Service First Bus app issues affecting the University bus FAQs on staff charges Explore
body: The University runs two bus services to campus from Monday to Friday during term-time. The U1 takes a circular route around Portsmouth, and the U2 goes to the Langstone sports site. The bus is primarily for student use, however staff can use it for university activities when there is capacity for a charge. In order to use the bus, you’ll need to download the First Bus app via the Play Store or Apple store to purchase digital tickets. Staff will need to either buy a day ticket priced at £3 or a calendar month-long ticket for £45. Alternatively, a day ticket can be bought for £3 when boarding the bus. The U1 circular route runs with stops at Bransbury Park in Eastney, Prince Albert Road, Francis Avenue, Fratton Bridge, Somers Road, Winston Churchill Avenue, Cambridge Road, Elm Grove, Kings Theatre, Festing Road and Eastney Health Centre. Buses run every 15 minutes during weekdays in term time. The U1 bus doesn't run at weekends, during the academic holidays or on public holidays. For more information and for up to date timetables view our University Buses page on MyPort. The U2 bus stops at Spinnaker Building, University Learning Centre, Fratton Bridge, Prince Albert Road and Langstone Campus. The service also stops at all First bus stops along the route (only times for the main stops listed above are shown in the U2 timetable). The U2 bus doesn't run on weekends, during the academic holidays or on public holidays. Staff will be charged £3 per day or £45 for a monthly ticket Scan your digital ticket as you board the bus and bring your University ID card Seats are available on a first come first served basis Seating capacity of the buses cannot be exceeded Alcohol is not permitted on the buses Not operational on public holidays (Bank holidays) or outside of term time. Updates on the service are available on the MyPort student website. We’re currently aware that University bus tickets are not listed on the First Bus app following an update. Tickets already activated should still appear within your 'tickets' on the app. Colleagues purchasing staff day tickets should purchase a ticket on board, or avoid updating their app. First Bus are investigating this issue, and updated instructions will be available shortly. How much will tickets cost? £3 for a day ticket (buy through app or on the bus) £45 for a month long pass (via the app) Free for students (after paying a 1p refundable admin fee) £3 for a day ticket (buy through app or on the bus) £45 for a month long pass (via the app) Free for students (after paying a 1p refundable admin fee) How do I get my bus ticket? Download the First Bus app. Select 'tickets' and then 'buy'. Scroll to find 'Portsmouth University' and select either the £45 monthly or the £3 day ticket. I'm having issues with the appVisit the First Bus website for further guidance. What if I’m a staff member and also a student?If you are primarily employed as a member of staff (even if you are also studying), you will need to purchase staff tickets. If you are primarily a student, including those on placement or with part-time work at the University, you are eligible to use student tickets. What support is available for staff with accessibility concerns?You can apply for a disabled person's bus pass or an older person’s bus pass from the Portsmouth City Council website. If you are in need of additional support or guidance please get in touch with estates.helpdesk@port.ac.uk. What if I travel with my child(ren)?There is currently no dedicated policy for staff travelling with children on the university bus. Standard First Bus child fares apply. How will staff and students be distinguished?Staff and students can be identified by their University ID cards, which may be requested alongside the app ticket. Your university email address will also help verify your status when purchasing tickets. What if someone doesn’t have a smartphone?Staff or students without smartphones should contact estates.helpdesk@port.ac.uk as early as possible to discuss options. I struggle with apps or organising tickets in advance – what can I do?If you're unsure of how to use the app, visit the First Bus website for further guidance. Where can I give feedback or ask questions?Please email estates.helpdesk@port.ac.uk if you have questions, concerns, or suggestions. title: Staff Essentials - Harassment Adviser Network
headings: Staff Essentials Harassment, bullying and intimidation University Harassment Adviser Network
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. View the Anti Harassment and Anti Bullying Policy The University is committed to tackling all forms of bullying and harassment and other unacceptable behaviours and ensuring that we treat each other with dignity and respect. The Policy gives a clear direction to all and will help us to continue to value all members of our community by providing a fair, supportive and discrimination-free environment that promotes equality of opportunity for staff, students and others and values diversity. A network of harassment advisers is available to help all staff who feel they may have been bullied or have themselves been accused of bullying. The advisers will inform you of your options and offer support, they will never tell you what to do. That decision is always yours. All discussions with a harassment adviser are confidential, and brief records of their discussions will be recorded only for statistical purposes. This information is anonymous and will be held in complete confidence. If you would like a confidential discussion please contact any of the advisers below. aaron.sayers@port.ac.uk adam.amara@port.ac.uk Dennis Sciama Building alexandra.sonu@port.ac.uk ali.drewett@port.ac.uk amanda.ward@port.ac.uk beth.clarkson@port.ac.uk Dariuz (Darek) Gorecki darek.gorecki@port.ac.uk St Michael's Building helen.ford@port.ac.uk St Michael's Building helen.hill@port.ac.uk katja.kujabi@port.ac.uk Rosalind Franklin Halls kerry.collier@port.ac.uk kieren.mcewan@port.ac.uk linda.spence@port.ac.uk patrick.meredith@port.ac.uk russ.bissett@port.ac.uk Sandra (Sandy) Walker sandra.walker@port.ac.uk stephanie.slack@port.ac.uk sukhi.hazra@port.ac.uk tony.crabtree@port.ac.uk

title: Serious incident - University of Portsmouth
headings: Serious incident
body: University of Portsmouth: Application, Fees & Funding The University has a Major Incident Management Team that will enact the Major Incident Action Plan should an incident occur. Should a serious incident occur there will be information updates, about the frequency and any actions needed, on the University of Portsmouth website. In case of emergency on campus, dial ext. 3333 for University Security or, off campus call 023 9284 3333.

title: Staff Essentials - Staff development
headings: Staff Essentials Staff development provision across the University is delivered by a range of Providers: Online Learning and Development platforms:
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. The University values its employees and their expertise and talent, and understands the impact they have in achieving its Strategy and contributing to its continued success. As such, the University recognises the importance of providing appropriate learning and development opportunities to ensure all employees are committed, motivated, competent and developed in their jobs, with staff working in partnership at all levels. Learning and Development Centre for Academic and Digital Innovation (CADI) Research and Innovation (RIS) Docebo - the University’s Learning Management System (LMS). Designed to offer an engaging user experience, Docebo enables colleagues to access eLearning, register for events, and explore a wide range of learning and development resources. Visit Docebo to complete all Core and Role-specific training. LinkedIn Learning - Equips you with the tools to excel in your current role, explore new career paths, and stay ahead of industry trends.

title: Staff Essentials - Professional Support Staff Association
headings: Staff Essentials Professional Support Staff Association
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. The Professional Support Staff Association (PSSA) is the body that represents all University support staff and in doing so provides them with a voice across the University by representing their interests on a variety of sub-committees and groups. It also provides a means whereby support staff can express their views and opinions to the Board of Governors, Academic Council and the Vice-Chancellor. The PSSA also aims to help promote and foster a spirit of cooperation between its members. There are more than 1,300 professional support staff working across the University in very diverse roles. As a member of professional support staff you are automatically a member of the PSSA and have the power to vote at general meetings. We are always interested in hearing your views and opinions on any subject related to your working life. We are also very keen to hear from any member interested in getting involved in the work of the PSSA. If you would like to contact us please email pssa@port.ac.uk. You can find out more about the PSSA by visiting our site: PSSA Google Site (internal access only) The University's Report and Support should be used to report incidents of socially unacceptable behaviours (this is available to both students and staff). Once you have made contact with this service, support will be provided by either an HR Adviser in the Employment Practice Team or from an Harassment Adviser. Details of the Harassment Adviser Network can be found here. Further information on relevant University policies can be accessed using the following links: Dignity & Respect and Anti-Harassment & Anti-Bullying. Contact with Report and Support will be treated in the strictest of confidence.

title: Working at Portsmouth
headings: Working at the University of Portsmouth Working with us is rewarding in more ways than one Current vacancies Microsoft 365 digital transformation Visiting scholars and researchers Staff benefits How to apply for a role Our People Strategy Hallmarks of a Portsmouth employee Starting work Commuting to Portsmouth Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Arriving from overseas Information for EU/EEA staff applying to Portsmouth Technician Commitment Our memberships and the organisations we work with We use cookies
body: We recruit the best people to deliver the best experience for our students We are a new breed of university in a buzzing, diverse city with plenty of stories to tell, and it's not just our students who enjoy the benefits. We've been awarded an Athena SWAN silver award for our commitment to gender equality, and we've got plenty of other awards and rankings successes to shout about too – but first and foremost, we're here to create bigger and better opportunities for all. We're building a brighter future and we're looking for people to help us get there. If that sounds like something you'd love to do, find out how to apply for a role with us today. Explore our latest external and internal job vacancies here at the University of Portsmouth, and apply for a new role at a top UK university. We're looking for talented people to help us become a digitally enabled and empowered university through M365, within a variety of permanent and fixed-term roles. We're a truly global university and we welcome visiting academics, scholars and researchers from institutions around the world. Discover the financial, personal and social benefits available when you start a new role at the University of Portsmouth – including staff discounts, pension schemes, generous holiday allowances and more. The University of Portsmouth encourages sustainable commuting to Portsmouth options for all members of staff. Find out how we're ensuring equality of opportunity for all by creating a working, learning, cultural and social environment based on dignity and respect. If you're coming to work at the University from overseas, find all the information you need, including getting your visa, finding accommodation and paying your council tax. Information and support for University staff from the EU/EEA. The University of Portsmouth has joined the Technician Commitment, ensuring visibility, recognition, career development and sustainability for technicians. We may use cookies to analyse visitor data, improve our website, show personalised content and give you a great website experience. For more information about cookies on our site and to tailor the cookies we collect, select ‘Manage settings’.

title: Access to Information
headings: Access to Information
body: This section of the website provides access to important information for University staff and students, as well as the general public. The Document Warehouse is a central store of all University policies, procedures, guidelines and strategies and should be linked to by staff and students from the rest of the website, in order to avoid duplication of documents. The Freedom of Information site has been created in response to the 2000 Act of the same name, which states that the University, as a public authority, must make available a large amount of information to the public. The site contains links to information produced and held by departments throughout the University and is divided into the classes recommended by the Act. University of Portsmouth © 2012

title: Academic Registry
headings: Academic Registry Explore
body: About Us and Who to Contact Welcome to the Academic Registry! The Academic Registry plays a central role in our institution’s commitment to maintaining academic excellence. Our role is to develop and support an academic quality regulatory landscape which supports the highest standards of academic quality, through academic regulations and policies that support a fair, transparent, and effective learning environment. Our Mission is to ensure the University’s academic quality environment complies with mandatory regulatory requirements, upholds academic standards, enhances the student experience, and supports all students, regardless of their background, to succeed. Academic Excellence: Promoting the highest standards of education and quality of student experience Integrity: Ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability in all our actions. Collaboration: Partnering with students, staff and external stakeholders to uphold and improve academic quality. Responsiveness: Adapting to the changing landscape of higher education through innovation and foresight. Working collaboratively across the University, and informed by sector leading-practice we will: Ensure all academic policies, regulations and procedures meet mandatory regulatory requirements whilst being efficient and maintaining a forward-thinking approach. Provide an assessment regulatory framework which supports student achievement, and eliminates unwarranted barriers to learning and success; Create a student and staff-centric academic quality environment that promotes high standards and continuous improvement; Generate and analyse reports on academic performance, student feedback and outcomes to drive informed decision-making for shaping the education portfolio and enhancing quality; Efficiently manage and compassionately resolve student casework relating to academic quality and regulations, providing support and solutions to minimise the impact on all students involved. Offer expert leadership and advice on academic quality and regulations to support the academic and professional services community. title: Financial statements and annual report
headings: Our financial strategy Accessing our statements Corporate governance Organisational structure We use cookies
body: Every academic year, our Board of Governors publishes an audit of our financial statements. This is to make sure our financial management is accurate and robust. This publication is a yearly overview of our financial performance including statements on income and expenditure, the university balance sheet, changes in reserves and cash flow. We have a robust financial strategy that delivers a successful and financially sustainable university. Over recent years, we've invested heavily in our estates and will continue to do so. And continue to deploy our resources efficiently. We will plan our capital investment more effectively to derive maximum strategic benefits. We will also increase our monitoring of our planned and actual investments. We publish our financial statements in PDFs. We want our PDFs to be accessible to everyone but we know that some older PDFs are not accessible to screen reader software. If you need a PDF in a different format, please contact us and we'll get back to you as soon as possible. View our financial statements: Financial Statements 2024-25 Financial Statements 2023-24 Financial Statements 2022-23 Financial Statements 2021-22 Financial Statements 2020-21 Financial Statements 2019-20 Financial Statements 2018-19 Financial Statements 2017-18 Financial Statements 2016-17 Financial Statements 2015-16 Find out more about our corporate governance, what shapes the way we work and learn how our organisation is governed. Learn more about the structure of the University, from the faculties and schools to the teams behind-the-scenes in our professional services departments. We may use cookies to analyse visitor data, improve our website, show personalised content and give you a great website experience. For more information about cookies on our site and to tailor the cookies we collect, select ‘Manage settings’.

body: This Freedom of Information (FOI) publication scheme highlights all information that is publicly available about the University. The information is arranged under 7 headings to help you find what you want quickly: Who we are and what we do What we spend and how we spend it What our priorities are and how we're doing How we make decisions Our policies and procedures The services we offer We have separate publication schemes for our subsidiary companies – University of Portsmouth Enterprise Limited (UPEL), University of Portsmouth Investments Limited (UPIL), University of Portsmouth Services Limited (UPSL), and ASTA Technology Limited (ASTA). If the information you want isn't available in our publication schemes, you can make a Freedom of Information request. This class has information on how the University is set up, including its legal status, location and contact details. It also includes information about organisations we work with. This includes information about the legal and corporate status of the University. The University of Portsmouth Higher Education Corporation is the legal name for the University, but we're more widely known as the University of Portsmouth. The Education Reform Act 1988 and the Further and Higher Education Act 1992 set out how universities must be constituted. The Instruments of Government (PDF) sets out the constitution for the Board of Governors, its officers and their roles The Articles of Government (PDF) sets out the rules for the conduct of the University, the procedures for the Board of Governors and Academic Council, and the appointment of senior officers of the University How we spend our money University archive (historical records covering the University and its preceding institutions) This section includes further information about the management and structure of the University, including descriptions of the boards and committees we're legally required to have. There's also information on the terms of reference and membership of these boards and committees. Board of Governors: Statement of Primary Responsibilities (PDF) – this highlights the responsibilities of the Board of Governors Standing Orders of the Board of Governors and its Committees (PDF) – this supplements the information in the Instruments of Government and Articles of Government Further information about the Board of Governors Academic Council – includes responsibilities of the Academic Council University Executive Board – members and contacts Central service departments list The University runs a number of key, strategic committees, in the first instance, information on these bodies can be obtained from Paul Mould, the Executive Director of Corporate Governance. University of Portsmouth Enterprise Limited (UPEL) University of Portsmouth Investments Limited (UPIL) University of Portsmouth Services Limited (UPSL) ASTA Technology Limited (ASTA) UOP Academic Services Limited (UOPAS) UOPM Sdn Bhd (UOPM – registered in Malaysia) List of approved collaborative and articulation partners The majority of student clubs, associations and non-academic organisations are run by the University of Portsmouth's Students' Union. title: Board of Governors
headings: Board of Governors Responsibilities of our Board of Governors Why do we have a Board of Governors? What does the Board of Governors do? Who is on the Board of Governors? What are the Board of Governors' committees? When do we meet? Board of Governors meeting agendas Board of Governors minutes Board of Governors register of interests We use cookies
body: Find out more about our Board of Governors and their recent meetings This page sets out the role and responsibilities of our Board of Governors. You can also visit our who's who page to find out more about the individuals that sit on our board. If you need more detailed information, please contact Corporate Governance on corporate-governance@port.ac.uk The University of Portsmouth gained its university status in July 1992. With this new status came the requirement that the University must operate in accordance with its Articles of Government and Instrument of Government, as approved by the Privy Council. The Board of Governors has ultimate responsibility for all University activities and for ensuring the efficient management of those activities. The following documents outline how the Board of Governors is governed, their primary responsibilities, and the Standing Orders of the Board and its Committees: Statement of Primary Responsibilities Articles of Government Instrument of Government Standing Orders of the Board of Governors and its Committees The Board of Governors is collectively responsible for determining the learning environment and mission of the University and for the direction of its activities. This includes a responsibility for determining the ongoing strategic plan of the University and for ensuring that the institution is being managed effectively. The Board of Governors discharges its responsibilities in accordance with the Committee of University Chairs (CUC) Code of Governance, which sets out its responsibilities for strategic planning, finance, audit, estate management, charitable status and public business. The CUC Higher Education Code of Governance (2020 Revision) encourages Institutions to include a Code of Conduct within their governing documents. The University of Portsmouth Code of Conduct lists the various documents which make reference, implicitly and explicitly, to the standards of behaviour required of its governors. In taking forward its responsibilities, the Board observes the seven principles of public life: The Board of Governors includes external governors, staff governors, student governors and the Vice-Chancellor. You can find out more about the current membership of the Board of Governors on our who’s who page. The Board of Governors conducts its work through a number of committees which report to the main Board: Audit and Risk Committee Finance and Infrastructure Committee Nominations Committee Senior Postholders Remuneration Committee Vice-Chancellor’s Remuneration Committee Exceptional and Urgent Approvals Group You can find out more about these committees and view the minutes of recent committee meetings on our committees page. The Board of Governors normally meets five times per year: in October, November, January, March, and July. The Governors' committees each meet between four to six times per year. You can find out more about the upcoming Board of Governors and committee meeting dates in the following document: Board of Governors Calendar of Meetings 2025/2026 Board of Governors Calendar of Meetings 2026/2027 Board meeting agendas from the current academic year and the two academic years preceding it can be found here. title: Student Communications Guidance
headings: Recruitment, Marketing and Communications 1. Principles 2. University-wide communications 3. Faculty-level communications School-level communications 4. Course and module-level communications Explore
body: The following student communications guidance for colleagues outlines general principles, channel options and responsible teams/departments for student communications. University-wide communications Faculty-level communications Course and module-level communications As part of our commitment to fostering a student-centric academic environment, the University is adopting Compassionate Communication practices including committing to: a culture of kindness mindful communications timely communications reflection and continuous improvement Our communications to students should also adopt: adopt our brand guidelines including an A-Z Editorial Style e.g. the date format should be 14 January and not 14th January align with the University’s values of being ambitious, responsible and open comply with data protection legislation in the use of student personal data right audience, right time, and right channel When choosing a communication channel, it's important to consider the audience, the message, and the urgency of the information. This is often written in summary as the three Rs - right audience, right time, and right channel. Internal Communications Team in Recruitment, Marketing and Communications for the majority of student communications. Contact: studentnews@port.ac.uk Library and Information Services for systems maintenance/upgrade messages. Weekly Student Newsletter - used for the majority of communications relevant to a large number of students MyPort - main student website under overall ownership by Internal Comms with delegated access and responsibility to teams and departments for their own content All-Student Emails - urgent and high priority communications SMS text - occasional use for high priority or emergency messages, managed by Internal Comms. Budget required (5p per text). Responsible: Nominated staff within faculties, usually the Executive Dean (and/or nominated rep) Channels: SITS Academic View Student emails via SITS Academic View - should be used for important, high priority faculty level messages, for instance in support of student completion of the NSS. Responsible: Nominated staff within faculties, usually the Heads of School (and/or nominated reps) Student emails via Academic View - should be used for important, high priority school level messages, for instance sharing information about school level events. Student emails via SITS Academic View - should be used for communicating things such as cancelled or postponed teaching sessions, late changes to location, any requirements for students to prepare in advance for teaching sessions, module or course level events and most things related to module or course specific activity that needs to be communicated where no other means is available. Moodle Forums - should be used for announcements related to learning materials and taught topics. They should encourage discussion and interaction around the learning context of the course. Anything that is of the utmost importance should not use forums as it is dependent on the user’s engagement with Moodle and their personal forum preferences Internal communications Email signature guidance Student Communications Guidance Email signature guidance Student Communications Guidance Press and Public Relations Institutional statements in response to conflict, crisis or natural disaster Contact and departments

title: Role of Occupational Health
headings: Human Resources Occupational Health Service Role of Occupational Health Explore
body: The Occupational Health Service is available to provide confidential advice and support on any health issues. A Referral from Line Management will be necessary. Issues for which Occupational Health can be contacted include: Health assessments for all new staff, with follow-up advice as appropriate. Ergonomic advice/workstation assessment where there are health problems. Health assessment/medical for any staff driving on University business (only required for minibuses or vans) Advice regarding counselling Health surveillance for designated workers. Support for first aiders Advice for those travelling abroad on University business - via the Occupational Health website or in person if vaccinations are required. Arrangement of early eyesight test if required for health reasons - via referral from line manager. Advice on rehabilitation following sickness absence, regardless of how short term, if an employee is in need of support or advice or management are concerned. We also work with the Health and Safety team to help identify hazards in the working environment, advise on aspects relating to Health and Safety legislation and follow up all work related accidents. Occupational Health should always be contacted: Immediately following an accident at work involving an incident with sharps or contamination with body fluids via the University Sharps Hotline by calling 011 8207 6190 or by emailing help@cordellhealth.co.uk. Where there is a health problem, which may have been caused by or exacerbated by work or that is affecting work. Prior to returning to work following sickness absence of longer than four weeks. If an employee has diarrhoea and vomiting for more that 24 hours (staff involved in food preparation). Where there is a health condition that is likely to be covered by current disability legislation and adjustments to workplace/duties/hours etc may be required. title: Referral
headings: Human Resources Occupational Health Service Referral Process Occupational Health Portal Access to Portal Portal User Guides for Managers Contact Details for Occupational Health Occupational Health Feedback Explore
body: Employees may be asked to attend Occupational Health at the request of their manager or HR. This may be for some of the following reasons: Concern that health is affecting work or work is affecting health. In pregnancy, following Risk Assessment Regarding long-term sickness absence Repeated short-term sickness absence whether or not there is an underlying health condition These referrals are done for the benefit of staff welfare and will entail an assessment either with the Occupational Health Physician, or an Occupational Health Nurse. For further support on the referral process, select an appropriate resource below: Introduction to Management Referrals - For Managers (video guide) Making a Referral to Occupational Health - Manager's Guide - Managers Guide What to Expect from a Referral to Occupational Health - Employee Guide Pre-placement Health Assessments Guide - Employee Guide What to expect from Health Surveillance Guide - Employee Guide To access the Occupational Health Portal, Managers will first need to attend the ‘Training Awareness for the Occupational Health Portal for Management Referrals’. Please note: If you are a manager and have an urgent referral, and have not yet completed the above training, please request portal access by email to: mediwork@cordellhealth.co.uk Please see the following short portal user guides to accessing and using the MediWork Portal: Mediwork Authentication Guide MediWork Managers Guide Mediwork Status and Meaning guide Migrating to MediWork Raising a referral in Mediwork as a line manager (Panopto video (10m), sign in via SSO) Appointment booking: admin@cordellhealth.co.uk or call 0118 207 6190 Release of reports: portsmouthhub@cordellhealth.co.uk Issues with portal (access or set up): mediwork@cordellhealth.co.uk If you would like to provide feedback to the Occupational Health Team please click on the appropriate link. title: Sickness absence
headings: Human Resources Sickness Absence Occupational Health Referral Explore
body: The Managing Sickness Absence and Ill Heath Policy sets out the procedure employees and their managers are required to follow in the event of absence from work due to sickness and the procedure to follow when ill health/disability prevents effective performance of an employee’s duties. It also identifies sources of support and guidance for employees and managers as well as outlining its use with other University procedures. A return to work discussion should be conducted after each occasion of absence, and in an appropriate confidential place. Whilst these meetings may be very informal the discussions should be recorded in note format, shared with both parties and kept locally in the employee’s personal file. Where an employee has been absent for long periods the return to work meeting should be held before the proposed return datewhich will assist in their re-introduction to the workplace. Managers are expected to complete the Return to Work Form after every period of sickness absence an employee has. The Return to Work form is available to Managers in iTrent. Guidance on how to complete this form can be found on Moodle. To refer a member of your team to Occupational Health, you will need to complete a Referral form. title: Risk Assessment for New and Expectant Mothers
headings: Human Resources Risk Assessment for New & Expectant Mothers Explore
body: Guidance for Managers wth relation to pregnant staff is available from HR. Please also ensure a Risk Assessment for New & Expectant Mothers form is completed as soon as you are aware that your staff member is pregnant. There may be physical, biological and chemical agents, working processes and conditions that they could be at risk from and changes may need to be made to their work in order to ensure their health and safety. The form is to be retained by the relevant department and not sent to Occupational Health unless there are any problems which may need our help to resolve. In this case please Refer the individual to us and enclose a copy of the assessment. title: Mental Health
headings: Human Resources How to be mentally healthy at work is a booklet written by Mind, the mental health charity, and may be a useful resource. Wellness Action Plans - information for managers Mindful Employer Actions to take if you are concerned about someone who says they feel suicidal E-Learning package for staff to support students who may have mental health issues Explore
body: If you are struggling with your mental health, you are encouraged to discuss this with your line manager and request a referral to the Occupational Health Service. If you are feeling overwhelmed by your symptoms and need more urgent support please contact the University Employee Assistance Programme on 0800 023 9324 (free from a UK landline) to talk to a trained counsellor, The Samaritans Freephone 116 123 (UK), or Mind. managing someone who is experiencing a mental health problem and want to know more about how you can support them interested in using the WAP with staff who are currently well, as a proactive tool to promote and maintain their mental health at work an HR professional looking to promote this guide to managers, to help them support the mental wellbeing of their teams" The University has chosen to sign up to the Mindful Employer Charter, an initiative which is aimed at increasing awareness of mental health at work and providing support for businesses in recruiting and retaining staff. This demonstrates the University of Portsmouth's commitment to Dignity, Diversity and Equality by ensuring that as an institution it is open and accessible to all, and values its staff and students for what they bring as individuals. Mindful Employer have produced two documents, one for staff and the other for line managers on dealing positively with mental health in the workplace. Keeping Well at Work: A practical guide for staff experiencing stress, anxiety or depression (.pdf) Mindful Employer: Line Managers' Resource (.pdf) Working with Anxiety: Mindful Employer has produced a film, Working with Anxiety which explores how anxiety affects the ability to work and carry out other day to day activities, and highlights key ways to aid recovery. The film considers how it feels to experience generalised anxiety, panic attacks, OCD and PTSD and some practical methods through which to aid and achieve recovery. NHS Campaign - Every mind matters Improving Access to Psychological Therapies (IAPT) NHS support for mental health conditions is available through IAPT. In Portsmouth, this service is called Talking Change and in the rest of Hampshire, it is called iTalk. Similar services are available throughout the rest of the UK. Please access these through your local IAPT webpages. Referral is either directly to the organisation or your GP can give you further information. Support is available for counselling, health anxiety support groups, social phobia, obsessive compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorders etc. Advice and information on stress is available in the University Learning and Development Toolkit https://app.goodpractice.net/#/uni-portsmouth-tfl/login The University's Employee Assistance Programme provides advice, support and counselling for UoP staff. Further information can be found at the link above. 1. If a member of staff makes you aware that they are having suicidal intentions or thoughts, please refer immediately to Occupational Health. 2. If you believe that the person means to harm themselves imminently and the person has the means to do this i.e. pills, a sharp implement, threatening to jump off a tall building or similar, the police need to be contacted as they are the only ones who can physically remove the person to a place of safety. 3. Encourage the person to seek help and to talk to someone they trust such as a good friend, their GP, a counsellor or a family member. 4. If the person is willing to talk to you, listen closely but try not to offer any advice. Do not offer your opinion and try not to interrupt. Having your support at this critical time in their life can sometimes be enough to help them. Other useful numbers and websites: The Samaritans (local branch) Freephone: 116 123 or Local rate charge: 02392 69 13 13 296 London Road, North End, Portsmouth, PO2 9JN NHS Direct - Telephone 111 - they can offer advice and onward referral to mental health professionals and crisis intervention teams http://www.nhs.uk/NHSEngland/AboutNHSservices/mental-health-services-explained/Pages/mental-health-emergencies.aspx HOPELineUK - is a confidential advice and support service for young people under the age of 35 who may be having thoughts of suicide or anyone concerned a young person may be having thoughts of suicide. Telephone: 0800 068 41 41 Lines are open 10am-10pm on weekdays 2pm-5pm on Bank Holidays https://www.papyrus-uk.org/help-advice/about-hopelineuk Suicide Prevention toolkit from Public Health England and Business in the Community Business in the Community has partnered with Public Health England to produce an online interconnected suite of toolkits to help every organisation support the mental and physical health and wellbeing of its employees. These materials are freely available and relevant to all employers, irrespective of your size, sector or familiarity with the subject. They are designed to help employers take positive actions to build a culture that champions good mental and physical health and provide a greater understanding of how to help those who need more support. For larger organisations, the toolkits are also useful resources to share with businesses in your supply chain and across your network. Every organisation has an opportunity to support and develop a healthy workforce and it doesn’t need to be complicated – these toolkits will help you to understand and act, step by step. They address topics often shied away from in the workplace, and can provide the first step for an employer to encourage an open, healthy and supportive workplace. http://wellbeing.bitc.org.uk/all-resources/toolkits/suicide-prevention-toolkit Help is at hand - support after someone may have died by suicide Posted: Thu, 25 May 2017 12:49:00 BST HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Occupational Health Service Role of Occupational Health Referral Sickness absence Risk Assessment for New and Expectant Mothers Mental Health Bullying & Harassment Information Job Hazard Information Protocols and Guidance notes Stress information Job Functional Assessment - Information for Managers Research Passports Disability Guidance Access to Work PGCE applicants Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement Role of Occupational Health Risk Assessment for New and Expectant Mothers Bullying & Harassment Information Job Hazard Information Protocols and Guidance notes Job Functional Assessment - Information for Managers Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

title: Job Functional Assessment - Information for Managers
headings: Human Resources Explore
body: A job functional assessment is essentially a comparison of the requirements of a job with the capability of an employee to fulfil that role. This will have been recommended by Occupational Health (OH) where there is reduced capability for medical reasons, with or without adjustments to that role already being in place. There are various factors which may inform how the assessment is conducted: There is no 'one size fits all' approach to these assessments. Usually it is helpful to use the job description as a guide to ascertain the various tasks that comprise a job role and then measure to what degree that task is able to be completed by the employee. This can be done by observing, for example, how easy is it for a Caretaker to walk up and down the stairs of a building when they may be suffering with reduced mobility or by a discussion with a Lecturer about what percentage of their teaching load can be carried out where they might be affected by feelings of stress. The information can then be fed back to OH by management and may help us advise on future appropriate support, adjustments or management of the employee. It may be helpful for managers to talk through how to conduct a particular job functional assessment with OH. Please ring ext 3187 to speak to the OH Nurse for your area if necessary. Funding is available from Access to Work where equipment required is valued at over £1000. Equipment with a value up to £1000 should be provided by the department. title: Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement
headings: Human Resources Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement Explore
body: As you can’t always be sure whether someone has a disability or not, it is best practice to make reasonable adjustments for anyone who is having problems at work. This way you will have done all you can to try to help someone work to the best of their ability and may mean you recruit and keep the most talented people within the University. Talk to the employee to find out exactly what they are having problems doing so you have all the facts. Sometimes the person will be able to suggest a solution themselves, but don’t expect every disabled person or someone suffering with a medical condition to be an expert either on their own disability or on the adjustments that can be made. Remember you can ask for help from either the HR Business Partnering Team or Occupational Health (via a referral). It is best practice that adjustments you agree to support as a manager are documented and reviewed to ensure that they are effective. The Tailored Adjustments Plan/Agreement Record provides you as the manager and the staff member/student with a framework to guide discussions and help you keep a record of agreed adjustments, impact and review. It is simple to use and contains helpful notes and examples of reasonable adjustments. Managers should take further advice from the HR Business Partnering Team if they are unable to accommodate any adjustments suggested or advised by Occupational Health, Access to Work or other similar agencies. title: Disability Staff Network
headings: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Disability Staff Network About the Network Joining the network Explore
body: Co-Chairs: Cathy Banks, Emma Hart and Nic BonneSponsor: Paul MouldContact the Co-Chairs: disabilitystaffnetwork@port.ac.ukJoin the Network The Disability Staff Network is open to all colleagues from across the University. The network is co-chaired by Cathy Banks, Emma Hart and Nic Bonne. Membership is open to any staff member with or without a disability and is a safe space offering invaluable peer-to-peer support, help and advice. provide a voice within the University of Portsmouth for disability equality connect members of staff who have a disability or long-term health condition, and their allies, in a welcoming and supportive community advocate for awareness of disability related issues across the University, challenging perceptions around both visible and hidden disabilities. By joining the network, staff will become part of a community with the opportunity to make a real difference. You’ll benefit from peer-to-peer support in a safe space and be signposted to internal and external support and advice. Join the Network. title: LGBTQ+ Staff Network
headings: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion LGBTQ+ Staff Network About the Network Joining the network Explore
body: Co-Chairs: Kathryn Thompson, Taomé Rees-Dottin, and Katie LovesonSponsor: Mike JohnsonContact the Co-Chairs: lgbtqstaffnetwork@port.ac.ukJoin the Network The LGBTQ+ Staff Network is an inclusive, supportive and welcoming community open to, and for, all LGBTQIA+ members of staff at the University. Co-Chaired by Kathryn Thompson, Taomé Rees-Dottin, and Bhavin Dedhia, the LGBTQ+ Staff Network comprises over 100 staff members from both professional and academic services. support personal and professional development provide social and professional networking opportunities and events engage with the wider community. We provide a safe and non-judgemental space to discuss the experiences and challenges of being part of the LGBTQ+ family, as well as promoting and celebrating our diversity and working towards improving awareness and education for those that wish to learn more. We advise on training and university policies that link to or impact LGBTQ+ identities and campaign to ensure that our voices are heard, recognised and respected within the workplace. We work with the University to ensure members of our community are being treated fairly and without discrimination. We are members of the University’s Equality Diversity and Inclusion Consultative Group and Stonewall’s Diversity Champions scheme, and have a voice on the Professional Support Staff Association. We collaborate with Portsmouth Hospitals NHS Trust LGBTQ+ and Portsmouth City Council’s EDI groups, working to improve the local community and we have a presence at Portsmouth Pride every year alongside the Students’ Union’s LGBTQ+ society. Although the community exists primarily for the benefit of the University’s community of LGBTQIA+ staff, any member of staff with an interest in associated matters, or who considers themselves an LGBTQIA+ ally, may request to join. Join the Network. title: Multicultural Staff Network
headings: Equity, Diversity & Inclusion Multicultural Staff Network About the Network Joining the network Explore Related Links
body: Chair: Bhavin DedhiaSponsor: Dominic PageContact the Co-Chairs: multiculturalstaffnetwork@port.ac.ukFind out more about the Multicultural Staff NetworkJoin the NetworkVisit the Multicultural Staff Network's website The Multicultural Staff Network includes over 100 colleagues from across the University. The network is an invaluable space for peer support. It is open to people of all heritage, particularly those of Black, Asian, and minoritised ethnic and/or religious backgrounds. We provide a brave and safe space to discuss the experiences and challenges of being a minoritised person We advocate for issues relating to race, religion and cultural heritage We advise on University policies that impact on our members We celebrate cultural differences and improve cultural awarenessWe support the University in addressing the patterns of racial inequity; microaggressions and; lack of parity within promotions and development opportunities We have a voice on the Athena Swan and Race and Equality Charter SAT, as well as the Equality Diversity and Inclusion Consultative Group Two of our current areas of focus are supporting anonymous recruitment, which has increased the number of Black, Asian and minoritised ethnic colleagues being shortlisted, as well as advocating for effective action on bullying and harassment. Make a difference by joining an impactful and diverse community of colleagues where you can celebrate and share what makes you you. Our monthly meetings help you to get to know people outside your department, increasing the number of smiley faces you’ll spot across campus. Join the Network.
