title: Effective Leadership and Management
headings: Human Resources Effective Leadership and Management Leadership Attributes Explore
body: We will align our academic and professional service staffing capabilities with our University Strategy 2015-2020, investing in development opportunities for staff that enable them to contribute fully to the delivery of our vision. One of the ways which we will do this is by ensuring that leaders and managers are able to support, engage and direct their colleagues in their work, performance and development and that the workforce is aligned and equipped to meet the changing University needs. Our ability to deliver academic excellence; provide an outstanding student experience and create a workplace culture that is supportive and inclusive, will all be influenced significantly by the quality of leadership and management at all levels. Read more in the People Strategy (2016-2020), which builds on the vision and values described in the University Strategy. At the Senior Leadership Awayday in April 2016, Peter Brook, Director of HR launched a new set of Leadership Attributes. Informed by focus groups, facilitated by the Leadership Foundation for Higher Education and Learning & Development Advisers, the attributes are designed to provide a clear set of expectations for our leaders and also to allow staff to understand what to expect from leaders at the University of Portsmouth. The purpose of the attributes is to Guide and support current and aspiring managers and leaders. They define what good leadership looks like in our organisation and provide a clear set of expectations and behavioural indicators as a benchmark. Inform a new leadership development programme to support the development of the attributes among our leaders and managers Provide a framework for self-assessment and personal development so that staff can plan the development for their current role and future aspirations Inform PDRs; to ensure there is a balanced focus on the behaviours expected (the way we do things) as well as the skills required for the job (what we do) Inform recruitment and selection decisions;, to select people with the attitude and behaviours needed as well as technical competence Inform local induction so that people know what behaviours are expected from the start. To achieve the ambitious aims of our mission and vision, we have developed the University Strategy. The Strategy brings to life what we need to do to make our Vision a reality. The grading process covers the evaluation of a "new" job/role. The definition of a 'new' job/role is where no other similar jobs/roles exists within the University at the grade required. The University HR Department supports academic staff involved in the recruitment and employment of Research Staff. The UoP locally agreed academic role profiles* are generic descriptors of the demands and responsibilities required of most academic staff. Information and guidance on the University Staff card, including card information and replacing a lost card is available from the Information Services MyPort page. You will also find information on the Induction Checklist, a structured framework which includes a health and safety checklist, and a list of essential University policies that all staff should familiarise themselves with. Line managers should also complete a Training and Development plan for new staff and ensure that staff new to the University attend a Staff Induction Conference. title: Developing My Team
headings: Human Resources Developing my team The way we work Core training The Personal Development Review (PDR) process and developing team members University of Portsmouth Staff Mentoring Scheme Exploring development opportunities for team members Staff aspiring to management roles Explore
body: Providing training and development opportunities for team members. Read about the University Mission and Vision , The Way We Work and The Portsmouth Hallmark The University of Portsmouth expects managers and staff to complete certain Core Training - ideally during the probationary period. Some elements are provided through e-learning and others through workshops. We all benefit from taking time out to think about how our work is going and what we are trying to achieve. The Personal Development Review (PDR) process offers a chance to discuss your own thoughts and hear another perspective on your contribution to the department and University. Professional, honest conversations enable us to celebrate successes and discuss problems. Mentoring is a supportive opportunity to aid staff in identifying and achieving career goals. It supports the development of a culture of equality and diversity and contributes to our Athena SWAN and HR Excellence in Research award commitment. The University Staff Mentoring Scheme is available for all staff. At the University of Portsmouth, we are committed to providing high quality learning and development events and opportunities, to ensure that staff understand their role. Our Learning and Development pages provide information about the full range of development opportunities and support for leaders and managers available at the University. The Learning and Development calendar of events lists scheduled dates for all our training events including Recruitment & Selection Training. Learning and Development offer a two-day event entitled Developing your Supervisory Skills. This is aimed primarily at those aspiring or new to the role of first line supervisor. The event is participative and designed to develop practical supervisory management skills and confidence in the role. title: Delivering Team Performance
headings: Human Resources Delivering team performance Academic Workload planning Measuring and Monitoring Performance Managing Under-Performance Procedure (MUPP) Disciplinary Procedure Explore
body: Monitoring and managing individual and team performance. Academic Workload Planning is about planning future workloads for the next academic year, providing a broad overview of all activities undertaken by each member of staff. The Performance and Development Review (PDR)- (for all staff) provides an opportunity to discuss your own thoughts and hear another perspective on your contribution to the department and University. Professional, honest conversations enable us to celebrate successes and discuss problems and feedback provides us with information about what we are valued for and what we could improve on. Departments can plan better too, when they understand the unique skills and experience each person brings to their job. The Managing Under-Performance Procedure sets out how the University will manage employees whose performance has fallen below the standards expected. The primary objective is to help employees to improve performance to an acceptable level in line with the requirements of their role, as summarised in the job description, through support and guidance. The procedure also outlines the steps and actions to be taken in the event of failure to improve performance. FAQs are available for the Managing Under-Performance Procedure. The Disciplinary Procedure document sets out the procedure employees and their managers are required to follow in the event of disciplinary issues that arise at work. It provides a clear and transparent framework to address misconduct which may arise as part of the employment relationship. The Procedure ensures that issues are managed fairly and reasonably. body: Our Recognition Ecosystem brings together a range of complementary schemes (including financial and non-financial awards) tailored to different contexts and levels of recognition, ensuring that contributions of all kinds are valued and celebrated. Find out more on our Recognition Ecosystem pages. The University recognises staff who have completed 25 and 40 years continuous service with the University, with a letter from the Vice-Chancellor celebrating the long service, contribution and accomplishments of the recipient. Continuous service includes service with Portsmouth Polytechnic and members of staff who transferred from Portsmouth College of Art, Design and Media and The Solent School of Nursing, with no break in service. On leaving employment with the University, any outstanding leave entitlement to date of leaving should be taken. title: Managing Change
headings: Human Resources Managing change Organisational Change Policy: Redeployment and Redundancy (OCRR) Online Resources Additional Support Explore
body: Guiding your team through change, and embedding new practices into business operations. The Organisational Change Policy: Redeployment and Redundancy (OCRR) sets out the University’s approach to dealing with organisational change that may lead to a reduction in employee numbers, however, the approach is applicable to any form of organisational change, in particular, the sections on consultation and communication and the business case approval process. Staff may be placed in redeployment for a variety of reasons, i.e. redundancy, end of fixed term contract, as a result of MSA, MUPP, Disciplinary process etc. The redeployment module has been created to allow all staff on the redeployment register direct access to all vacancies as HR receive them. Staff placed in redeployment will have visibility of new vacancies and the opportunity to apply for them, before they are published on the recruitment website. If you have a member of staff who is being placed in redeployment, the Online Redeployment Guide- Applying for Vacancies (Candidates Only) will provide them with help and guidance on applying for vacancies. Change is an ever-present and essential part of daily life, and the University is a constantly evolving organisation. While this can make it a dynamic and vibrant place to work, change needs to be managed effectively if we are to realise the benefits within the University. Delivering Successful Change presents a four-stage approach to planning, delivering and embedding change. Whatever your role or grade, the Change Management section of the innovative online Learning & Development Toolkit is packed with professional development materials designed to assist you in dealing with the day-to-day challenges of your job. How Does This Change Make Me Feel? The ACAS How to manage change advisory booklet can help line managers to manage major change more effectively. The JISC Online Change Management Info-Kit was developed from a HEFCE (Higher Educational Funding Council for England) Good Management Practice Project led by the University of Luton (now the University of Bedfordshire) entitled ‘Effecting Change in Higher Education’. Please note: The JISC website states the content has been archived but the Info-kit can still be accessed and is still relevant. The project team consulted widely on aspects of change in the sector and put together theories, approaches and tools that resonated with them and with those they talked to about their experiences of the practical difficulties of managing change. The HR Business Partnering Teams provide professional guidance and support for individuals and managers on change issues. Your HR Business Partner should always be consulted if you are considering a change initiative that will impact on our employees. The Employee Assistance Programme can offer you support, information and advice on a range of change issues, you can also call and talk in confidence to one of their telephone Helpline advisors. Our Occupational Health department can advise and support where an individual’s health and well-being may be affected by change. Our Internal Communications department can advise on any aspect of staff communications including communication plans for change initiatives. You may also be interested in these handy tips for communicating well with your team and your colleagues, for example, how to hold better meetings, planning your messages and relying less on email. title: Managing leave and absence
headings: Human Resources Managing leave and absence Annual leave Bank Holiday entitlement Half-day working start/finish times Christmas closure dates Managing sickness absence Entitlement to Sick Pay for Variable Hours Staff Family friendly entitlements Compassionate leave Sabbaticals Other absence and leave Adverse weather and travel disruption Unauthorised absence Explore
body: The annual leave year for all staff runs from 1 October to 30 September in any year and you should ensure your staff take their entitlement within this time-frame. Academic and Research staff receive an annual leave entitlement of 35 days (259 hours) per year. These staff include: Deans, Heads, Professors, Readers, Researchers, University Tutors and Graduate Teaching Assistants. Professional Services staff receive an annual leave entitlement of 32 days (236.8 hours) per year. Part-time staff have a pro-rata entitlement to leave based on the number of hours/weeks they work (their fte). Temporary staff on variable hours contracts have an entitlement to 32 days per annum pro rata and this will be calculated in proportion to the number of hours worked. All annual leave must be submitted for your authorisation in advance, via Employee Self Service (ESS). Information on how to authorise annual leave is available on our HR Systems page. If you are going to be absent for an extended period, you should set up a redirect for a colleague to authorise leave requests in your absence. Information about this is also available on our HR Systems page. Joining or leaving the University - leave entitlement Holiday entitlement for part-years on starting (and leaving) is calculated on complete days of service. If a member of your team is leaving employment at the University, any outstanding leave entitlement to date of leaving should be taken. If this is not possible, their outstanding leave entitlement will be paid with the final salary. Leave already taken that is in excess of their entitlement will be deducted from the final salary. All staff benefit from a proportional entitlement to Bank Holiday and Christmas closure days off, which is included within the annual leave entitlement. The entitlement period runs from 1 October to 30 September each year. If a member of staff wishes to take a half-day, the system will deduct half of their working day, regardless of start and end times. For example, if you work a 7 hour day, it will deduct 3.5 hours. The University is closed over the Christmas period. Staff are not expected to take annual leave to cover this time: working days within the period are allocated as a combination of statutory and customary holidays. University Christmas Closure dates. Our Managing Sickness Absence page sets out the process both employees and their managers are required to follow in the event of absence from work due to sickness and the procedure when ill health/disability prevents effective performance of an employee’s duties. You can also download the Sickness Absence and Ill Health Policy. Managers are required to record sickness absence in iTrent via MSS from day one, as soon as the absence is notified. Absences must be recorded as open-ended and updated on the employee’s return to work. Managers must also conduct a return-to-work discussion after every period of sickness absence. These discussions should take place in an appropriate confidential setting and provide an opportunity to support the employee’s return to work. Where an employee has been absent for a prolonged period, the discussion should take place before the proposed return date to support their re-introduction to the workplace. Managers must complete and submit the Return to Work form via MSS following each discussion to ensure accurate documentation and appropriate follow-up. University’s Occupational Health Service provides advice to individuals and managers on work related health problems and health problems that can affect work. You can refer a member of your staff to Occupational Health (OH) for advice if their health is affecting their fitness to carry out their role, or the role is adversely affecting their health. For staff who have been referred, you can advise them to visit our Referral to Occupational Health webpage for further information. There is also a Staff Guide to Occupational Health Referral and Advice available to download, which also provides information and advice. Additional information is available on our Ensuring Team Health, Safety and Wellbeing section. If you have any staff on a Variable Hours Contract, they are entitled to claim Occupational Sick Pay in line with their contract of employment. Full details are available at our page Entitlement to Sick pay for Variable Hours Staff. The University provides a wide range of Family Friendly entitlements * including information & guidance regarding Adoption/Maternity/Paternity Leave & Pay, Parental Leave, the Right to Request Flexible Working and Time-off for Dependants. Line managers are authorised to grant up to five days' leave on compassionate grounds. Requests for leave of more than five days should be made to the Director of Human Resources, who is authorised to grant up to 10 days leave in any one year. In exceptional circumstances, leave in excess of 10 days may be granted. Line managers must notify HR of any compassionate leave taken by their staff by emailing details to the HR Service Centre. Any academic member of staff, on an open-ended contract to undertake research, innovation and teaching, and who has three years’ continuous service with the University of Portsmouth at the time of commencing the sabbatical, is normally eligible to apply for a sabbatical. Periods of maternity, paternity, adoption leave or long-term sickness absence do count towards the three years’ continuous service. Our Sabbatical Leave Policy provides full details. Information & guidance is available regarding other absence and leave including: Time off for public duties The University recognises that adverse weather conditions can prevent employees from reaching their normal place of employment. All employees are expected to make every reasonable effort to attend for work, but without compromising their health and safety. Where employees are unable to attend for work because of adverse weather, they should report this to their manager as early as possible. If member of staff reports that they are unable to get to work because of adverse weather, and the University is open, then the individual should be offered to take the time as either unpaid, annual leave or flexi-time that they will need to work back at a later date, or where appropriate, work from home on agreed areas of work. If the University takes the decision to close because of adverse weather, casual and PTHP staff who were scheduled to be in work should be paid for the hours they would have been expected to work whilst the University was closed. Timesheets should be completed in the normal way. Staff taking booked annual leave for all or part of the time when the University was closed will not get these days credited back. Unauthorised absence is not acceptable and may lead to disciplinary action including dismissal. title: Managing Individual Contracts
headings: Human Resources Managing individual contracts Supporting team members on secondment Regrading an existing role Dealing with flexible work requests Disclosure and Barring Service Checks (DBS) Research Passports Vetting policy Police Apprenticeship consortium, Non Police Personnel Vetting Managing visa restrictions Explore
body: Managing the institution's contractual relationships with individual team members. The University of Portsmouth is committed to creating an equitable, developmental and motivating working environment, which values and empowers people at all levels. Secondment opportunities can be an appropriate means of achieving this commitment. The Secondment Policy provides the basis for all formal internal and external secondment arrangements across the University. International Secondment The term ‘international secondment’ refers to the temporary transfer of a member of staff from their normal role to do another role on a fixed term basis outside of the UK internationally. The University's International Secondment policy is designed to provide managers and staff with information about the main issues to be considered when seconding a member of staff overseas. The Regrading Process covers the evaluation of an existing job/role that is currently filled by a member of staff, where it is deemed the role has grown in level of responsibility/competence- find out more about regrading an existing role*. The Employment Rights Act 1996 gives all employees the chance to request a change to their contractual terms and conditions of employment to work more flexibly. This could be a change in their working pattern, or perhaps the number of hours they work. For more information, read the Right to Request Flexible Working -Guidance The University’s Human Resources (HR) department is responsible for administering DBS checks for staff where they are required for employment purposes. A Research Passport is the mechanism for non-NHS staff (that is, higher education institution employees who have no contractual arrangement with the NHS) to obtain an Honorary Research Contract or Letter of Access when they propose to carry out research in the NHS. The Vetting policy Police Apprenticeship consortium, Non Police Personnel Vetting (NPPV) is applicable to all University employees who have access to Police apprenticeships data and/or are likely to visit police premises during their employment. The appropriate vetting is identified as Non Police Personnel Level 2 (NPPV). Right to Work document check Under the Immigration Act 2014, we have a duty to prevent illegal working by carrying out appropriate document checks to confirm that an applicant has the right to work in the UK. Employing Tier 4 Sponsored Students This guidance provides additional guidance for those managers who employ, or are likely to employ, students who are in the UK under a Tier 4 Sponsorship visa. This includes Undergraduate, Postgraduate - Taught and Postgraduate - Research students. Visas and Immigration- Quick Reference Migrants we sponsor under the points based system (Tiers 2, 4, and 5) have very specific record keeping and reporting rules we must follow. title: Ensuring Team Health, Safety and Wellbeing
headings: Human Resources Ensuring team health, safety and wellbeing Ill health, managing sickness absence and medical issues Managing health and safety Travelling overseas on University business Wellbeing Family friendly entitlements Hybrid working Recognising and dealing with issues Support networks Further advice and information Explore
body: Monitoring and managing team and employee health, safety and wellbeing, and ensuring compliance with legislation. Our Managing Sickness Absence page sets out the process both employees and their managers are required to follow in the event of absence from work due to sickness and the procedure when ill health/disability prevents effective performance of an employee’s duties. You can also download the Managing Sickness Absence Procedure. The University’s Occupational Health Service provides advice to individuals and managers on work related health problems and health problems that can affect work. Our Occupational Health pages also contain information and advice on Mental Health. The Mindful Employer Charter is an initiative which the University of Portsmouth is signed up to, which works to increase awareness of mental health in the workplace. The University Disability Guidance sets out the process for putting in place appropriate adjustments for staff with disabilities. It is recognised that some disabled employees may need time off for a reason relating to their disability.The Disability Absence- Guidance for Managers and Staff provides clarity to both managers and staff. The University provides an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) which offers a free, confidential Helpline which provides access to information, advice and support (including management support), and onward referral to telephone or face-to-face counselling where appropriate. The service is designed to help individuals deal with a range of problems, no matter how big or small, which may affect their personal lives or performance at work. The Employee Alcohol and Drugs Policy covers both the use and misuse of alcohol or drugs that may impair an individual’s performance or affect their behaviour. The term ‘drugs’ has been used in this Policy to include all drugs and substances, legal or illegal, which adversely affect behaviour or performance. The University considers that a strong health, safety and wellbeing culture together with a safe and secure environment are fundamental to its success. To find out more, visit the Health and Safety Policy webpage where you can download the Health & Safety Policy Statement and access the Health & Safety A-Z. Without exception, overseas travel must be formally approved in principle by the Head of Department and/or Line Manager. This is by means of authorised FIN1 (staff) or FIN30 (student). If the trip is being financed by an external organisation and therefore does not require a FIN1/ FIN30, authorisation from the Head of Department and/or Line Manager must still be sought. Please see the University Overseas Travel section for full information and guidance on the procedures for travelling overseas on University business. The Wellbeing section of the Staff Essentials pages contains also information and advice on Mental Wellbeing, as well as Family, Physical Activity and Diet and Nutrition. The Dignity and Respect policy sets out the University’s commitment to delivering a working and learning environment of dignity and respect, free from harassment and bullying, ensuring we adhere to the Equality Act 2010. The Policy sets out the responsibilities of everyone in ensuring all feel valued and respected and are able to work and study in a discrimination-free environment that promotes equality of opportunity and where difference is valued and respected by all. The University provides a wide range of Family Friendly entitlements * including information & guidance regarding Adoption/Maternity/Paternity Leave & Pay, Parental Leave, the Right to Request Flexible Working and Time-off for Dependants. This guidance has been developed to define and support the evolution of hybrid working practices for staff and workers employed by the University of Portsmouth. The purpose of the hybrid working guidance is to establish the standards, working practices and supported configurations of hybrid working solutions. Hybrid working will be evolving as it is implemented over the 2021/22 academic year and it will be reviewed regularly as we learn together how to best work with our new arrangements. We would ask that you first familiarise yourself with the University of Portsmouth Hybrid Working Guidance. Hybrid Working Guidance Hybrid Working Guidance for Managers Hybrid Working Flowchart The following links provide useful information will help you to deal with unexpected situations in the workplace. Fire Safety (inc Evacuation Procedure) Managing Health & Safety is essential in creating a safe working environment Reporting Faults or requesting maintenance The term ‘whistleblowing’ is the disclosure of information by an employee or worker (see the definition in section 1 of the Whistleblowing Policy for a full definition of those individuals who come within these categories) which relates to some danger, fraud or other illegal or unethical conduct in the workplace. Dignity and Respect policy- Dignity and respect is undermined by harassment and bullying. Both of these are regarded seriously and may be treated as grounds for disciplinary action. The University takes a zero tolerance approach towards harassment or bullying. It is also expects that dignity and respect towards others is demonstrated by all who work or study at the University. Grievances are concerns, problems or complaints raised by a staff member with managers regarding working conditions or relationships with colleagues. It is clearly in the University’s and employee’s interests to resolve problems early before they escalate into major difficulties. You can download the Grievance procedure and FAQs. The Investigation Guidelines document is designed to help employees and managers conduct effective investigations. When investigating an issue it is important to take care to deal with all parties in a fair and consistent manner. The nature and extent of an investigation will depend on the matter being investigated. The University provides a number of networks intended to support issues relating to harassment, bullying and intimidation in the workplace, or more general advice, counselling and guidance, available at any time of the day or night. As part of the University's commitment to equal opportunities, the Equality and Diversity Unit has supported the development of a number of staff networks. These exist to allow staff from similar backgrounds to meet, network and discuss the issues that matter to them. Advice on harassment, bullying and intimidation is provided through a trained Harassment Advisor Network. Find out all about the wide range of work carried out by the Equality and Diversity team within the University, covering all aspects of equality and diversity for both staff and students . As part of the University's commitment to equal opportunities, the Equality and Diversity Unit has created a number of staff networks. These exist to allow staff from similar backgrounds to meet, network and discuss the issues that matter to them. The HR Business Partnering teams provide professional guidance and support for individuals and managers across all HR policies and procedures across the University, with each team supporting different Faculties and Professional Services. They also provide advice on employment solutions and best practice / legislative advice. The University Chaplaincy provides a quiet space for socialising, relaxing, reflection, thought, stillness and prayer. The Chaplains are trained to listen and be alongside people at the high and low points of life in a non-judgemental way. They work confidentially across the University and the service is open to all staff and students of all faiths and none. title: Business Partnering Team
headings: Human Resources The HR Business Partnering (HRBP) Team Business Partnering Team by Faculty/Department FACULTIES Business & Law Creative and Cultural Industries Humanities and Social Sciences Science and Health Technology Institute of Professional and Flexible Learning (IPFL) DEPARTMENTS Academic Registry (DSAA) Academic Services (DSAA) Careers and Employability Services Centre for Academic and Digital Innovation Estates & Campus Services Finance Global Human Resources Library & IS London Campus Recruitment, Marketing and Communications Department of Research and Innovation Sport and Recreation Student Services Corporate Governance Planning Transformation Directorate Explore
body: The HRBP Team brings together specialist functions across HR business partnering, employee relations, EDI and staff wellbeing. Strategic HR Business Partners: Work closely with Executive Deans and Directors to align people strategies with local goals and people plans. They act as a consultant and advisor on workforce planning, organisational design, culture, and change initiatives. They use data and insights to identify opportunities that improve performance, engagement, and capability, while also anticipating future workforce needs. They also play a key role in supporting and embedding wider HR initiatives, ensuring consistent adoption and impact across the institution. Employee Relations Team: Each Employee Relations Adviser (ERA) is dedicated to providing support to various departments and faculties and should be regarded as the initial point of contact for all employee relations matters including: Early intervention; tailored guidance on simple ER issues through to complex case management, including: performance management; absence management; discipline and grievance; cessation of fixed term contracts. The team is also heavily involved in grading reviews and the review of employment policy and best practice and will lead on trade union liaison on employment and policy matters. HR Coordinator: Involved in the whole range of HR activity to support the team and managers including progressing contractual work as well as more complex appeals preparations, Job evaluation (grading) Equity, Diversity and Inclusion Team: Provide expert consultancy to support the creation of an inclusive working, learning and social environment where all colleagues and students feel like they belong and can thrive. The team leads key engagement and awareness activities, including staff network support, regular communications and events marking cultural and awareness occasions. In addition, the team informs University policy and practice by guiding EDI objectives, providing project management for the University's EDI charter commitments and delivering specialist training. Through comprehensive data monitoring and reporting, the team identifies trends that drive evidence‑based improvement across the institution. Staff Wellbeing Manager: Provides guidance and support to colleagues and managers on matters relating to staff wellbeing, including offering an informal space for individuals to talk through challenges impacting their wellbeing and signposting to appropriate support. The Manager also leads the development of wellbeing initiatives, campaigns, events, and resources that raise awareness, strengthen community and belonging, and promote a positive and healthy working environment. (link to Online Wellness Hub) A copy of the HR Business Partnering team structure can be found here. title: Employee Benefits Platform
headings: Human Resources Employee Benefits Platform Already registered? Overview Who does this apply to? What do I need to consider? What do I need to do? What else do I need to know? How do I get help? Explore
body: Please click on Employee Benefits Platform (Vivup) to access the portal. The platform has 100’s of fantastic discounts and offers, you’re sure to find many that are relevant to you. Whether you’d like to save money on your grocery shopping, days out and entertainment or household purchases, there are plenty of offers for you. In addition to the benefits platform Vivup also provide our Employee Assistance Program and Your Care Wellbeing Platform. All current University employees are eligible to access the Employee Benefits Portal. Access to the portal is free while you remain an employee of the University. Please note that if you are on a Zero Hours contract or are not a direct employee of the University, you are not eligible for this benefit. As access to the portal is dependent on your being a University employee, if you are planning to leave the University then you will need to make sure that you don’t sign-up to discounts that will become available after you leave, as your account will be cancelled. This wouldn’t affect existing discount cards or evouchers with money still on them. The portal is only for University employees. You are welcome to purchase items through the portal for friends and family but you must not share your login or password or let anyone else use the portal with your account. You will be automatically registered with the portal within one month of joining the University and will receive an email from email@employeebenefits.vivup.co.uk asking you to complete the registration when you will be requested to set a password. If you do not receive this email or wish to be registered sooner, please raise a Hornbill ticket with Reward & Benefits, Get in touch, and we will add you to the system. Once registered you can access the portal at http://www.port.ac.uk/mybenefits or download the Vivup app on your phone to access while out and about. You need to be aware that personal data will be held and processed by a third party and used in accordance with their terms and conditions. The University has no access to most of the data you provide to Vivup. Nothing personal about you will be shared with the University apart from your name, email address, department and employee number which is used to confirm that you are an employee of the University of Portsmouth. We also receive anonymised information on numbers of registrations, site visits, numbers of orders etc. If you have any issues please please raise a Hornbill ticket with Reward & Benefits, Get in touch, or the Vivup Help Services: Helpline: 01252 784540 Email: customersupport@vivup.co.uk Opening times: Monday - Friday; 8am - 6pm for email and telephone; Monday - Friday 10am - 4pm for the chat function; Saturday - Sunday 10am - 2pm for the chat function only HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Health and Wellbeing Retail Discounts Employee Benefits Platform Events and New or Limited Time Offers Financial Wellbeing Travel Discounts Leave and Absence Leisure, Food and Drink Discounts Pay and Recognition Personal Development My Reward and Benefits menu Life Insurance Hardware and Software Recognition Retail Discounts Employee Benefits Platform Employee Benefits Platform Events and New or Limited Time Offers Leisure, Food and Drink Discounts My Reward and Benefits menu Hardware and Software Occupational Health Service Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

title: Events and New or Limited Time Offers
headings: Human Resources New or Time Limited Offers The Akash restaurant - 10% Discount FREE Spring Skin & Hair Bundle set, worth up to £34 - courtesy of grüum! Discounts on Sailing Holidays Mortgage Webinars Explore
body: The following offers have been made available to University of Portsmouth employees. The University of Portsmouth will not be liable for any actions taken as a result of these offers. The Akash restaurant, conveniently located in the heart of Southsea's Albert Road, is offering all employees a 10% discount off the total bill on meals eaten in the restaurant. Simply show your staff pass on arrival at the restaurant. This family run restaurant has been serving Portsmouth since the 1970's with the philosophy "good food, good company and genuine hospitality". To find more information on the delicious food available, please visit The Akash website and to reserve a table follow this link. Offer valid for dine-in only Excludes Fridays and Saturdays Excludes the month of December Cannot be used in conjunction with any other offer (including student meal deals) The Akash reserves the right to withdraw this offer at any time Address: 99 - 101 Albert Road, Southsea, Hampshire, PO5 2SG Spring’s calling for a fresh start. Choose one of three feel-good sets packed with planet-friendly skin and hair favourites, worth up to £34. Thoughtfully curated, beautifully effective, and yours FREE - just cover delivery. grüum is a Manchester brand on a mission to make skin, hair, and self care products that are kind to you as well as the world we share. Their passion lies in creating effective, affordable, planet friendly personal care for everyone. Naturally. Pick the set that suits your routine and let your spring glow unfold. 3-Step Skin Kit - £32 NOW FREE Cleansing Balm – Melts away make-up and daily grime, leaving skin soft, calm and comfortably clean. Exfoliating Toner – Gently smooths and brightens with glycolic acid for fresh, radiant skin. Facial Serum – Lightweight and nourishing, powered by kakadu plum to boost glow and hydration. Skin & Hair Refresh - £29 NOW FREE Hydrating Shampoo Bar – A creamy coconut cleanse that leaves hair soft, shiny and refreshed. Gentle Face Wash Bar – Mild, low-waste cleansing that purifies without stripping. Roll-on Fragrance – Choose between a fresh floral spring scent or a warm, woody blend for an easy, on-the-go lift. Skin Saviour Set - £34 NOW FREE Facial Moisture Bar – Deep, water-free hydration that melts into skin. Intensive Hydrating Balm – A rich rescue for dry patches, hands and everything in between. Body Butter Bar – Velvety nourishment that leaves skin supple and softly scented. Worth up to £34 and completely FREE - just cover delivery. Limited bundles, three beautiful choices, zero fuss. Pick your spring set and let simple, sustainable self-care do the rest. Redemption Instructions Add your free set to the basket One free set per person, while stocks last. Delivery charge applies. UK/NI only Cannot be used in conjunction with any other offer or discount For full T&C’s please visit https://www.gruum.com/terms-conditions/ All employees, students and alumni can get a 10% discount on all trips within Europe at More Sailing If you want an adventure on the sea, take a look at the trips available. The 10% discount can be redeemed by using the code student10 when booking on the site. We have partnered with Charles Cameron & Associates (CC&A) to provide colleagues with completely free of charge financial education and mortgage advice, guidance and brokerage services. CC&A will be offering monthly webinars focusing on (a) first time buyers, (b) moving home, (c) remortgaging, and (d) buy-to-let. Details of the webinars available and the easy booking process are available here, the next webinars for each category are: General: 14 April 2026, 12.00pm to 1.00pm First Time Buyers: 16 April 2026, 12.00pm to 1.00pm Home Movers: 22 April 2026, 12.00pm to 1.00pm Buy to Let: 26 May 2026, 12.00pm to 1.00pm Remortgages: 28 April 2026, 12.00pm to 1.00pm You can book an individual online appointment, access the educational content, or book on to a webinar online, by email: info@ccameron.co.uk, or call 020 7953 7040. title: Health and Wellbeing
headings: Human Resources Health & Wellbeing Employee Assistance Programme Occupational Health Your Care - Wellbeing Platform 24/7 Health Support Line Ravelin Sports Centre Greenwich Leisure Limited - 20% discount South Coast Rx Physiotherapy Uni-Health Free Eye Test and Glasses for Display Screen Equipment use Dentistry The Chaplaincy Staff Networks Learning and Development Team Explore
body: The University of Portsmouth cares about the health and wellbeing of all its employees and has a number of resources and support programmes available to you. Please click on the links below to find more information on the options available to you. If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk Speak in confidence to an independent and fully qualified counsellor 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year to discuss any emotional, personal or work-related problems. Talking to one of Vivup’s experienced counsellors or psychotherapists can help you understand a problem, make a tough decision, try a different approach or just learn how to cope with a difficult situation. The aim is to support you and find a solution to whatever is worrying you. The University's Occupational Health Service provides advice to managers and employees on work related health problems We believe we are all happier and healthier when able to take control of our own wellbeing, so in addition to our Employee Assistance Program you also have access to ‘Your Care’, a dynamic wellbeing platform offering a range of personal, emotional and financial wellbeing tools to help you be a happier, healthier you! • Assessments - Identify the risk of developing leading lifestyle-related diseases, along with mental health and financial scores • Goals - The option to set health-boosting goals with small, sustainable lifestyle changes • Activity Data - Connectivity with your health apps to automatically track goal progress • Resources - Blogs, videos, recipes and more resources providing inspiration and motivation • Support - Access to 24/7 support and counselling (via our EAP), 365 days a year Visit Health & Wellbeing at http://www.port.ac.uk/mybenefits to access the platform Would you like to speak to a medical professional regarding a health concern? Provided by AXA Health, the 24/7 health support line is available to help put your mind at ease. Whatever the health question is, the team of nurses, counsellors, midwives and pharmacists is on hand to support you. Some of the ways they can help include: Your health - if you have a question or a worry about your health, you can speak to a nurse. Medical results - If you've received some medical test results that you don't understand, they can support you with any medical jargon. Prescribed medication - if you have a question about medication that you've been prescribed, the team of pharmacists is on hand to help. Pregnancy questions - you can speak to a midwife about any pregnancy-related questions that you may have. Support with stress - you can talk to a counsellor about how you're feeling. To speak to a medical professional call 0345 850 0266 and tell them you are an employee of the University of Portsmouth and accessing the scheme through the Hampshire Chambers of Commerce. Please note: Nurses and counsellors are available 24/7. Midwives and pharmacists are available 8am - 8pm Monday to Friday, 8am - 4pm Saturday and 8am - 12pm Sunday. The University has an outstanding, award winning, sports centre. Students, staff and local residents are all able to enjoy the facilities, with a range of classes and courses, personal training and very affordable membership with great benefits available. Staff members can access discounted membership. Not quite ready to take out a membership, how about trying the Super Session Pass which allows you to have 10 Gym, Swim or Fitness Classes at a discounted rate. As an employee of the University of Portsmouth you can get a 20% discount at Gyms provided by Greenwich Leisure Limited: Chingford Leisure Park Leyton Leisure Centre Leytonstone Leisure Centre Waltham Forest Feel Good Centre Walthamstow Leisure Centre Ready to apply? Click on the Greenwich Leisure Membership page Need some help in completing the membership page please see this document. Conveniently located in Ravelin Sports Centre, South Coast Rx Physiotherapy offer all staff a 10% discount off all initial appointments. They also run a Pilates class every Wednesday 10.30am - 11.30am Access Private Medical Insurance with an Education Discount with Uni-Health.co.uk The team specialises in helping Lecturers, Teachers and Staff choose the most suitable Private Healthcare policy and find any discount available to you. You are entitled to receive a free eye test and support towards the cost of eyeware, should you require glasses solely for the use of Display Screen Equipment (DSE). This is provided in the form of a Specsavers voucher that will grant a free eye test and £45 towards the cost of glasses that are required for DSE use. Detailed information and a link to request a voucher is available via Hornbill. The Dental Academy provides dental care by students to some patients who do not currently have a dentist. Find out more. The Chaplaincy is available to all employees and students for a confidential chat, somewhere to have lunch with friends, or just some quiet space. Find out more. Staff Networks allow employees from similar backgrounds to meet, network and discuss the issues that matter to them. Find out more. Visit the Learning and Development (L&D) UoP Wellness Hub which has details of both internal and external tools and resources to support staff in their health and wellbeing. body: If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk Please complete this form if you are aware of any local businesses that would be willing to offer a discount. You now have access to a huge range of discounts across the UK’s major retailers including supermarkets, technology, fashion, travel and much more! If eligible, you will be registered on the platform within a month of joining the University and will receive an email from email@employeebenefits.vivup.co.uk asking you to accept the registration. To find out more about the platform please follow this link or if already registered, access the savings at https://port.ac.uk/mybenefits. The platform has 100’s of fantastic discounts and offers, you’re sure to find many that are relevant to you. Whether you’d like to save money on your grocery shopping, days out and entertainment or household purchases, there are plenty of offers for you. The Vivup Employee Benefits Platform is available both online and via a mobile phone app. Over time the platform will bring together all of our employee benefits in to one easy to access place. Register now at http://www.port.ac.uk/mybenefits Accessible through our Vivup Employee Benefits platform, you could find a brand new electric or plug-in hybrid car with an inclusive motoring package and one convenient monthly amount taken from your gross salary. Our car benefit scheme provides all that and more! To access the scheme, please register at http://www.port.ac.uk/mybenefits Spring’s calling for a fresh start. Choose one of three feel-good sets packed with planet-friendly skin and hair favourites, worth up to £34. Thoughtfully curated, beautifully effective, and yours FREE - just cover delivery. grüum is a Manchester brand on a mission to make skin, hair, and self care products that are kind to you as well as the world we share. Their passion lies in creating effective, affordable, planet friendly personal care for everyone. Naturally. Get access to offers on food and essentials, tech, travel, home delivery and more for free. Discounts are available on second hand bikes, but are dependent on the bikes and will be priced accordingly Velopy, 151-153 New Road, PO2 7QS - 10% off new bikes, servicing and repairs but NOT parts or accessories. title: Travel Discounts
headings: Human Resources Travel Discounts Ferries and Hovercraft Trains and Buses Cars and Bikes Employee Benefits Platform (Vivup) Explore
body: If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk As a University, we aim to reduce congestion in Portsmouth and improve the air quality for everyone who studies, lives and works in our city. A range of travel choices and discounts are available for you, as we continually look for more offers for staff. As an island city, we are well connected across the Solent to the Isle of Wight, Gosport and Hayling Island. There are a number of choices for foot and bike passengers and cars to the Isle of Wight. Travelling by train or bus significantly reduces your environmental impact locally when compared to the equivalent car journey. The closest train stations to the University are Portsmouth and Southsea and Portsmouth Harbour and are a short walk to the University. In addition to the city bus services, there are regular daily direct coach services to and from Portsmouth and London, Birmingham, Heathrow and other locations around the country together with the University Bus service which runs during term time. The university offers a salary sacrifice scheme to allow the purchase of electric and hybrid vehicles together with discounted car parks. Discounts are available on bikes including a salary sacrifice scheme to purchase a bike for travel to work. The Employee Benefits Platform offers travel discounts across accommodation, holiday discounts, cruises, travel and airport extras and car hire transport. title: Financial Wellbeing
headings: Human Resources Financial Benefits and Wellbeing Pension Schemes LGPS Shared Cost Additional Voluntary Contributions (AVCs) Wesleyan - Pension Webinars FREE Will Writing Service FREE Mortgage Education, Advice and Brokerage Services FREE Legal Advice Tuition Fee Discounts PhD/Professional Doctorate support for Academic Staff Funded Apprenticeships The following websites are aimed at staff with either financial worries or looking for financial advice and can provide information and confidential advice. Please note that the University of Portsmouth neither endorses nor accepts any liability for any actions taken as a result of advice obtained from these websites. Money Helper citizens advice Explore
body: If you have any queries on the information in these pages, please contact reward@port.ac.uk The University is proud to offer competitive and leading pension schemes to all our employees, and these form one of the biggest benefits available to you as an employee. Please go to our Pensions page to access the information you need to know. As a member of the Local Government Pension Scheme, you have a unique opportunity to invest in your financial future in a cost efficient way. Shared Cost AVCs allow you add additional value to your pension while saving National Insurance and Tax on these contributions. A basic rate taxpayer can add £100 to their pension contributions at a cost of only £68.12. AVCs allow you to add extra value to your pension at any stage of your career. They are linked to LGPS but work more like a personal pension where you build up a separate pot of benefits with additional flexibilities, such as choosing how much you wish to pay and how it is invested. The University have secured a partnership with mymoneymatters which allows you to save both National Insurance and Tax on all AVC payments. To find out more please visit the mymoneymatters website. We encourage you to read through the information and understand the options available. Wesleyan Financial Services (who are specialist financial advisors, regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA)), offer a free public webinar for any members of either the Local Government, Teachers' or National Health Service Pension Schemes. We have partnered with the Taylor Lucas Partnership to make available their will writing services at no cost to all University of Portsmouth colleagues (and also partners or spouses). This is a service which normally costs around £90-100 for a single will and £140-150 for two mirror wills. The simple act of leaving a will can significantly reduce the administrative and emotional burdens on your loved ones at a time when frankly they are having a difficult enough time coping with having lost you. A Will is also the only legal way to specify who is to become the guardian of any children you may have under the age of 18. This is a quick, easy, and free way to achieve certainty and peace of mind for you and your loved ones. You can find all the information you need here and here's a brochure which explains the scheme. We have partnered with Charles Cameron & Associates (CC&A) to provide colleagues with completely free of charge financial education and mortgage advice, guidance and brokerage services. Whether you are a first-time buyer, looking to re-mortgage or finance an investment property, they can help you access the best possible financial solutions for your needs. (Please note - any advice provided to you by CC&A is a strictly individual arrangement between yourself and CC&A. The University of Portsmouth will not be liable for that advice or any actions you take as a result of it under any circumstances.) For more information, please click here. If you live or work in the Portsmouth area, you can get free and confidential legal advice from the University's General Legal Advice Clinic. You'll receive advice from our law students studying law degrees in the Portsmouth Law School. Their advice is guided by qualified solicitors and an experienced tutor. They can't give advice on issues to do with immigration and nationality, criminal law or criminal proceedings. They also can't give advice where a conflict of interest may arise, for example disputes which involve the University, but if they can't help, they'll put you in touch with someone who can. Follow this link to find out more information. The University offers a 30% discount to self-funding, Student Loans Company funded and externally sponsored University of Portsmouth staff members undertaking part-time HE3 courses and to the children of university staff members undertaking undergraduate and postgraduate taught full-time and part-time study. The detailed conditions and how to apply are set out in the Tuition Fees Discounts Policy. The University will waive the tuition fees for academic staff (those on full or fractional contracts) wishing to commence a doctoral programme of study in the Faculty in which they are based. This will apply providing such study concords with the strategic interests of the Faculty, and a suitable local supervisory team can be found. For more information, please consult the Faculty Sponsored Doctorates for Academic Staff document. If you are interested in exploring this opportunity further then please discuss with your Head of School in the first instance. NOTE: This opportunity is only available to those academic staff who currently (i) do not possess and (ii) are not already enrolled on a programme of doctoral study. Staff in various roles across the University can complete an apprenticeship delivered by the University of Portsmouth or an external provider. Degree Apprenticeships offer staff the opportunity to develop skills and knowledge in a professional field while working towards the completion of a degree. This is particularly suitable for those in the early stages of their careers. Apprenticeships for University staff are funded by the Apprenticeship Levy, meaning that staff can achieve a full degree without the financial costs. Non-Degree Apprenticeships are available for staff using external providers. Find out more about apprenticeships and how you can learn while you earn here. We invite applications from existing University staff who are on an open-ended contract, or a fixed term contract that does not expire before the end of the selected course. All staff must have the support of their line manager prior to applying. If you have any questions, please contact staff-apprenticeships@port.ac.uk Free impartial guidance on: Benefits Family and Care Money Troubles Savings Everyday money Homes Pensions and retirement Work Pensions and retirement For more information follow this link. title: Leisure, Food and Drink Discounts
headings: Human Resources Leisure, Food and Drink Discounts The Akash restaurant - 10% Discount Theatre discounts for staff at the New Theatre Royal and the Kings Theatre How to claim your complementary membership 20% off food and drink in venues in Portsmouth, Walthamstow and beyond! Stay Lucky Health@Handleys Portsmouth Historic Dockyard - Exclusive Discounts Discounts on Sailing Holidays Ravelin Sports Centre Greenwich Leisure Limited - 20% discount South Coast Rx Physiotherapy Music at the University Portsmouth Football Club - Discounted Tickets Discounted Tickets Food on Campus - Loyalty Scheme The Square Kitchen at Portsmouth Guildhall - 20% Discount The Water Mill, Calbourne, Isle of Wight - 20% Discount Dell Technologies Microsoft 365 (Office) Apps Anywhere Explore
body: The Akash restaurant, conveniently located in the heart of Southsea's Albert Road, is offering all employees a 10% discount off the total bill on meals eaten in the restaurant. Simply show your staff pass on arrival at the restaurant. This family run restaurant has been serving Portsmouth since the 1970's with the philosophy "good food, good company and genuine hospitality". To find more information on the delicious food available, please visit The Akash website and to reserve a table follow this link. Offer valid for dine-in only Excludes Fridays and Saturdays Excludes the month of December Cannot be used in conjunction with any other offer (including student meal deals) The Akash reserves the right to withdraw this offer at any time Address: 99 - 101 Albert Road, Southsea, Hampshire, PO5 2SG The University has partnered with the New Theatre Royal and the Kings Theatre and as part of the partnership staff and alumni have been offered complementary Matcham Membership for anyone who wishes to sign up and receive the following benefits: 10% discount on drinks at the theatre bars Priority Advanced Booking ahead of the general public on most shows Ticket price savings on selected shows, up to 2 per member per event Exclusive membership offers and events Discounts from associated partners Move your tickets across performances of the same show for free, wherever possible. If you would like to take advantage of this opportunity please: email Memberships@portsmouththeatres.org.uk Include the subject line – “University of Portsmouth Staff – Complimentary Matcham Membership” Include the following text: I would like to sign up for UoP Staff Free Matcham Membership please. 4. As requested by NTR, please provide the following information: Phone number (for mobile tickets) Home address (some people request printed tickets) Confirm ” I am a member of staff at University of Portsmouth” Would you like to receive 20% off food and drink (excluding promotional offers and Craft Union sites) at venues throughout the country? The University of Portsmouth have partnered with the Stonegate Group to receive 20% off food and drink at any of 4,000 venues* nationwide provided you pre-book. This includes Portsmouth venues The Dockyard on Guildhall Walk, The Liquorist and the Slug and Lettuce at Gunwharf Quays and venues in Walthamstow including Goose, Bell and Lord Palmerston. To take advantage of this great offer, choose your venue from one of Stonegate Group's venues and pre-book your table by completing this form. *excluding Craft Union sites Looking for unique gifts or a great fresh coffee and snack? Look no further! The fantastic team at Stay Lucky is offering a 10% discount on everything in-store, including delicious drinks for all colleagues. This charming independent gift and coffee shop at 91 Elm Grove is perfect for finding cool gifts and cards, or simply treating yourself to a fantastic coffee from local roaster Moon Roast Coffee – they even roast their beans nearby in rural Hampshire! As well as the discount if you spend £10 or more in store on a Wednesday you can have a free coffee. How do you redeem this offer? Just pop in, show your staff ID at the till, and enjoy the discount! Health@Handleys is your local spot for feel-good food. Freshly made smoothies, nourishing plates and a space to reconnect, recharge and enjoy. Health@Handleys is offering University employees a 10% discount. Simply head to 59 Palmerston Road, Southsea and show your staff ID at the till to receive this great discount. If you want to see the menu first, please see their website. Explore 500 years of naval history at Portsmouth Historic Dockyard and save with this great offer: 20% discount on Portsmouth Historic Dockyard tickets to both University of Portsmouth Staff and Students. Tickets are valid for 12 months of unlimited access to all the attractions See HMS Victory, HMS Warrior, the Mary Rose and more, by taking advantage of this exclusive deal. To take advantage of this great discount, visit the Visitor Centre in person and present your valid staff ID card. All employees, students and alumni can get a 10% discount on all trips within Europe at More Sailing If you want an adventure on the sea, take a look at the trips available. The 10% discount can be redeemed by using the code student10 when booking on the site. Get fit, boost your mood, play, train and compete at our sustainable sports centre in the historic Ravelin Park in Old Portsmouth. As a member of staff you get a discount on a range of flexible memberships. For more information and to join up follow this link. As an employee of the University of Portsmouth you can get a 20% discount at Gyms provided by Greenwich Leisure Limited: Chingford Leisure Park Leyton Leisure Centre Leytonstone Leisure Centre Waltham Forest Feel Good Centre Walthamstow Leisure Centre Ready to apply? Click on the Greenwich Leisure Membership page Need some help in completing the membership page please see this document. Conveniently located in Ravelin Sports Centre, South Coast Rx Physiotherapy offer all staff a 10% discount off all initial appointments. They also run a pilates class every Wednesday 10.30am - 11.30am. The University Music Department offers a wide range of musical activities to all students, staff and members of the community. Follow this link for more information. As a member of staff you can watch Pompey in action at Fratton Park for a discounted price of £16. To book, first register with the Pompey e-ticket site then, once logged in, click on this link to access the £16 discounted University of Portsmouth tickets. Once you’ve selected your seat in your match of choice, scroll down the Price Class Options to find the University of Portsmouth ticket. And remember to take proof of University I.D. with you to the match for spot checks at the turnstiles. Register online and you can use your staff card to collect loyalty points against your purchases at Food on Campus tills which you can redeem against future purchases. The scheme will also provide the chance of collecting double points, seasonal bonus points, and if you buy any hot drink you can get your ninth one for free! We were one of the first universities to join the Kale Yeah! loyalty scheme developed by Friends of the Earth to combat climate change. Collect a stamp each time you choose a veggie or vegan meal at campus cafes and once you’ve collected six stamps, you’ll receive a free meal. For details on venues and further information please visit the Catering Pages You can start collecting points straight away but you must register your card before you can spend your points and vouchers. The Square Kitchen is open 10am - 3pm, Monday to Friday serving fresh quality food at great prices and a further 20% discount available for University of Portsmouth employees (and students) accessed by presenting your UoP ID. They also have a Loyalty Card scheme for regular customers. Go to Visit Portsmouth to download the menu. The Water Mill, Calbourne on the Isle of Wight is offering a 20% discount on stays for University of Portsmouth staff and students. Located in the beautiful countryside, The Water Mill is just a short journey away from many of the Isle of Wight’s most popular tourist hotspots including Carisbrooke Castle, The Needles and Compton Beach. Accommodation options include the mill’s traditional country cottages, luxury eco lodges and a campsite where you can pitch up your tent or campervan. To get 20% off your next visit, use the code UOPMill when booking. You can contact the Watermill at thewatermill@hotmail.co.uk or on 01983531227, or visit their website. Access exclusive offers and discounts of up to 20% across Dell Technologies systems and electronics including Laptops, Desktops, Alienware, Monitors and Accessories for personal / home use. Click here for full details. Look out for seasonal exclusive offers and promotions. Microsoft offers a 30% discount for University colleagues and students on the cost of a personal subscription. Colleagues can activate the discount on the Microsoft Workplace Discount page. AppsAnywhere is the University Apps portal that allows you to stream apps and download software for study and work - available anywhere, on and off-campus, anytime. Click here for further information. title: Personal Development
headings: Human Resources Personal Development Learning Development (L&D) Linkedin Learning Docebo Learning at Work Staff Apprenticeships Tuition Fee Discounts PhD/Professional Doctorate support for Academic Staff Funded Apprenticeships Learn a Foreign Language Entrepreneurs in Residence Innovation Connect - 40% discount on work space Library Access Explore
body: If you have any queries on the information in these pages please contact reward@port.ac.uk Please click on the links below to find more information on the personal development opportunities open to you as an employee. The Learning & Development team offer a wide-range of workshop events, eLearning courses and resources to support you in your staff development. Linkedin Learning is a great online learning and training resource, available free to all students and staff through your University account. For more information visit the Linkedin Learning page. The University’s new Learning Management System (LMS). Designed to offer an engaging user experience, Docebo enables colleagues to access eLearning, register for events, and explore a wide range of learning and development resources. Visit Docebo for Core and Role-specific training. Staff can access a range of courses from the Learning at Work department. You can choose from a range of qualifications including: Certificate in Higher Education in Professional Studies (Level 4) Bachelor's and Master's courses in Business, Engineering, Maritime Studies, Creative Technologies and Enterprise, Applied Computing, Applied Science and Professional Studies PGCert and MSc courses in Occupational Health & Safety Management and Occupational Health & Safety Management and Occupational Health, Safety & Environmental Management Enrolment is in August / September and January each year. As a member of staff, you may be entitled to a 30% discount on course fees. Apprenticeships are available at the University of Portsmouth as either new roles as apprenticeships or existing staff using apprenticeships for staff development. The University offers a 30% discount to self-funding, Student Loans Company funded and externally sponsored University of Portsmouth staff members undertaking part-time HE3 courses and to the children of university staff members undertaking undergraduate and postgraduate taught full-time and part-time study. The detailed conditions and how to apply are set out in the Tuition Fees Discounts Policy. The University will waive the tuition fees for academic staff (those on full or fractional contracts) wishing to commence a doctoral programme of study in the Faculty in which they are based. This will apply providing such study concords with the strategic interests of the Faculty, and a suitable local supervisory team can be found. For more information, please consult the Faculty Sponsored Doctorates for Academic Staff document. If you are interested in exploring this opportunity further then please discuss with your Head of School in the first instance. NOTE: This opportunity is only available to those academic staff who currently (i) do not possess and (ii) are not already enrolled on a programme of doctoral study. Staff in various roles across the University can complete an apprenticeship delivered by the University of Portsmouth or an external provider. Degree Apprenticeships offer staff the opportunity to develop skills and knowledge in a professional field while working towards the completion of a degree. This is particularly suitable for those in the early stages of their careers. Apprenticeships for University staff are funded by the Apprenticeship Levy, meaning that staff can achieve a full degree without the financial costs. Non-Degree Apprenticeships are available for staff using external providers. Find out more about apprenticeships and how you can learn while you earn here. We invite applications from existing University staff who are on an open-ended contract, or a fixed term contract that does not expire before the end of the selected course. All staff must have the support of their line manager prior to applying. If you have any questions, please contact staff-apprenticeships@port.ac.uk The School of Education, Languages and Linguitics is responsible for the delivery of the Institution Wide Language Programme (IWLP). IWLP provides opportunities for all campus based taught students from across the University to learn a foreign language alongside their studies. As a member of staff you also have the opportunity to learn a language for free. Please be aware that student applications are prioritised and places are subject to availability and cannot be guaranteed. The languages you can study are Arabic, British Sign Language, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Mandarin and Spanish. Please speak to your line manager before applying. Our Entrepreneurs in Residence (EIRs) offer business start-up advice to our students, alumni or staff to launch or grow their business. Entrepreneurial staff can get a 40% discount on work space at Innovation Connect for their first year to work on their business. Joining Innovation Connect, which is based at the University in 3 different centres, means staff will be part of an engaged business community, get access to networking events with experts as well as free business support from Barclays Eagle Labs to help them grow their business. Your staff pass provides access to the University Library, allowing you to borrow and renew loans. For more information on the library facilities available, please click here. title: Life Insurance
headings: Human Resources Life Insurance Explore
body: Some employees may be automatically enrolled in our workplace life insurance scheme at no cost. Whether an employee is in-scope for this depends on their contract, pension scheme entitlement and status (see 'Eligibility' below). Where eligible policy members die in employment while aged under their normal State Pension Age, their nominated beneficiaries may receive a life insurance payment equal to 3 times basic annual salary, pro-rated for part time. This cover is provided from day 1 of eligibility, and is not subject to medical assessment or exclusions based on pre-existing medical conditions. To identify whether you are in scope for this policy, please consider whether you fall into one of the following scenarios: Are eligible for Local Government Pension Scheme (LGPS), Teachers’ Pension, or NHS Pension Scheme in your employment contract, but have opted out of that scheme for any reason Are an active contributing member of ‘MyPensionPlus’ Are a member of UoP London, whether actively contributing in a pension scheme or opted out If you are an active contributing member of LGPS, Teachers’ Pension or NHS Pension Scheme, then you will normally be eligible for a ‘Death in Service’ grant as part your ongoing membership. Further information will be found within individual scheme guidance. You should maintain up-to-date beneficiaries directly through your pension provider - this page is not relevant to you and you must not use the nomination process below as it will take no effect. Other employees who do not fall into one of the above categories are not enrolled in a life insurance scheme, and should consider whether to pursue a private arrangement. Nominating Beneficiaries Employees who are in scope will be contacted in writing and must complete a Death Beneficiary nomination as soon as possible, this is done via Hornbill. Please select People Hub, Reward & Benefits and then Life Insurance Nominations and complete the form. You can add as many nominees as you wish but please make sure that the total percentage of payments adds up to 100%. It is important that you keep this updated at all times to ensure it is paid in line with your wishes. To update your nomination, please complete a new version of the form. The most recent copy will take sole effect. If, for example, you have nominated someone and now wish to add an additional person, be sure that you include both individuals on the new form. The main features of the life insurance cover are: The member's State Pension Age less one year 3 x Salary (pro-rated for part-time employees) Basic annual salary pre-salary sacrifice (pro-rated for part-time employees), excluding commission, allowances, overtime and any other fluctuating emoluments Up to 3 months from the date the member is made redundant, or until alternative employment is found if sooner Group Life Insurance is provided on a discretionary basis which will be reviewed annually. The University may need to pass your personal details on to the policy provider. More information on our processing of personal information for necessary administration is provided on the Data Protection page: www.port.ac.uk/about-us/structure-and-governance/legal/data-protection. Online GP Access via MetLife The current life assurance policy (provided by MetLife) grants 24/7 unlimited access to Virtual GP Services with GP24. GP24 is provided by Health Hero and is accessible to you and your immediate family and dependants living in the same household. There are 2 ways to access the service: By phone: call 0330 043 5198 and give the MetLife Code: MetLifeGP24 Online: Create an account with MetLife GP-24 at https://metlife.gp-24.com/. Use your work email address and input the code: MetLifeGP24. An email confirmation will be generated within 24 hours. Please ensure you follow the instructions in this email. Please note that this is a non-contractual benefit that may be reviewed or withdrawn at any time. Please address any further queries on Group Life Insurance to reward@port.ac.uk.
