title: Who Can Help?
headings: Human Resources Career Development - Who can help? Things you can do to help yourself Things your Manager can do to help Things the University can do to help Explore
body: Build a portfolio of evidence to demonstrate your skills and professional experience Identify your skills and strengths Be aware of available resources to support you eg Learning & Development Toolkit, AUA etc Be involved in work opportunities -work shadowing, working groups, events etc Look for opportunities - Volunteer to do things – people like someone who is helpful – if you do basic things well you are more likely to be offered work with more responsibility Take time to reflect on events or work experiences – what you have gained / learnt/ where does it add value and impact Write up summaries, reports, papers, publications- discuss and share with others Discuss your personal development as part of PDR or interim discussion Developing individuals is part of a line managers role. Discuss the following type of opportunities with your line manager: On-the job training. Where learning is achieved through performing new tasks and activities, normally under the supervision of an experienced colleague. Job-shadowing. This involves spending time with another member of staff to learn about their role and what it involves. Delegation. Where individuals are given a development opportunity to deliver a short term task. Depending on the activity that is delegated, this can be an excellent way to develop planning, decision-making, problem-solving and leadership skills. Reading materials (e.g. books, journals). Reading about a subject matter is often the best way of finding out about it, but this is probably not suitable for more practical subjects. E-learning. Many topics can now be studied online, which offers convenience as the resources are normally available 24/7. Coaching. A form of one-to-one support aimed at developing a person’s skills and knowledge so that their job performance improves. Mentoring. Similar to coaching, but takes a longer-term approach to facilitating personal and career development. Secondments to other departments/ Work Shadowing Involves sending individuals to different areas of the organisation for a period of time to perform a job role relevant to that department. Gives them the opportunity to learn how other departments work, and to acquire skills and knowledge they wouldn’t get from working in your team. Project working. Gives individuals the opportunity to work with other departments on defined objectives and outcomes. Can encourage learning about other aspects of the organisation, and develop skills such as planning, strategy, communication and team working. Blended learning. When the focus is not just on one development activity, but on a variety, e.g. a mix of different activities are used to develop skills and abilities. This can cater for different learning styles and circumstances, and can prevent the learning process from becoming stagnant. Provide resources to help support staff in their roles – Learning & Development Toolkit, HEaTED, Employee Assistant Programme , other resources etc Signpost AUA support for administration professionalism Promote UoP opportunities that are available – work shadowing, working groups, summer events etc, NVQ etc Consider professional work shadows/swaps Provide a range of learning and development events to improve personal effectiveness HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Occupational Health Service Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

title: Working at Portsmouth
headings: Working at the University of Portsmouth Working with us is rewarding in more ways than one Current vacancies Microsoft 365 digital transformation Visiting scholars and researchers Staff benefits How to apply for a role Our People Strategy Hallmarks of a Portsmouth employee Starting work Commuting to Portsmouth Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Arriving from overseas Information for EU/EEA staff applying to Portsmouth Technician Commitment Our memberships and the organisations we work with We use cookies
body: We recruit the best people to deliver the best experience for our students We are a new breed of university in a buzzing, diverse city with plenty of stories to tell, and it's not just our students who enjoy the benefits. We've been awarded an Athena SWAN silver award for our commitment to gender equality, and we've got plenty of other awards and rankings successes to shout about too – but first and foremost, we're here to create bigger and better opportunities for all. We're building a brighter future and we're looking for people to help us get there. If that sounds like something you'd love to do, find out how to apply for a role with us today. Explore our latest external and internal job vacancies here at the University of Portsmouth, and apply for a new role at a top UK university. We're looking for talented people to help us become a digitally enabled and empowered university through M365, within a variety of permanent and fixed-term roles. We're a truly global university and we welcome visiting academics, scholars and researchers from institutions around the world. Discover the financial, personal and social benefits available when you start a new role at the University of Portsmouth – including staff discounts, pension schemes, generous holiday allowances and more. The University of Portsmouth encourages sustainable commuting to Portsmouth options for all members of staff. Find out how we're ensuring equality of opportunity for all by creating a working, learning, cultural and social environment based on dignity and respect. If you're coming to work at the University from overseas, find all the information you need, including getting your visa, finding accommodation and paying your council tax. Information and support for University staff from the EU/EEA. The University of Portsmouth has joined the Technician Commitment, ensuring visibility, recognition, career development and sustainability for technicians. We may use cookies to analyse visitor data, improve our website, show personalised content and give you a great website experience. For more information about cookies on our site and to tailor the cookies we collect, select ‘Manage settings’.

title: Staff Mentoring at UoP
headings: Human Resources What does Mentoring at UoP mean? Being a Mentor Being a mentee Explore
body: Mentoring is a supportive learning partnership where a more experienced individual (the mentor) is assigned to act as an adviser or guide to a colleague (the mentee). A mentoring partnership is a confidential safe place to explore ideas and facilitate learning and professional development. A formal programme using mentoring software enables us to identify needs across the University and offers a fair and consistent approach ensuring that all staff have equal access to be matched to a mentor who can offer appropriate support. The programme gives staff the opportunity to engage in mentoring activity and contribute to the organisation’s capability whilst actively promoting a ‘sharing mind-set’. Mentoring can help staff continuously develop their skills, increase their knowledge and understanding of the University and HE sector and aid them in building resilience to better respond to transition and change. There is also a positive personal impact for mentors, who report that the process is not only a rewarding and developmental experience but also an opportunity to reflect on their own experience and practice. Mentoring partnerships are generally finite and should come to a natural end when the mentee's objectives have been achieved. Anyone can be a mentor if they have skills to share. Mentors are passionate about supporting others by sharing their knowledge, expertise and experience and offer valuable ideas and advice when appropriate. Becoming a mentor is a great way to stay connected with the wider community and an opportunity to make a positive impact within the organisation. In addition, mentors often express satisfaction from passing on knowledge and playing a role in the development of their colleagues. Ideally, mentors should be prepared to invest (approximately) one hour per month to the activity for a period of 12 months. Mentees can be any member of staff who would find it useful to have a neutral mentor who can help them identify and achieve specific objectives. Mentees are passionate about growing professionally, learning new skills, navigating their career path and expanding their professional networks. Mentees should be prepared to invest (approximately) one hour per month for a period of 12 months. title: How do I join the Staff Mentoring Programme?
headings: Human Resources How do I join the Staff Mentoring Programme? Explore
body: Joining the programme is quick and easy. From the landing page just sign in from your PC and a guidance document is accessible from the Guidance and Support Tab. There are different invite codes for Mentees and Mentors so please use the relevant code below: invite code (774ebd) to join as a mentee invite code (071a9e) to join as a mentor 1) Create your profile to demonstrate skills, knowledge and experience and to identify areas in which you would like to develop.2) Review matches and begin your Mentoring Journey to enable mentoring partnerships that suit your individual circumstances and support you to achieve career and personal development.3) Communicate with your mentoring partner and decide together the parameters of the relationship, such as how often you will meet and set your goals for the partnership. There is an expectation that the mentee will make initial contact with the mentor by using the chat or meeting options available on the platform.4) Meet as often as possible either in person or via email, phone or video chat. You are encouraged to meet approximately once a month in a neutral, private space, so that you have an opportunity to think, talk and reflect.5) Actively engage with the platform and resources available to cultivate your mentoring partnership6) Agree goals and milestones and record these on the system to evidence the progress of your mentoring partnership. title: FAQs and case studies
headings: Human Resources FAQs and Case Studies Mentee Case Studies‌ Mentor Case Studies‌ Explore
body: "Meeting with a mentor offers the space and freedom to focus on your own development and priorities, anything from building on ideas following training or Aurora, to overcoming difficult situations and making progress towards new approaches to work and projects, or learning about new areas. Being a mentee has enabled conversations about CPD and interests outside of the general job role/line management structure. This has been beneficial when adapting to different roles within the University, as it is easier to be honest about challenges and opportunities, learn from the Mentor's perspective and experience, and figure out ways forward." Emma Scott-Ward (Mentee), Global Admissions Team Leader "When I embarked upon the mentoring programme I thought very carefully about who I wanted to be my mentor. I am currently a middle manager for the university in a very operational role managing 9 staff, but I wanted to be mentored by someone who, although would have previously been in an operational role, is in a far more senior and strategic position now. I wanted to learn how they approached building their career, what skills they had focused on to take them from one level to the next and whether that was a level I aspired to. It was also important to me that I was mentored by someone whom I admired and respected and felt I could learn from. One of the reasons I would really recommend having a mentor is not only the knowledge and coaching you receive, but also the reassurance you get from it. None of us are the finished article and it is encouraging to hear what your mentor has learnt along the way in terms of what they have focused on, how to handle certain situations and what techniques/ coping strategies they have tried which work for them. I realise my mentor is also still learning, and that has given me tremendous confidence and faith in my own ability. Even though I may yet take a different direction to my mentor, I always feel inspired and energised when I return to my desk." Anonymous (Mentee) "My experience of mentoring is not about solving problems it is about a transformational journey that I take with my mentee. I believe I benefit as much from being a mentor as my mentee. The opportunity to talk through challenges a mentee may experience provides a different perspective for both of us. This allows me to broaden my understanding of differing situations and rethink what a successful outcome looks like for the mentee. The diversity of my mentees provides fresh insights that enable me to appreciate other people's perspectives, developing my own self-awareness.’’ Dr Liza Howe-Walsh (Mentor), Reader of International Human Resource Management, DBA, MSc, BA (Hons), PG CERT Ed, SFHEA, MCIPD "To be able to give useful advice and support to my mentees on their developmental journey, I have to honestly assess myself and look into different scenarios of how I would or did act in certain situations. Being a mentor allows me to reflect on my own daily practices and approaches as a manager, helping me to improve and become an empathetic and empowering manager. I feel I can share my previous experiences with my mentee, positive and negative, enabling them to take advantage of that experience to help them grow, be happier and more comfortable at work. I enjoy being able to help my mentee to improve their work-life balance and helping them to work toward achieving their career aspirations.’’ (Anonymous) Mentor HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Occupational Health Service Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

Kaushar is a Management and Training Consultant. He is the founder member and Director of Aksaa - that specialises in delivering soft skills training and Islamic Cultural Awareness and Cultural Competence Training for public sector professionals. He is a qualified Health and Social Care professional with experience in managing large and complex projects. He started his career in accountancy and then moved to Social Services where he worked as a qualified Social Worker for several years. title: Notice Periods
headings: Human Resources Notice Periods Explore
body: Details of the standard notice periods on completion of the probationary period are as follows: Heads of Departments, Deans and Senior Managers Please refer to your individual Contract of Employment. If you are a Professor, the notice period you must give is three months. The University, as your employer must also give you three months’ notice in the case of termination of your employment. Lecturers, Senior Lecturers, Principal Lecturers and Readers If you are a Lecturer, Senior/Principal Lecturer or a Reader, the notice period you must give is two months. The University, as your employer must give you three months’ notice in the case of termination of your employment. If you are a Research staff member, the notice period you must give is one month. The University, as your employer must also give you one months’ notice in the case of termination of your employment. If you are on scale point 31 and below, the notice period you must give is one month. If you are on scale point 32 and above, the notice period you must give is two months. If you are unsure what scale point you are currently on, you can find out by checking your salary (as stated on your payslip, available via ESS) against the current University pay-scales which will give your scale point and grade. The University, as your employer must give you the following notice period in the case of termination of your employment, dependent on your length of continuous service: Less than 8 years continuous service: two months Between 8-11 years continuous service: one week for each year of continuous service More than 12 years continuous service: 12 weeks HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Occupational Health Service Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

title: Issues to Consider
headings: Human Resources Leaving the University - issues to consider Explore
body: Once your notification of leaving has been actioned by the People Operations Team you will be able to view your confirmed adjusted leave entitlement via ESS. If you need to know your leave entitlement before this, an estimate can be provided. Submit a query to PeopleHub to request this information. Please note the following points: When you leave the University, any outstanding annual leave (pro-rata to the leaving date) should normally be taken before the termination date. However, where this is not possible, payment may be made in lieu of leave accrued but not taken. If your final leave balance shows that you have taken more than the accrued leave entitlement for the current leave period, we will make a deduction in respect of the excess leave taken from your final payment. Annual leave entitlements shown in ESS reflect the entitlement for the full year: if you are checking your balance, please remember that this entitlement will only be reduced proportionately when a leaving date has been input into the system. If you are leaving within 3 months of returning from maternity, adoption or shared parental leave (or an equivalent period if you have reduced your hours) when you have received University Maternity Pay, the Payroll department will need to reclaim the University Maternity Pay/University Adoption Pay from you. Please check the Maternity Leave and Entitlement Policy for further details. If you work Less than 52 Weeks per Year, for example if you are employed as a term time member of staff, and paid “average pay” you will need to make a comparison between actual pay due to you (including holiday pay) and average pay paid to you to date to ensure that you leave with the correct total pay. The amount of adjustment in pay will be detailed in your acknowledgement letter from Human Resources. Fixed Term Contract Employees: if you are employed on a fixed term contract, HR will write to you 2 months before the end date of your contract and you will given access to the redeployment module which will enable you to apply for university vacancies before they are published to the wider audience. International Staff: If you are employed via a Certificate of Sponsorship, the University is required to inform the Home Office once you have left our employment. Please let your HR Officer or the HR Compliance Adviser (ext 8563) know if you have obtained employment elsewhere in the UK as we need to include this information. title: Staff Leavers Procedures and Checklist
headings: Human Resources Staff Leavers Procedure & Checklist To prepare for your departure from the University it is important that you consider the following: Explore
body: Your line manager should provide a copy of the resignation letter to Human Resources (HR) when you are leaving the University, in order that the resignation may be acknowledged and a Leaver’s Pack sent out to you and copied to your line manager. The Staff Access to University Facilities and Leavers Procedures document provides more information. Once notification of your resignation or retirement has been received in the People Operations Team , we will contact you within 3 working days to acknowledge receipt. Please note that we will send an email to your University email account. If you have not heard from the HR Service Centre after this period, please confirm with your line manager that it has been sent to us for action. Home address- It is your responsibility to check that your correct home address is recorded on the HR System and updated when necessary using Employee Self Service. University Equipment/Property- You must return any University equipment/property (keys, mobile phone, laptop, uniform etc) before leaving. University Staff Card- You must return your Staff Card to your line manager by your leaving date. University Car Park Permit- You must return your car parking permit to Transport Services, Mercantile House, by your leaving date. For any queries relating to the parking permit, please contact the Car Parking Office on 023 9284 2805 or email car.parking@port.ac.uk IT access- Your Line Manager will request closure of your email and other accounts, as described in the Staff Access to University Facilities and Leavers. Please remember that your access to your university account will cease on your leaving date – if this needs to be continued, please contact IS ( ext. 7777). P45- Your final payslip and P45 will be forwarded to your home address after your salary has been paid. You are asked to check your address held by the University using the Employee Self Service facility. This will show the current home address we have recorded for you and allow you to update your address if you wish. If you do not have access to this facility, please provide details of the change to the People Operations Team before you leave. P60 & Payslips- You should take copies of your electronic payslips and P60s using Employee Self Service - you will not be able to access these after your leaving date. Training Record -You can print a copy of your Personal Learning Account details through Employee Self Service to take to your next employer - you will not be able to access these details after your leaving date. Salary Exchange Schemes- If you are currently enrolled in a Salary Exchange scheme, there may need to be an adjustment to your final salary. ‘First Bus’ Bus Pass- If you leave the University during the validity period of the bus ticket, the balance of the ticket price will immediately become payable. Where the outstanding balance of the ticket is less than your final salary, the Payroll department will make the appropriate deduction. If the balance exceeds your final salary, Payroll will contact you to arrange collection of the outstanding balance. easit Portsmouth discounted rail tickets- If you leave the University, your easit card/s will no longer be valid and should be sent to easit via Green Portsmouth, Unit 1 St Andrew's Court. Cycle to Work scheme If you are currently in the cycle to work scheme, any balance remaining on your bike will either need to be paid in full to the University or will be deducted from your final monthly salary. See the University rules for the cyclescheme for full details. Childcare Vouchers- Entitlement to receive Childcare Vouchers will cease when your employment with the University comes to an end. It is your responsibility to advise Edenred by logging into your Edenred account (www.childcarevouchers.co.uk) and completing the 'Amendment to Terms' form. Relocation Assistance- If you are resigning within the first two years of commencing your employment you will be required to repay a percentage of the money that you have received according to the following scale: - within one year of appointment: 100% of sum paid - after one year but within 18 months: 50% of sum paid - after 18 months but within 2 years: 25% of sums paid Wherever practical and in most cases this sum will automatically be deducted from the recipient’s salary in stages from date of notice to the date of leaving in equal instalments. If there is an outstanding balance you will be required to repay the amount due before they leave the University’s employment. Staff Discount Course Fees Staff discount and Staff family members discount of course fees are only available for the duration of your employment at the University. Full fees will be payable once your employment has ceased. title: Our Strategy
headings: University strategy 2030 Our mission Our values Ambitious Responsible Open Our vision Top-25 UK University Outstanding and transformational learning Leading-edge research Strong partnerships Connected, inclusive community Agile and responsive Our strategic goals Forging connections with and for our students to ensure outstanding and transformational learning for all Maximising educational gain and transformational growth Boosting student employment​ and graduate distinctiveness Engaging students with our community and with global challenges Ensuring consistently high-quality, engaging experience and outcomes Driving change through our leading-edge research connected to society’s global challenges Enhancing our research culture Expanding our world-leading research Forging strategic partnerships and connections Tackling economic, environmental and societal challenges Connecting education to future workforce and skills needs through strong partnerships at regional, national and global levels Connecting our educational offer to future needs Innovation and expansion in health-science Connecting globally through TNE growth Proactive business development into new markets Nurturing a connected, inclusive community where our students and people thrive together Improving progression to HE and widening access Fostering a connected and inclusive community Connecting through community and civic partnerships Boosting regional and national economic growth Connected across our University community to ensure we are agile and responsive Supporting our people Promoting student voice and community Continuous improvement Digital innovation Modernising our Estate Financial sustainability We use cookies
body: Our path to distinctiveness To create, share and apply knowledge, to make a difference, to individuals and society. We pursue bold ideas in an environment where creativity, innovation and success is encouraged and celebrated We nurture and value relationships and partnerships that foster a global outlook We inspire and support staff and students to achieve their potential and meet the challenges of society We never settle for second best We act with integrity for the greater good We insist on upholding the highest academic and professional standards We respect and celebrate diversity and equal opportunity through an inclusive culture We aim for sustainability across all our activities We work consultatively and collaboratively to benefit from new perspectives We share ideas and maintain transparency We are trustworthy and do what we say we will do We listen and respond to the needs of our students and staff A top-25 UK University, internationally recognised for excellence through our connection with our students, communities and society Forging connections with and for our students to ensure outstanding and transformational learning for all Driving change through our leading-edge research connected to society’s global challenges Connecting education to workforce and skills needs through strong partnerships at regional, national and global levels Nurturing a connected, inclusive community where our students and people thrive together Connected across our University community to ensure we are agile and responsive We will achieve this goal through these objectives: Monitoring and boosting educational gain and transformational growth through our curriculum, enabling students to curate their development, gaining essential transferable skills reinforced by micro-credentials to strengthen their skills for employment. Ensuring our students’ journey develops and empowers them to succeed as highly employable graduates by incorporating real and simulated work-based learning in every course, including employing significant numbers of students, directly and via partners. Redesigning our curriculum to connect students with local, regional and global challenges through cross-disciplinary credit-bearing modules, boosting research engagement, and expanding problem-based learning​. Enabling all students to engage with transformational education while balancing study, work and life, through a culture of innovation and inclusion that recognises and supports the changing needs of our students. We will achieve this goal through these objectives: Connecting and inspiring our colleagues and students in an ambitious and inclusive research community. Building critical mass and resilience in our identified areas of strength by attracting, developing and retaining excellent researchers and by further embedding nationally-recognised good practice. Supporting growth in our world-leading research by establishing new research centres and institutes and nurturing existing ones, to promote the engagement, collaboration and impact of our researchers. Our internal systems will support our growth ambitions, including targeted support for large research bids. Building new and developing existing collaborative connections with regional, national, global partners and business, to rapidly expand the positive impact of our research and innovation on society. Applying our research and innovation strengths to solving national and global societal and economic challenges including environmental sustainability and supporting coastal communities. We will achieve this goal through these objectives: Being a university-of-choice by collaborating with stakeholders, including government and employers, to proactively identify and respond to future workforce and skills needs regionally, nationally and globally. Becoming a premier health-science provider, meeting regional and national needs through new course developments and research supported by strong connections with stakeholders. Expanding our high quality educational opportunities to new markets through transnational education (TNE) provision offered with partners in target countries. Introducing new approaches to connecting with employers to significantly expand our range of employer-focused qualifications including Degree Apprenticeships and continuing professional development. We will achieve this goal through these objectives: Partnering with local schools and colleges to realise students’ future potential and increase progression rates to higher education from under-represented communities. Creating an inclusive community where our students and people feel a strong sense of belonging and well-being, supported by our range of student and staff services. Expanding work-based learning and student volunteering, focusing on initiatives that improve outcomes for our local and regional community. Supporting business and government stakeholders to boost economic growth, including through business support, incubator activities and student enterprise. We will achieve this goal through these objectives: Engaging our people through our vision and values, connecting their individual objectives, workloads, recognition and reward with this strategy. Promoting diversity and inclusion and empowering our people to develop in their careers, upskilling in digital and transferable skills plus continuing professional development (CPD). Embedding our commitment to the student voice throughout the University to ensure we meet students’ needs & expectations. Developing a welcoming and integrated physical and digital campus to promote an inclusive, engaged and connected community. Developing lean, effective services that focus on collaborative strategy delivery. Promoting a connected and collegiate problem-solving approach, including partners and suppliers, to support continuous improvement. Investing in corporate systems and the modern workplace to drive effectiveness and cost-savings. Improving data-driven insight to inform timely and effective decision-making. Investing in our Estate Masterplan to provide a modern, efficient estate that supports our strategy. Focusing on environmental sustainability in our decision making and operations. Strong financial management to ensure long term financial stability and internal alignment of resources with strategic needs. Targeting sufficient levels of cash generation to reinvest in our long-term strategy, infrastructure and estate. title: Recruitment and Selection: Modular Programme
headings: Human Resources Recruitment and Selection Training Other roles and training For further information Accessing the document Explore
body: As you’ll be aware, we’re not currently running the Recruitment & Selection (R&S) Training as a new programme based around the new recruitment system is being developed as part of the HRF transformation project. R&S Training is the collection of courses required to be able to act as a Trained Interviewer (TI) on an interview panel. As all interview panels must have at least one TI and it has been some time since the training has been run, we recognise there is a need for more people to act as Trained Interviewers. As an interim solution, we’ve created a document summarising key things the people responsible for the recruitment and selection process must be aware of, and processes we must follow to remain compliant. It’s also full of information to help you run the best possible recruitment and selection activities, maximising your chances of getting the right person into your vacancies. This document summarises information from a suite of training modules that make up R&S Training. Anyone acting as an interim Trained Interviewer will first need to read the document. We also strongly recommend you refer to it throughout recruitment for guidance at each stage. At the end of the document you’ll find a link to a Google Form for you to complete to confirm you’ve read it. We’ll use the responses to this form to keep a list of those people who can act as an interim Trained Interviewer on a recruitment panel, should no one who has completed the full training programme be available. We’ll also add you to the mailing list for the Recruitment Bulletin, which will keep you up to date with any changes to legislation or university policy relevant to recruitment and selection. When the new R&S Programme is launched, we’ll contact those on this list and invite them to complete the full training. After completing the new programme, you’ll be recorded as having full Trained Interviewer status. Those who have previously completed the R&S Training programme are also encouraged to read through the document, as the information has been updated to reflect recent changes - particularly Right to Work rules and processes. In order to act as a Convenor on a piece of recruitment; the person responsible for updating the recruitment system, you only need to have completed Module 6: Recruitment and Selection: On-line Recruitment and an online version of this module will be developed. Everyone involved in the recruitment process should have completed the Diversity & Inclusion in HE eLearning package and Online Unconscious Bias eLearning package, ideally within the last 12 months. Throughout the document, you’ll find contact details for people or teams that you can get in touch with if you have questions about a particular part. If you have a question you can not find a contact for in the document, please contact The People Hub or for UKVI, RTW or visa questions, please contact Helen Ducane, HR Compliance Adviser at hr-ukvi-compliance@port.ac.uk Please click here to access the: Recruitment & Selection Training Document PLEASE NOTE: This document will be updated by the Recruitment Team as required by either legislative or UoP policy changes. Therefore, copies should not be saved locally, but rather you should bookmark the document and refer back to the digital copy. title: Delivering Successful Change
headings: Human Resources ‌‌‌‌Delivering Successful Change Delivering Change ‌ Explore
body: Change is an ever-present and essential part of daily life, and the University is a constantly evolving organisation. While this can make it a dynamic and vibrant place to work, change can present challenges for individuals and groups, and needs to be effectively managed if we are to realise the benefits of making changes within the University. Where change is likely to impact on the employment, contracts and/or general terms and conditions of staff, there is a legal requirement to consult formally with the recognised Trade Unions and the staff involved. This is detailed in a separate policy, the University’s Organisational Change Policy: Redeployment and Redundancy however, following the recommended approaches in this guidance for communication and engagement may also assist with this process. If there is any question of a possible change affecting the employment, contracts and/or terms and conditions of staff, you must consult immediately with your HR Business Partner, who will advise you on the process to be followed. Involving your team in the need for change, and the plan to implement change, is essential in making sure your team is supportive. Change is usually required to solve a problem, to do something more effectively or to adapt to a changing environment. Discussing these challenges with your team, and jointly creating a plan to solve the problem, will ensure your team owns the change and is keen to make it happen together. Implementing a change without consulting your team will make understanding and accepting the need for change much more difficult. The first step in delivering your change is to think about why the change needs to happen, the type of change you are planning, what formal procedures must be followed (e.g. investment proposals, budget preparation, potential redeployment or redundancy implications, and so on), what precisely you are intending to change, and the key features of the change. The first step in delivering your change is to think about why the change needs to happen, the type of change you are planning, what formal procedures must be followed (e.g. investment proposals, budget preparation, potential redeployment or redundancy implications, and so on), what precisely you are intending to change, and the key features of the change. Once you understand the change you want to put in place, you can put the necessary preparations in place, including support for individuals to deal with change, identifying and sequencing change activities, developing your stakeholder management and communications plans, and revising relevant policies and processes ready for your change. Once you understand the change you want to put in place, you can put the necessary preparations in place, including support for individuals to deal with change, identifying and sequencing change activities, developing your stakeholder management and communications plans, and revising relevant policies and processes ready for your change. You are now in a strong position to guide your team through the change by managing the completion of planned activities, monitoring progress, communications and feedback, and amending your approach or identifying new activities or interventions to ensure your change outcomes are achieved. You are now in a strong position to guide your team through the change by managing the completion of planned activities, monitoring progress, communications and feedback, and amending your approach or identifying new activities or interventions to ensure your change outcomes are achieved. Once the changed state has been reached, there is still work to be done to reinforce changes and ensure activity doesn’t slip back to the old ways. New operating policies and processes must be shared, staff supported through additional training and clarifications, and issues arising in the new state actively captured and resolved Once the changed state has been reached, there is still work to be done to reinforce changes and ensure activity doesn’t slip back to the old ways. New operating policies and processes must be shared, staff supported through additional training and clarifications, and issues arising in the new state actively captured and resolved Throughout the delivery of the change you will need to review progress and reflect on how change outcomes match your expectations. This will help you to learn from your experiences and use this learning to adapt and improve your approach to the change. title: Staff Essentials - How to be a Better Communicator
headings: Staff Essentials How to be a Better Communicator
body: Welcome to Staff Essentials, providing news, events and useful information for University of Portsmouth staff. These pages are all about communication and employee engagement and can be used by any member of staff, but particularly managers, as a guide should you need to refer to sections to deal with any situation. The pages are largely based on information from an inspirational book, How to be a Better Communicator, by David Oliver and Chris Carey. The pages contain almost 150 easy to read nuggets of advice covering key aspects of leadership communication, including communicating during change, team meetings, developing a personal style and understanding the audience. Sections also cover practical skills such as better presentations, effective email and writing. The main sections taken from the book are supplemented with practical information about communicating at the University of Portsmouth plus some further studies, examples and theories. As a manager, you may already be doing many of the things listed, but there should be some ideas and suggestions you haven’t tried before. Communicating and engaging with staff is one of the toughest things for any leader to get right, and while these pages don’t pretend to have all the answers, they may help make things a little easier. Although targeted at managers, the information contained here will be of use to all staff, particularly some of the sections on dealing with change, effective email and writing skills. Use the navigation on the left to view the various topics covered. The Staff Communications Policy details how we communicate at the University of Portsmouth. You might also be interested in the Staff Email Policy. If you have any questions please contact staffessentials@port.ac.uk © David Oliver and Chris Carey 2008

title: Cars and Bicycles
headings: Human Resources Car and Bike Discounts Car Benefit Scheme University Car Parks - Discounted Charges Gunwharf Quays - Discounted Charges Car Share with colleagues Cycle to Work Scheme EELO Folding Electric Bike Local Bike Shops easitPORTSMOUTH bike discounts Bike Security Explore
body: Are you looking for a brand new electric or plug-in hybrid car with an inclusive motoring package and one convenient monthly amount taken from your gross salary? Our car benefit scheme can do all that and more! Your complete driving package includes insurance, servicing, replacement tyres, MOT maintenance, Road Tax, Breakdown Cover and Lifestyle Protections. To find out more information, see what cars are available and obtain personal quotes, please access the scheme via the Vivup benefits platform. Not registered for Vivup? It's easy to do by following this link and entering some basic information and it will also give you access to a huge range of discounts across the UK's major retailers including supermarkets, technology, fashion travel and much more! Note: the scheme is open to employees with a minimum of 6 months service. For full eligibility criteria please see our car benefit scheme page. Staff are entitled to park in university car parks during their working day only at a discounted rate, subject to certain exceptions. In order to take advantage of this, you will need to register to obtain a parking permit and set up an account to allow parking fees to be debited. For further information and details of how to apply please click on Staff Parking. If eligible to use the University car parks, you will need to register for both a parking permit and Evology to pay for your parking. Please ensure your details are kept up to date and match in both systems. Full details on the car parking policy and regulations can be found here. The University provides electric vehicle (EV) charging stations for staff memebers with University car parking permits. For full details of pricing and location of chargers please visit the Electric Vehicle Charging page. Staff are able to park at Gunwharf Quays main car park at a concessionary rate of £4.50 per day during Hampshire term times only on Mondays to Fridays with a maximum stay of 12 hours per day between 7am and 7pm. In order to take advantage of this offer, please take a ticket when you enter the car park and take this ticket, together with your staff pass, to the car parking office for validation. Please be aware that certain dates during term times are excluded, on these days signs will be displayed in the car park showing “No Brand Partner Parking” and the full cost of parking will be applied. The Gunwharf Quays overflow car park at Wickham Street is available at the concessionary rate of £4.50 during Hampshire school holidays. Weekday Car Parking Season pass - £80 per month, Mondays to Fridays only, excluding bank holidays. To access this pass download the QuikPark app, search for Gunwharf Quays and add the monthly pass. This pass allows 24 hour weekday access so you can come and go as much as you need. For more information see Gunwharf Quays parking. You can use easitShare to connect with others looking to share their car journeys (and other commuting trips). Sign up for free on their website and you can immediately begin your search for a car-share match. Once you've found a match, the agreement can be flexible and fit around you. Save money on your commute to work by purchasing a new bike and spreading the cost over a year through payroll deductions. This is a salary sacrifice scheme so you'll make savings by paying less tax and National Insurance contributions The University’s scheme is provided by CycleSolutions. Applications are open throughout the year, with a maximum spend of £1,500 (after discounts applied). Visit CycleSolutions for full information. The EELO1885 Disc Explorer is a high performance folding bike with a range of 40 miles from one charge. The ideal choice for the everyday commuter or those who want to explore the countryside for the day. We are pleased to offer you a discount of 10% off an electric bike from EELO using the discount code UoP10. Please note this is not available as part of the salary sacrifice cycle to work scheme and the discount applies to the bikes only and cannot be used against the Spring Buy One, Get One Half Price offer. To find out more information, please see the brochure, or to order please visit the EELO website. Staff and students will need to show their University of Portsmouth ID card to benefit from the following discounts at local bike shops: Bike U Like, 148 Albert Road, PO4 0JS - 5% off all services Southsea Cycles, 235-237 Albert Road, PO4 0JR - 10% off all services Portsmouth Cycle Exchange, Gothic Buildings, Victoria Road N, PO5 1PH - 10% off new bikes, servicing and repairs but NOT parts or accessories. Discounts are available on second hand bikes, but are dependent on the bikes and will be priced accordingly Velopy, 151-153 New Road, PO2 7QS - 10% off new bikes, servicing and repairs but NOT parts or accessories. Discounts are available on second hand bikes, but are dependent on the bikes and will be priced accordingly With an easitPortsmouth card you can save money on cycling equipment, including 10% off everything in store at Halfords, including new bikes. To access all the bike discounts and more, simply register with easitPORTSMOUTH No matter where you leave your bicycle, D lock the frame and both wheels to the stand. There are over 1,500 cycle hoops and two-tier cycle racks across campus to help you lock your bike securely. A number are in covered cycle cages with swipe card access at these locations: Burnaby Terrace Car Park Eldon Building (Middle Street) Harry Law underground car park To gain access to all the above cycle cages email campus.security@port.ac.uk to request access via your staff card, quoting the following details: HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Health and Wellbeing Retail Discounts Events and New or Limited Time Offers Financial Wellbeing Travel Discounts Ferries Trains and Buses Cars and Bicycles Leave and Absence Leisure, Food and Drink Discounts Pay and Recognition Personal Development My Reward and Benefits menu Life Insurance Hardware and Software Recognition Events and New or Limited Time Offers Travel Discounts Ferries Trains and Buses Cars and Bicycles Leisure, Food and Drink Discounts My Reward and Benefits menu Hardware and Software Occupational Health Service Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

title: Trains and Buses
headings: Human Resources Train and Bus Discounts Season Ticket Advances EasitPORTSMOUTH - 15% off travelling by train University Bus Discounted fares on First Bus - Commuter Travel Club Explore
body: Please follow the link for eligibility details and guidance on how to apply for Season Ticket Advances Get 15% off travelling by train with an easitPORTSMOUTH card on Southern Railway and South Western Railway plus a number of other travel and retail discounts. The easit card costs £5.90 (September 2024) and is available to University staff as part of the easitPORTSMOUTH network. Register for a card or login to your account on the easitPORTSMOUTH website. There are two university bus services which run during term time only. For more information on costs, routes and how to pay for the bus, please visit the relevant Estates and Campus Services page. Get discounted fares on First Bus by joining the Commuter Travel Club and following the steps below: Select University of Portsmouth from the drop down menu. Enter your work email address to validate that you work for the university. Download the First Bus App to your smart phone and set up an mTicket account. Purchase your discounted Commuter Travel Club ticket on the website and it will be sent straight to your phone. title: Ferries
headings: Human Resources Ferry and Hovercraft Discounts Gosport Ferry discount Wightlink Ferry Staff Discount Hovercraft to the Isle of Wight - 25% staff discount for personal travel Hovercraft Work Travel (excluding commuting) Staff Discount Hovercraft and easitCARD 25% staff discount for personal use Explore
body: As an island city, we are well connected across the Solent to the Isle of Wight, Gosport and Hayling Island. There are a number of choices for foot and bike passengers and cars to the Isle of Wight. Travelling between Gosport and Portsmouth is faster by water, with the Gosport Ferry taking only 4 minutes to cross the harbour. Ferries run from 5.30am to midnight, up to 7.5 minutes at peak times. Gosport Ferry offers all staff a 10% discount on quarterly season tickets, with and without a bicycle. To purchase your ticket you need to take the following to the Gosport Ferry ticket office: Original Staff ID card as proof of ID A photocopy of Staff ID card 2 passport style photos of yourself Download and complete the Gosport Ferry University of Portsmouth discount form. Wightlink is offering staff members 15% off car travel and 20% off foot travel when travelling for personal use to and from the Isle of Wight. To take advantage of this discount, please book online. Tickets must be booked online via the link provided and are not available via phone or in the terminal. Please note this discount cannot be applied to season tickets, Wightlink offers a variety of season and multi trip tickets which may help you to save if you frequently use the ferries. Full details can be found on their website. You can travel between Ryde on the Isle of Wight and Southsea in just 10 minutes with Hovertravel. The frequent service runs 7 days a week for foot passengers. Hovertravel offer a number of different ticket options including Hover Academic Tickets which are available for students and staff subject to proof of eligibility, your university card, and are available to purchase in 10, 20 and 40 single journeys, resulting in a discount on full fare tickets. All staff can access a 25% discount off standard adult and child single, day and period return fares for leisure travel for themselves plus up to 4 companions travelling on the same ticket. Book online with promo code UOPSTAFF25. Terms and conditions apply: This discount is available online only using the promo code above at the checkout Not available in the terminal or over the phone Discount available on standard adult, senior, student and child single, day and period returns only Not available on Family fares Tickets must be booked online at least one day prior to travel Valid from Southsea to Ryde only Cannot be used in conjunction with any other offers, discounts or combos Valid for one member and up to 4 companions (must travel together on same ticket) Promo code must not be shared, other than those it is intended for use Staff ID for member must be shown at boarding gate or extra charges may apply This offer is valid for bookings and travel until 31/12/2026 Standard Hovertravel booking Terms & Conditions apply Hovertravel reserve the right to withdraw this offer if not used in accordance to the terms and conditions stated above. Hovertravel is offering staff a 50% discount off standard adult single, day and period return fares for work travel and commuting purposes only from Portsmouth to the Isle of Wight. To take advantage of this discount, please book online using the promo code HTUOP50. Tickets must be booked online via the link provided and are not available via phone or in the terminal. Please note this discount cannot be applied to season tickets. You can save on regular travel by hovercraft using a variety of season and multi trip tickets. See their website for full details. Terms and conditions: This discount is available online only using the promo code above at the checkout Not available in the terminal or over the phone Discount available on standard adult, senior, student and child single, day and period returns only Not available on Family fares Cannot be used in conjunction with any other offers, discounts or combos Valid for one staff member Promo code must not be shared, other than those it is intended for use Staff ID for member must be shown at boarding gate or extra charges may apply This offer is valid for bookings and travel until 31/12/2026 Standard Hovertravel booking Terms & Conditions apply Hovertravel reserve the right to withdraw this offer if not used in accordance to the terms and conditions stated above. For work and commuting purposes only, not to be used for leisure travel. Buy an easitCARD for £5.90 and enjoy 25% off travel on the Hovercraft for you and a companion using the promo code, EASIT25. Full details and how to apply can be found here. title: Learn a Language
headings: Human Resources Learn a Language Explore
body: The School of Languages and Applied Linguistics is responsible for the delivery of the Institution Wide Language Programme (IWLP). IWLP provides opportunities for all campus-based taught students from across the University to learn a foreign language alongside their studies. Furthermore, each year there are a limited number of places reserved for staff to also learn a language. At present, there are seven languages that can be studied, Arabic, British Sign Language, French, German, Italian, Mandarin and Spanish. IWLP is an important part of the University’s commitment to global engagement. Furthermore, it can encourage the development of intercultural awareness in a mobile working environment and is recognised as a ‘career-enhancing activity’. Each year there is a number of course places available to University staff where a module has already recruited to the minimum class size requirement. Staff places will be allocated on a first come, first served basis. However, those who have not previously undertaken an IWLP module will be prioritised over those who have. There will be no charge to staff, nor their department. However, their participation in an IWLP class will need to be supported by their line manager and would be expected to feature in their PDR. Staff can submit an application request via email to iwlp@port.ac.uk. Staff are reminded that places are subject to availability. For further information on the how the programme works; the routes available and application dates please see the Student Learn a Foreign Language page. title: Living our Values Awards
headings: Human Resources Living our Values Awards Scheme Explore
body: Living our Values Awards Scheme The Living Our Values Awards scheme is a tiered, nomination-based individual recognition scheme that aims to highlight and reward the exceptional demonstration of our organisational values. This scheme provides meaningful recognition through monetary awards, celebrating individuals who go above and beyond in exemplifying the behaviours and principles that define our culture. Living Our Values Awards can be nominated at 5 levels of reward, with more impactful behaviours resulting in greater monetary recognition. Who can make a Living Our Values Award nomination? Anyone can nominate anyone for a Living Our Values Award using Hornbill, giving a statement describing the positive impacts made and justifying the reason for the level of nomination. Nominations will then be evaluated by an executive panel and a final decision and level of award will be determined. title: Rapid Reward: ThankUoP
headings: Human Resources Rapid Reward - Thank UoP Thank UoP Nominated Issuers Explore
body: Rapid Reward - Thank UoP Thank UoP is a manager-led, rapid, retail voucher-based recognition scheme that enables timely and meaningful recognition of individual contributions. It supports senior managers in embedding a culture of appreciation within their teams and aligns daily leadership with our institutional values. Thank UoP is intended to bridge the gap between messages of appreciation and a formal nomination for a Living Our Values Award to reward colleagues quickly when they ‘go the extra mile’ in smaller yet important ways. Thank UoP vouchers may be awarded in multiples of £10, to a maximum of £50 for a single award. Sending and Receiving a Thank UoP Voucher Thank UoP uses the Highfive platform and app, where designated Issuers can award vouchers to accompany a Highfive. The Issuers are nominated managers or administrators in each faculty, department. If you aren't one of these nominated users, then you should contact the right person (see list below) in your area by email, including the information of your nominee and the rationale justifying an award. title: Peer Recognition: Highfive
headings: Human Resources Peer Recognition - Highfive Explore
body: Peer Recognition - Highfive Highfive is our peer-to-peer recognition solution that makes it easy for colleagues to celebrate and appreciate one another in a timely and meaningful way. Accessible by all colleagues, Highfive allows anyone to send a personalised message of thanks or praise to a colleague who has gone above and beyond, demonstrated our values, or made a positive impact. Highfives that are not sent as ‘private’ will be visible to all on the University platform so it’s also a great way of enabling a colleague’s manager to see the positive impact that they are having. Everyone can send a Highfive, regardless of their role, team, or level of seniority, to say “I see and value what you’ve done.” You might want to send a Highfive if a colleague: Went out of their way to help you or someone else Demonstrated one of our core values—Ambitious, Responsible, or Open Delivered excellent work or exceeded expectations Made your day easier with a kind gesture or positive attitude Contributed to team success or supported cross-department collaboration How to send a Highfive Highfives are sent through our Employee Benefits Platform (Vivup). title: Appreciative Management Practice
headings: Human Resources Appreciative Management Practice - the Importance of Appreciation Explore
body: Appreciative Management Practice - the Importance of Appreciation The foundation of our approach to recognition is Appreciative Management Practice: a leadership mindset that values, acknowledges, and builds on the strengths and contributions of individuals and teams and builds a connected community. When managers regularly show appreciation it creates an environment where our entire community will be more engaged, motivated, and resilient. Appreciative Management Practice fosters trust, strengthens relationships, and encourages a positive working environment where colleagues feel seen and valued. It ensures that acknowledgment is not just reserved for major achievements but woven into day-to-day leadership. This helps reinforce our values in real time, supports wellbeing, and contributes to a culture where all colleagues are inspired to do their best work and grow within the University. The principles of Appreciative Management Practice are applicable whether a senior leader, manager, colleague or team member. Each of us should adopt an appreciative approach and we are all encouraged to do so. Does Appreciative Management Practice mean we have to change how we manage people? The practices and behaviours we have highlighted here are already a part of good management skills embodied daily across our University. However, we can can use this as a prompt to refresh and remind ourselves to find the time to make sure we do this at every opportunity, showing thanks and demonstrating kindness to our colleagues. title: Apprenticeships- What are they and how do they work
headings: Human Resources Apprenticeships – What are they and how do they work? What are Apprenticeships? Explore
body: “An apprenticeship is a job with training to industry standards. It should be about entry to a recognised occupation, involve a substantial programme of on and off-the-job training and the apprentice’s occupational competence should be tested by an independent, end point assessment. Apprenticeships are employer-led: employers set the standards, create the demand for apprentices to meet their skills needs, fund the apprenticeship and are responsible for employing and training the apprentice. But the needs of the apprentice are equally important: to achieve competence in a skilled occupation, which is transferable and secures long term earnings potential, greater security and the capability to progress in the workplace. Institute for Apprenticeships An apprenticeship is a programme of training to gain the required knowledge, skills and behaviours for a specific job role to enable career progression. Apprenticeships last for a minimum of 12 months and cover a range of industries and occupations. Apprenticeships are open to all age groups from school leavers aged 16 to those who have been working for years or are seeking to start a new career. What types of apprenticeships is the University offering? Apprenticeships are available to both existing staff and new recruits to the University of Portsmouth, under the following models: New roles as apprenticeships Entry level roles: these are likely to be Grade 2 or Grade 3 roles in Professional Services. They include Intermediate or Advanced Level Apprenticeships provided by approved external training providers. The University appointed its first recruited apprentices in October 2017 and they have made exceptional progress and the programme has enabled positive outcomes for those that have now successfully completed. Degree level roles: these are roles that require more specialised knowledge and the individuals will work towards a Degree Apprenticeship at the University of Portsmouth. (If a specialist subject is not offered by the University, another HEI provider could deliver the programme.) Entry level roles: these are likely to be Grade 2 or Grade 3 roles in Professional Services. They include Intermediate or Advanced Level Apprenticeships provided by approved external training providers. The University appointed its first recruited apprentices in October 2017 and they have made exceptional progress and the programme has enabled positive outcomes for those that have now successfully completed. Degree level roles: these are roles that require more specialised knowledge and the individuals will work towards a Degree Apprenticeship at the University of Portsmouth. (If a specialist subject is not offered by the University, another HEI provider could deliver the programme.) Existing staff using apprenticeships for staff development Intermediate, Advanced or Higher Level Apprenticeships: training can be accessed where specific technical knowledge and skills are required for the role. Training will be provided by approved external training providers. To make an enquiry about an apprenticeship, please complete this enquiry form. Degree Apprenticeships: higher level academic study based on work role, to enhance skills required. This is likely to be offered by the University of Portsmouth, however if a specialist subject is not offered by the University, another HEI could deliver the programme. Intermediate, Advanced or Higher Level Apprenticeships: training can be accessed where specific technical knowledge and skills are required for the role. Training will be provided by approved external training providers. To make an enquiry about an apprenticeship, please complete this enquiry form. Degree Apprenticeships: higher level academic study based on work role, to enhance skills required. This is likely to be offered by the University of Portsmouth, however if a specialist subject is not offered by the University, another HEI could deliver the programme. New teaching staff The Academic Professional standard is offered by School of Education and Sociology in collaboration with DCQE for staff involved in teaching and supporting learning. This programme is for early career teachers to support the development of academic knowledge, skills, values and behaviours, enabling staff to successfully deliver contemporary pedagogic practice in higher education. Successful completion of the course will provide three formally recognised qualifications: Postgraduate Certificate in Higher Education Certificate of Academic Professional Apprenticeship Higher Education Academy Fellowship The Academic Professional standard is offered by School of Education and Sociology in collaboration with DCQE for staff involved in teaching and supporting learning. This programme is for early career teachers to support the development of academic knowledge, skills, values and behaviours, enabling staff to successfully deliver contemporary pedagogic practice in higher education. title: Non-Degree Apprenticeships
headings: Human Resources Non-Degree Apprenticeships Explore
body: Non-Degree Apprenticeships could provide the training needed for staff who require learning and skills in their current role. Apprenticeships are available at a range of levels: Intermediate (level 2), Advanced (level 3) or Higher (levels 4-7). The HR Apprenticeship Team have contracts set up with local training providers to deliver the training needed at this level. We currently have provision for the following apprenticeship courses: Business Administration - level 2, 3 and 4 Customer Service – level 2 and level 3 Data Analyst – level 4 Team Leading and Management – level 2, 3, and 5 Associate Project Management level 4 Digital Marketer level 3 Accountancy level 3 and 4 Laboratory Technician level 3 There are many more Apprenticeship subjects available, to find a specific apprenticeship standard click here. There are set processes in place for undertaking an external apprenticeship. Whether you are a staff member or a manager, the first step is to contact the HR Apprenticeship Team: staff-apprenticeships@port.ac.uk or to complete this enquiry form HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Occupational Health Service Equality and Diversity Contact and departments

title: Academic Professional Apprenticeship
headings: Human Resources Academic Professional Apprenticeship (APA) The application window for January 2022 intake is now OPEN The deadline for applications has been extended to 10th January 2022 Funding Four easy steps to developing your career Step 1 - Complete the Application Form Step 2- Join us at a Pre Boarding Session Step 3 - Attend a virtual Applicant Information Session Step 4 - Induction The application window for the Academic Professional Apprenticeship is now open. The application window for the January 2022 intake will close on Monday 10th January 2022. Please ensure applications are submitted before this date. Explore
body: The Academic Professional Apprenticeship (APA) provides a framework for early career teachers, aiming to support you in the development of your academic knowledge, skills, values and behaviours, enabling you to successfully deliver contemporary pedagogic practice in higher education and further enhance your role within the University. Completion of this course will provide you with three formally recognised qualifications: Postgraduate Certificate in Higher Education Postgraduate Certificate in Higher Education Academic Professional Apprenticeship Certificate Academic Professional Apprenticeship Certificate Higher Education Academy Fellowship Higher Education Academy Fellowship The course delivery occurs via an active blended learning approach, which is underpinned by 4 key workshop days in year 1. The online activities are designed to be flexible, allowing you to tailor your studies around the requirements of your workplace role. To support you in your studies, protected time is allocated in your workload plan whilst you undertake this course.Additionally, you will have the support of a highly experienced course team and a subject specialist within your department, who will act as your mentor. The Academic Professional Apprenticeship is fully funded for teaching staff through the Apprenticeship Levy, subject to meeting an eligibility criteria (for those staff who are not eligible a comparable pathway will be offered). Please complete an APA Application form to register on the course. Start your application journey by completing this form Prior to joining the course, we would like to warmly invite you to attend four online pre-boarding sessions. These 1.5-2 hour sessions are specifically designed to support new staff, prior to the course induction: Session 1: I Wish I’d Known That Before I Started! From Surviving to Thriving at the University of Portsmouth – First Steps in Learning and Teaching Please note: this event will run on Wednesday 15th September 2021 and will be repeated on Tuesday 16th November 2021 so please book your attendance on one of these dates. Session 2: A-Z of Higher Education: Policy to practice Please note: this session will run on Thursday 23rd September 2021 and will be repeated on Monday 22nd November 2021, please book your attendance on one of these dates. Session 3: Introduction to Blended and Connected Learning Please note: this session will run on Monday 4th October 2021 and will be repeated on Tuesday 14th December 2021, please book your attendance on one of these dates. Session 4: Achieving Balance and Well-Being Please note: this session will run on Wednesday 13th October 2021 and will be repeated on Thursday 9th December 2021, please book your attendance on one of these dates. Prior to joining the course and to ensure that your enrollment on to the course runs smoothly, you should attend one of the three virtual Applicant Information Sessions which are being held on: Thursday 23rd September 2021, 12.00-14.00 Thursday 23rd September 2021, 12.00-14.00 Tuesday 9th November 2021, 11.00-13.00 Tuesday 9th November 2021, 11.00-13.00 Wednesday 8th December 2021, 13.00-15.00 Wednesday 8th December 2021, 13.00-15.00 During these sessions you will be provided with an overview of the course structure and have an opportunity to ask any questions you may have regarding your future studies. When you complete your application form (Step 1) you will be asked to select an Applicant Information Session. You will then be sent an invitation to your chosen session (this will be held on Zoom). Your induction and first workshop day will be held on: Wednesday 26th January 2022 (09.30-12.30 and then 13.30-16.30hrs) There will be three additional workshop days over the calendar year which will take place on: Wednesday 16th March (APD workshop 2) Wednesday 16th March (APD workshop 2) Wednesday 22nd June (APD workshop 3) Wednesday 22nd June (APD workshop 3) Wednesday 29th June (APD workshop 4) Wednesday 29th June (APD workshop 4) If you have any questions, please email apa@port.ac.uk. title: Application process for the University’s Degree Apprenticeship Scheme
headings: Human Resources Application process for the University’s Degree Apprenticeship Scheme Application requirements 2 Step Degree Apprenticeship Application Process 3 Step Approval Process It is essential that your application should include: Explore
body: This opportunity is open to staff who are on an open-ended contract or a fixed-term contract which does not expire before the end of the Degree Apprenticeship programme. Applicants must have their line manager’s approval and be able to spend 20% of their paid working hours on their study. Apply for a University Degree Apprenticeship. Before making an application, an initial conversation must take place with your line manager. If your manager agrees this would be an appropriate development opportunity and that core activities can be managed, you will then be required to write a proposal to be submitted to your manager outlining the following: The reasons for applying for a Degree Apprenticeship How the content of the course profile will enhance your capabilities within your current role How the learning from the Degree course will enhance or add skills that will enable you to successfully complete PDR objectives, and ensure the successful delivery of the ambitions, priorities or enablers of the new University Strategy How the work that will be missed due to the 20% off the job training will be managed, ensuring service delivery levels will not be negatively impacted. Please note, undertaking and completing a Degree Apprenticeship will not automatically lead to promotion or an increase in Grade or level of responsibility. Skills Matrix - this questionnaire checks your current skills, knowledge and behaviours in relation to your chosen apprenticeship to check that you have a need for new learning. If the questionnaire indicates that you are already competent, you may need to discuss alternative options with you line manager. If the score indicates a need for new learning, you will be sent a link to the HR Application form. HR Application form - this gathers the required information to enable HR to check applicants are eligible for funding, enables course leaders to check applications meet course entry requirements and provides University Admissions Centre with the information required to consider the application. If applicants give consent in the form, they will not be required to provide information that is already held by HR, however applicants will be asked to provide copies of documents to Admissions in order to comply with government requirements for funding." Your application will be reviewed by the HR Apprenticeship team who will check your funding eligibility. If you meet this criteria your application will be passed to stage 2. Applications will then be presented to the academic teams to assess whether you meet the entry requirements of the course. If approved, your application will be passed to stage 3. Applications will be considered by the University Admissions Centre who will need to check your passport and certificates, and will confirm your academic suitability for the course. If successful you will then receive your offer of study. If at any stage your application is not approved, you will be notified by email. Please note, all applications from staff members must follow this HR application process in order for funding to be allocated from the University's Apprenticeship Levy Account. Staff applications sent directly to Admissions will be delayed and may jeopardise individuals gaining a funded place. What you will gain from completing this Degree Apprenticeship programme How your department/school/professional service or other work area will benefit from your completing this Degree Apprenticeship programme Confirmation that you believe you are eligible to be enrolled on the Degree Apprenticeship programme Confirmation that you are committed to attending and successfully completing the programme. Where applicable, you may be required to attend the course on a non-working day or make a contribution from a partial non-working day if part-time; Evidence of your line manager’s support and commitment, including agreement that you will be released for one day a week from your work for the duration of the course. Where there are more applications than places, a waiting list will be created and individuals will be contacted when future programmes become available. Find out more Apprenticeships – What are they and how do they work? The HR Apprenticeship Team manage applications for all University staff wishing to undertake any apprenticeships: Apprenticeship Coordinator: Penny BuckmasterApprenticeship HR Lead: Helen Keetch To contact the team, please email: staff-apprenticeships@port.ac.uk HR Business Partnering Team Learning and Development My Rewards and Benefits Occupational Health Service Equality and Diversity Contact and departments
